productTitle: EarFun Free 1S Wireless Earbuds, 4 Mics ENC Bluetooth Earbuds for Clear Call, 7mm Composite Drivers, Powerful Sound, App for Custom EQ, Sweatshield™ IPX7 Waterproof, Wireless Charging, Game Mode, 30H
reviewTitle: Works well but hope you don't have an issue with it
reviewText: Nice headphones with good sound. Unfortunately after 6 month's use, the case would not charge and it would just flash the white LEDs erratically and quickly.  If you look this up, this does seem to be an issue for some people.  I tried everything, obvious things such as swapping the cable - in case my cable was damaged.  Of course, the problem was with the case and not the cable!  This is where I bring in the negative part of the review.  You do not want to engage with Earfun customer services.  The help I got was initially a video showing me how to plug the cable into the back of the case! Fair enough that there may be a small minority of the population who may not know this but I think most people know how to put the cable in so that it charges.  Especially someone who is going to use wireless earphones! I, again, reiterated that the case was not charging and received a response that I would have to take a video of the cable and charger showing that it was not charging and then attach the same cable and charger to another device to prove that the charger and cable were working and film another video.  Then I would have to repeat the process twice with a different cable and video - so a total of 4 videos to send! It was at this point that I complained to Amazon about Earfun's customer service.  Thankfully, Amazon have top notch customer service and were able to resolve the issue for me.  In conclusion, yes, they are nice earphones but if you have an issue with them, prepare to send emails and numerous videos to their head office in China and hope that they will resolve it for you! I will not buy Earfun products again - not due to their sound quality but customer service.  It is a well known fault and they essentially put you off making a warranty claim.

productTitle: EarFun Wireless Earbuds, [What Hi-Fi Awards] Air Bluetooth In-Ear Headphones with 4 Mics ENC, Sweatshield™ IPX7 Waterproof, Clear Sound, Deep Bass, Wireless Charge, App Customize EQ, 35H, White
reviewText: (I've updated this review twice, eventually arriving at 5 stars - see the updates at the end)Firstly, these buds have excellent sound for the price. They're not quite as well rounded as the audiophile type headphones, but they are really very, very good. They're certainly plenty good enough for a run around the block, or the commute to work. The sound isolation by virtue of being an earplug design is good, but I can still hear enough around me to be situationally aware.The charge case is nice too - and seems to provide plenty of power for a good long time. The buds are held in by a little magnet so there's a clear "in" and "out". The stalks go into two deep holes which have springy contacts in them for charging. Those holes are possibly a problem if some fluff gets in there, or if you need to clean the contacts.There are some wrinkles though - hardly surprising given the price. Firstly, skipping tacks, pause/resume, adjusting the volume etc all require a number of "taps" with a finger in a particular ear. This is okay, but it's prone to problems mis-counting the number of taps you intend. If your finger doesn't go right onto the bud, it may not be counted, if you go too quick, it may not be counted, if it's a Tuesday, it may not be counted... you get the idea. This has lead to more than a few pauses when I wanted to skip, volume when I wanted to pause and I've lost count of how many times it's told me it's switching to "game mode" (which is missing a consonant, so sounds more like "gay mode").Also, when putting the buds back into the charger, sometimes things don't work right. First it was the left one, now the right - when I put them in, sometimes they charge (and disconnect bluetooth), sometimes they just stay there. If the latter, then they'll eventually run out of battery, and re-connecting them to bluetooth seems overly hit-and-miss. Some days it takes several attempts to get both ears to work at the same time.When this first happened, I contacted Earfun, who it's fair to say were great. They told me to clean the contacts (not so easy down the bottom of the tube in the charger), and push hard and whatnot. On that occasion it worked, but subsequent times it's been a lot less successful. My guess is the actual buds get themselves in a mess and don't start to charge/disconnect when they should, and so need some "resetting" to make them behave. The little LED on the buds sometimes does some very weird things, and sometimes the buds seem to try to talk but garble instead. There's no reset button (or apparently a way to switch them off/on or whatever), so getting them back to themselves isn't always easy. Sometimes you just have to let them run out of battery and then recharge them to get them to work. This all smells of a software problem on the buds (not upgradeable), so I hope Earfun can sort it for a subsequent model.All in all though, these are great, and I would recommend them to anyone looking for a decent set of buds (and Earfun themselves look to be good). The problems with them are very annoying but not a show-stopper (for me at least). Not quite "apple" yet, but then they're like a 10th of the price.UPDATE: Earfun have got in contact to smooth over the above (was a 3 star review). Adding a star because that's unexpected from them, is above and beyond what I'd ever reasonably expect and another clear plus point for these buds.Update 2: (Moved up to 5 stars) Earfun replaced the old with the newer model (the one with app connectivity, and it comes with a little cleaning stick). The app is plenty good enough (probably won't need it more than once or twice), and thankfully doesn't need you to register to use it. I've more or less left my buds as 'default', except to turn off Game Mode. The app provides a way to update the earbuds firmware, so I guess this is a way for Earfun to improve what they can without needing to replace the hardware.Further, the actual buds work better than before. The finger touch thing seems more 'definite' than before, so a lot less pauses when I meant skip, or whatever. They seem to pair with my watch more reliably than before too, and so far have charged flawlessly. Earfun seemed to think previous problems were with dirty contacts, and have provided a little cleaning stick this time, so time will tell if that's still a problem here. So far, the buds seem to disconnect and charge as they ought to, so I don't expect random 'battery low' issues (the app can tell you the charge of each bud too - if you need such a thing).All in all then, 5 stars as they sound great, work well and are at a price you can't beat. Earfun service has been excellent too.

productTitle: EarFun Wireless Earbuds, [What Hi-Fi Awards] Air Bluetooth In-Ear Headphones with 4 Mics ENC, Sweatshield™ IPX7 Waterproof, Clear Sound, Deep Bass, Wireless Charge, App Customize EQ, 35H, White
reviewText: These are great value. I bought a couple of pairs of these a while ago for my kids as a budget alternative to Apple AirPods. I borrowed my son’s and I was so impressed by the sound quality I bought myself a pair too.They fit my ears far better than AirPods and don’t fall out at all - which AirPods seem to do all the time (especially when I’m running) and because of how well they fit, these block out external sound far better (they’re not noise cancelling, but the rubber ear tips do a great job of creating a seal in your ear to block out external sound).Unlike AirPods they can only connect with and remember one device at time. But reconnecting is very easy with the button on the charge case.They hold their charge really well and last a long time. I have no reason at the moment to doubt the 35+ hour claim on the box, but I’ve come no where near testing them for this long.Sound is good, although I actually find them a bit too bassy with some tracks - but this only makes me lower the volume, which can’t be a bad thing.

productTitle: EarFun Wireless Earbuds, [What Hi-Fi Awards] Air Bluetooth In-Ear Headphones with 4 Mics ENC, Sweatshield™ IPX7 Waterproof, Clear Sound, Deep Bass, Wireless Charge, App Customize EQ, 35H, White
reviewTitle: Great battery life, a bit uncomfortable.
reviewText: Amazing value, really solid pair of earbuds. In particular, the battery life is great and they connect and disconnect easily. Mic seems good and nice sound.I have found them a little uncomfortable to wear though. Sometimes, after an hour or so, I have to take them out. I have tried a few different sizes to the squidgy bits that probably have a name, but no good fit for long term use so far.I saw some reviews complaining the touch controls were too sensitive. I have the opposite problem. Pausing and playing a podcast with a double tap sometimes works and sometimes doesn't. I've found two very firm taps works best. But it is annoying as it takes me 5-6 seconds to get it to pause sometimes and with each failed attempt I'm often changing the volume (single tap).Also, for those other single bud-at-a-timers, volume up is on 1 bud, and volume down is on the other, so you won't be able to effectively use the volume controls when using only 1 bud.

productTitle: EarFun Wireless Earbuds, [What Hi-Fi Awards] Air Bluetooth In-Ear Headphones with 4 Mics ENC, Sweatshield™ IPX7 Waterproof, Clear Sound, Deep Bass, Wireless Charge, App Customize EQ, 35H, White
reviewText: I did some research before purchasing these earbuds and have to say that so far with a week or so of use have been very happy with the performance.  As noted elsewhere,  the bass is a tad forward but not unpleasantly so. With most audiophile equipment, a burn in period is to be expected.  These, right off the bat performed better than I had expected.  To my ears the treble is fine - clear and crisp, providing a fairly well defined sound stage that belies their modest price. Battery life compares well with more premium buds and range is equally good. I have no need for noise cancelling so do not miss this feature and currently rate these for audio, fitment and battery life to be at least equal to my premium earbuds. Absolutely excellent value to performance ratio!

productTitle: EarFun Wireless Earbuds, [What Hi-Fi Awards] Air Bluetooth In-Ear Headphones with 4 Mics ENC, Sweatshield™ IPX7 Waterproof, Clear Sound, Deep Bass, Wireless Charge, App Customize EQ, 35H, White
reviewText: Until I read the What HiFi 5 Star review, I didn’t know anything about Earfun products. My £180 Apple AirPods are giving up; 30 mins and the right hand, errr, “pod” gives up… and the left one follows 10 mins later. Do I want to spend another £200 on a pair of EarPods when these are somewhat more economical? No. So I bought these and theybare…. they are FANTASTIC! They’re as good as the Californian (Chinese) products; just as well made, are brilliantly designed, have a longer life, stay in your ear comfortably, charge wirelessly, are perfect to use in the gym as they’re IPX7 rated… and that means ensures that the device can be submerged in water up to 1 meter for 30 minutes without any damage - and finally and most importantly, they sound absolutely superb. Just buy them before everyone finds out!

productTitle: EarFun Wireless Earbuds, [What Hi-Fi Awards] Air Bluetooth In-Ear Headphones with 4 Mics ENC, Sweatshield™ IPX7 Waterproof, Clear Sound, Deep Bass, Wireless Charge, App Customize EQ, 35H, White
reviewTitle: Ok sound, reliable connection and great battery life
reviewText: The case is much bulkier than expected, easily twice or three times the size of an AirPods case, not that easily pocketable.The sound is ok, I expected better. It's quite middle heavy, bass is not too bad but not much treble. It's excellent for calls and microphones seem to work well, not that great for music.It still deserves 5 stars from me if I factor in the price as setup was dead easy, it connects reliably, presented well in the box and the build quality seems good.Battery life is excellent, it is replacement for a dying pair of AirPods that only lasts 15 minutes after as little as 2 years of very light use.

productTitle: EarFun Wireless Earbuds, [What Hi-Fi Awards] Air Bluetooth In-Ear Headphones with 4 Mics ENC, Sweatshield™ IPX7 Waterproof, Clear Sound, Deep Bass, Wireless Charge, App Customize EQ, 35H, White

reviewText: **Updated... my earbuds were faulty and inrecieved a full refund (an offer of exchange was also made). Customer service for this company is OUTSTANDING. They reached out to me. This is with a Samsung tab, s23 phone, and steam deck oled. It's really annoying to the point I'm using my old headphones, so what was the point in buying need ones? Waste of money I'm sad to say.

They certainly feel like a more expensive set with good quality plastic and a decent feel, way better than my last unbranded set which were half the price but a 10th of the quality. If you’re on a budget and don’t expect studio quality sound, these are worth a fo

productTitle: EarFun Wireless Earbuds, [What Hi-Fi Awards] Air Bluetooth In-Ear Headphones with 4 Mics ENC, Sweatshield™ IPX7 Waterproof, Clear Sound, Deep Bass, Wireless Charge, App Customize EQ, 35H, White
reviewTitle: Constant problems but fast response from manufacturer
reviewText: I have had some kind of issue with these since day 1. The left earbud doesn't work very well. Most of the time when I try to use these in the morning I find that only the right headphone connects and though it is possible to solve this issue it is a frustrating 5 minutes of constantly attempting to pair them until it works.It also seems that once I've managed to connect to it, the left earbud wants to keep connecting even when it is in the charging case. Frustrating again when someone calls me and I miss it because the audio is only coming out of one earbud safely tucked away in its case where it is meant to be charging and not connected to my phone.Finally, they have recently become fully unusable thanks to the left earbud refusing to charge past about 10%. They last about 10 mins of my walk now before they start disrupting my music with "battery low... disconnected.... pairing... disconnected... pairing... battery low... disconnected... pairing... power off"UPDATE: The manufacturer was very responsive when I emailed and are sending a new pair. For now I am updating the review to 4 stars, which may further change depending on how the new pair perform.

reviewText: These were recommended to me and as they are relatively cheap I decided to buy them. Easy to link to phone, comfortable, absolute bargain for the price.

productTitle: EarFun Wireless Earbuds, [What Hi-Fi Awards] Air Bluetooth In-Ear Headphones with 4 Mics ENC, Sweatshield™ IPX7 Waterproof, Clear Sound, Deep Bass, Wireless Charge, App Customize EQ, 35H, White
reviewText: Bought these after seeing their review in What HiFi. I needed to replace my running headphones that had given up after a couple of years of service and these sounded OK. Got a pair and I quite like them. But....I have found that they will not stay in my ears whilst running. Have been out a few times since getting them and they just won't stay in. The left one will pop out every hundred metres and the right every five hundred. It's annoying and frustrating. I've tried all of the ear tips but none of them help. I've even tried some from other headphones but I just cannot get them to fit well enough to stay in my ear. I am a bit of a sweaty runner so perhaps the added lubrication there is the cause. Maybe I have unusually large earholes. Who knows. I'd just say they are not really for running. For walking and less strenuous stuff they're perfectly fine. The sound is not the best, but I've paid much more for headphones that sound a lot worse. They're a bit heavy on the base but again, it's not like they're couple of hundred pounds and designed for audiophiles. I'd say they were great value for money and good enough for day-to-day use, just not for running.

productTitle: EarFun Wireless Earbuds, [What Hi-Fi Awards] Air Bluetooth In-Ear Headphones with 4 Mics ENC, Sweatshield™ IPX7 Waterproof, Clear Sound, Deep Bass, Wireless Charge, App Customize EQ, 35H, White
reviewTitle: Overall pretty decent for the money.
reviewText: You can certainly get cheaper and of course more expensive but these are good for the money, the touch controls work pretty well, something I had trouble with on a cheaper set and the sound quality is excellent, you may not think so if you're a bit of an audiophile.I've used them for an hour at the gym with no issues but early today, I was pottering around the house and they started to drop connection, I've got them paired to my Apple Watch so it wasn't a distance issue, and it hadn't been much more than and hour. I've popped them back on charge and I'll try them again, hopefully this isn't a sign of them starting to let me down as I'm otherwise very happy with them.

productTitle: EarFun Wireless Earbuds, [What Hi-Fi Awards] Air Bluetooth In-Ear Headphones with 4 Mics ENC, Sweatshield™ IPX7 Waterproof, Clear Sound, Deep Bass, Wireless Charge, App Customize EQ, 35H, White
reviewTitle: Awkward fitting and sensitivity and a terrible app
reviewText: The sound quality is pretty good (not 5* What Hifi) good strong bass. Ive tried all sizes of buds. None fit that well. Once fitted the odd shape means that do not hang vertically but the stems point directly forwards, and quite frankly they look ugly. Others have mentioned the annoying over sensitive touch pads. It is impossible to insert them without turning on an unwanted feature (game mode on, game mode off - in an annoying Chinese voice). Also they pair too eagerly and turn back on your music track that you have just closed. Once you get used to them they perform reasonably well and the volume control works. Pause and skip are a little more tricky. Unless you have an equaliser on your phone you will be disappointed with the Earfun app - it is absolutely abysmal with 4 presets that make almost no difference. I will keep them however as the sound is good. But I will only use them indoors as they look awful and fall out easily. A wired pair work best outside. A good effort Earfun, but these are a work in progress.

reviewText: Reviews over time became so important that it now seems they are not very useful. A simple question is why recommend headphones (by what hifi) when they have a major flaw? How can these be 5/5 when they have a major flaw for everyone?The flaw is simple, when you touch the headphones to adjust them falling out of your ear slightly, which almost everyone will, the volume goes up or down. There is no way to stop this. If you are connected to a tv it also shoots up an incredible amount. Along with a beeping noise, it means you're always at odds adjusting the sound. It shouldn't be like this, they must have found this when testing? If they did any..There needs to be a way to disable it, looking at their online support there is not.Also one of the earphones had a scuff and scratch on it and amazon makes it impossible to get money off nowadays and you'd have to just return it.

reviewText: I bought these with very little expectation (who are EarFun?) but have been quite impressed with the quality of the product. They're not heavily used but the battery life is good. If your a audiophile definitely not the pair for you but if your just an average Joe just wanting to listen to your music/podcast etc they hard to beat especially when they are on offer.Battery on these are excellent easily lasted me a 3+ weeks with my use before I had to plug in the carry case to charge.Overall really happy with them for the price, only issue I've ever had with them is when your sweating at the gym certain movements/exercises can cause them to slowly come out of your ears which mid set can be annoying but saying that they aren't uncomfortable & have never aggregate my ears like other pairs in the past have also the ear bud controls off the ear aren't the best imo but I just use my phone so isn't that much of a Biggie personally to me but other people might use that feature more.

reviewTitle: Quality product at pocket money price
reviewText: Got these to replace my JVC earbuds which were £25 on sale at Sainsburys [£50 before markdown]. Read the reviews in What HiFi and then saw them on offer. Was worried that the review might have been over enthusiastic. Needn't have worried, these earbuds are amazing  value for the money, and make the JVC buds sound inferior in every respect. These buds will only ever be used to listen to my audio collection via pc, so I can't comment on their functionality as a phone accessory. However, they perform brilliantly on a variety of music from my collection such as DSOTM, Tubular Bells, Big Big Train, Rolling Stones. Much better than the JVC's. The range of the buds is better as well, I can be anywhere on the ground and first floors and they don't lose signal. I apparently have small earholes, and these buds have the silicon earpieces that fit perfectly so that the sound is channelled into my ears. I would heartily recommend these earbuds and would consider purchasing other earfun products over some of the other Chinese brands that Amazon sells at the same price point.

reviewText: Pros:-- Fantastic sound quality for music, media and calls-- Holds charge well-- Left & Right clearly marked-- There's only one way to fit the buds back in the pod (hard to get it wrong)-- No wires to tangle-- Different sized tips included-- Cleaning wand included-- Thoughtfully designed all roundCons:-- The touch controls are a faff-- Potentially easy to loseI've been reluctant to get some of these because I'm great at losing things; I didn't think they'd sound better than wired headphones; and I'm annoyed with phone companies for removing the headphone jacks from their high end phones.But these EarFün buds sound soooo good that I'm a total convert!I got some OnePlus wireless buds for my OnePlus phone, but I kept seeing these in reviews coming up as top value, and great sound for the price.I can't resist a deal, so I took a risk, and I'm very, very happy. These sound better than any wired earphones I've experienced and better than the OnePlus ones I've got.They're not perfect; the touch controls are hard to memorize, and a bit of a faff; and I imagine they're easy to lose!But they're so good that, if I lose one, I'll re-buy this same pair rather than stick with my back-ups.

productTitle: EarFun Wireless Earbuds, [What Hi-Fi Awards] Air Bluetooth In-Ear Headphones with 4 Mics ENC, Sweatshield™ IPX7 Waterproof, Clear Sound, Deep Bass, Wireless Charge, App Customize EQ, 35H, White
reviewText: If you can live with the performance issues then these are a solid purchase at the price point. Since buying, I have endured several irritating problems, including the earbuds failing to connect necessitating forgetting/resetting the Bluetooth pairing, one earbud failing to connect while the other works fine, both earbuds disconnecting during use, the battery dropping off suddenly despite being at 60%, and others. While not deal-breaking on their own, these problems, combined, can be a serious annoyance.Despite this, the sound is great for the price. It lacks a bit of clarity at higher volumes, particularly listening to louder, more complex genres like metal, but the sound is full-bodied and punchy. It's a shame about the performance issues because without them, these would represent incredible value for money.

reviewText: Very good

productTitle: EarFun Wireless Earbuds, [What Hi-Fi Awards] Air Bluetooth In-Ear Headphones with 4 Mics ENC, Sweatshield™ IPX7 Waterproof, Clear Sound, Deep Bass, Wireless Charge, Game Mode, App Customize EQ, 35H
reviewText: (I've updated this review twice, eventually arriving at 5 stars - see the updates at the end)Firstly, these buds have excellent sound for the price. They're not quite as well rounded as the audiophile type headphones, but they are really very, very good. They're certainly plenty good enough for a run around the block, or the commute to work. The sound isolation by virtue of being an earplug design is good, but I can still hear enough around me to be situationally aware.The charge case is nice too - and seems to provide plenty of power for a good long time. The buds are held in by a little magnet so there's a clear "in" and "out". The stalks go into two deep holes which have springy contacts in them for charging. Those holes are possibly a problem if some fluff gets in there, or if you need to clean the contacts.There are some wrinkles though - hardly surprising given the price. Firstly, skipping tacks, pause/resume, adjusting the volume etc all require a number of "taps" with a finger in a particular ear. This is okay, but it's prone to problems mis-counting the number of taps you intend. If your finger doesn't go right onto the bud, it may not be counted, if you go too quick, it may not be counted, if it's a Tuesday, it may not be counted... you get the idea. This has lead to more than a few pauses when I wanted to skip, volume when I wanted to pause and I've lost count of how many times it's told me it's switching to "game mode" (which is missing a consonant, so sounds more like "gay mode").Also, when putting the buds back into the charger, sometimes things don't work right. First it was the left one, now the right - when I put them in, sometimes they charge (and disconnect bluetooth), sometimes they just stay there. If the latter, then they'll eventually run out of battery, and re-connecting them to bluetooth seems overly hit-and-miss. Some days it takes several attempts to get both ears to work at the same time.When this first happened, I contacted Earfun, who it's fair to say were great. They told me to clean the contacts (not so easy down the bottom of the tube in the charger), and push hard and whatnot. On that occasion it worked, but subsequent times it's been a lot less successful. My guess is the actual buds get themselves in a mess and don't start to charge/disconnect when they should, and so need some "resetting" to make them behave. The little LED on the buds sometimes does some very weird things, and sometimes the buds seem to try to talk but garble instead. There's no reset button (or apparently a way to switch them off/on or whatever), so getting them back to themselves isn't always easy. Sometimes you just have to let them run out of battery and then recharge them to get them to work. This all smells of a software problem on the buds (not upgradeable), so I hope Earfun can sort it for a subsequent model.All in all though, these are great, and I would recommend them to anyone looking for a decent set of buds (and Earfun themselves look to be good). The problems with them are very annoying but not a show-stopper (for me at least). Not quite "apple" yet, but then they're like a 10th of the price.UPDATE: Earfun have got in contact to smooth over the above (was a 3 star review). Adding a star because that's unexpected from them, is above and beyond what I'd ever reasonably expect and another clear plus point for these buds.Update 2: (Moved up to 5 stars) Earfun replaced the old with the newer model (the one with app connectivity, and it comes with a little cleaning stick). The app is plenty good enough (probably won't need it more than once or twice), and thankfully doesn't need you to register to use it. I've more or less left my buds as 'default', except to turn off Game Mode. The app provides a way to update the earbuds firmware, so I guess this is a way for Earfun to improve what they can without needing to replace the hardware.Further, the actual buds work better than before. The finger touch thing seems more 'definite' than before, so a lot less pauses when I meant skip, or whatever. They seem to pair with my watch more reliably than before too, and so far have charged flawlessly. Earfun seemed to think previous problems were with dirty contacts, and have provided a little cleaning stick this time, so time will tell if that's still a problem here. So far, the buds seem to disconnect and charge as they ought to, so I don't expect random 'battery low' issues (the app can tell you the charge of each bud too - if you need such a thing).All in all then, 5 stars as they sound great, work well and are at a price you can't beat. Earfun service has been excellent too.

productTitle: EarFun Wireless Earbuds, [What Hi-Fi Awards] Air Bluetooth In-Ear Headphones with 4 Mics ENC, Sweatshield™ IPX7 Waterproof, Clear Sound, Deep Bass, Wireless Charge, Game Mode, App Customize EQ, 35H
reviewText: These are great value. I bought a couple of pairs of these a while ago for my kids as a budget alternative to Apple AirPods. I borrowed my son’s and I was so impressed by the sound quality I bought myself a pair too.They fit my ears far better than AirPods and don’t fall out at all - which AirPods seem to do all the time (especially when I’m running) and because of how well they fit, these block out external sound far better (they’re not noise cancelling, but the rubber ear tips do a great job of creating a seal in your ear to block out external sound).Unlike AirPods they can only connect with and remember one device at time. But reconnecting is very easy with the button on the charge case.They hold their charge really well and last a long time. I have no reason at the moment to doubt the 35+ hour claim on the box, but I’ve come no where near testing them for this long.Sound is good, although I actually find them a bit too bassy with some tracks - but this only makes me lower the volume, which can’t be a bad thing.

productTitle: EarFun Wireless Earbuds, [What Hi-Fi Awards] Air Bluetooth In-Ear Headphones with 4 Mics ENC, Sweatshield™ IPX7 Waterproof, Clear Sound, Deep Bass, Wireless Charge, Game Mode, App Customize EQ, 35H
reviewTitle: Great battery life, a bit uncomfortable.
reviewText: Amazing value, really solid pair of earbuds. In particular, the battery life is great and they connect and disconnect easily. Mic seems good and nice sound.I have found them a little uncomfortable to wear though. Sometimes, after an hour or so, I have to take them out. I have tried a few different sizes to the squidgy bits that probably have a name, but no good fit for long term use so far.I saw some reviews complaining the touch controls were too sensitive. I have the opposite problem. Pausing and playing a podcast with a double tap sometimes works and sometimes doesn't. I've found two very firm taps works best. But it is annoying as it takes me 5-6 seconds to get it to pause sometimes and with each failed attempt I'm often changing the volume (single tap).Also, for those other single bud-at-a-timers, volume up is on 1 bud, and volume down is on the other, so you won't be able to effectively use the volume controls when using only 1 bud.

productTitle: EarFun Wireless Earbuds, [What Hi-Fi Awards] Air Bluetooth In-Ear Headphones with 4 Mics ENC, Sweatshield™ IPX7 Waterproof, Clear Sound, Deep Bass, Wireless Charge, Game Mode, App Customize EQ, 35H
reviewText: I did some research before purchasing these earbuds and have to say that so far with a week or so of use have been very happy with the performance.  As noted elsewhere,  the bass is a tad forward but not unpleasantly so. With most audiophile equipment, a burn in period is to be expected.  These, right off the bat performed better than I had expected.  To my ears the treble is fine - clear and crisp, providing a fairly well defined sound stage that belies their modest price. Battery life compares well with more premium buds and range is equally good. I have no need for noise cancelling so do not miss this feature and currently rate these for audio, fitment and battery life to be at least equal to my premium earbuds. Absolutely excellent value to performance ratio!

productTitle: EarFun Wireless Earbuds, [What Hi-Fi Awards] Air Bluetooth In-Ear Headphones with 4 Mics ENC, Sweatshield™ IPX7 Waterproof, Clear Sound, Deep Bass, Wireless Charge, Game Mode, App Customize EQ, 35H
reviewText: Until I read the What HiFi 5 Star review, I didn’t know anything about Earfun products. My £180 Apple AirPods are giving up; 30 mins and the right hand, errr, “pod” gives up… and the left one follows 10 mins later. Do I want to spend another £200 on a pair of EarPods when these are somewhat more economical? No. So I bought these and theybare…. they are FANTASTIC! They’re as good as the Californian (Chinese) products; just as well made, are brilliantly designed, have a longer life, stay in your ear comfortably, charge wirelessly, are perfect to use in the gym as they’re IPX7 rated… and that means ensures that the device can be submerged in water up to 1 meter for 30 minutes without any damage - and finally and most importantly, they sound absolutely superb. Just buy them before everyone finds out!

productTitle: EarFun Wireless Earbuds, [What Hi-Fi Awards] Air Bluetooth In-Ear Headphones with 4 Mics ENC, Sweatshield™ IPX7 Waterproof, Clear Sound, Deep Bass, Wireless Charge, Game Mode, App Customize EQ, 35H
reviewTitle: Ok sound, reliable connection and great battery life
reviewText: The case is much bulkier than expected, easily twice or three times the size of an AirPods case, not that easily pocketable.The sound is ok, I expected better. It's quite middle heavy, bass is not too bad but not much treble. It's excellent for calls and microphones seem to work well, not that great for music.It still deserves 5 stars from me if I factor in the price as setup was dead easy, it connects reliably, presented well in the box and the build quality seems good.Battery life is excellent, it is replacement for a dying pair of AirPods that only lasts 15 minutes after as little as 2 years of very light use.

productTitle: EarFun Wireless Earbuds, [What Hi-Fi Awards] Air Bluetooth In-Ear Headphones with 4 Mics ENC, Sweatshield™ IPX7 Waterproof, Clear Sound, Deep Bass, Wireless Charge, Game Mode, App Customize EQ, 35H
reviewText: Great price, especially when on sale. Good sound though the fit can be a bit tricky. Worth trying the different tips. productTitle: EarFun Wireless Earbuds, [What Hi-Fi Awards] Air Bluetooth In-Ear Headphones with 4 Mics ENC, Sweatshield™ IPX7 Waterproof, Clear Sound, Deep Bass, Wireless Charge, Game Mode, App Customize EQ, 35H
reviewText: **Updated... my earbuds were faulty and inrecieved a full refund (an offer of exchange was also made). Customer service for this company is OUTSTANDING. They reached out to me. I didn't have to do anything. I'd buy from them again and just hope I was unlucky with my previous product.**Ok. So these are a great price. They sound amazing! Way better than any other ear buds I've owned (maybe even better than my galaxy buds FE) and for me what's great is that they don't FALL OUT when I'm working out. They just stay in with the biggest ear buds attachment (other sizes included). They last last for ages on one charge (don't think I've I've even recharged the case yet!). HOWEVER, it's really p***ING me off that every time I use them I have to go to go through the whole pairing process every time! And sometimes they doesn't even work and they just don't connect. This is with a Samsung tab, s23 phone, and steam deck oled. It's really annoying to the point I'm using my old headphones, so what was the point in buying need ones? Waste of money I'm sad to say.

productTitle: EarFun Wireless Earbuds, [What Hi-Fi Awards] Air Bluetooth In-Ear Headphones with 4 Mics ENC, Sweatshield™ IPX7 Waterproof, Clear Sound, Deep Bass, Wireless Charge, Game Mode, App Customize EQ, 35H
reviewText: I’d live to have the money for some high end headphones like Bose etc, but wanted something I could use as hands free in the car. These came out well in reviews so decided to give them a go. They’re perfectly fine for the purpose, although the mic picks up a lot of background noise (I’m told) but I was pleasantly surprised by the quality of sound whilst playing music. They won’t compete with the high end products, but then they’re not £200. I now use them more for working out or when travelling and I’m very happy with them. They certainly feel like a more expensive set with good quality plastic and a decent feel, way better than my last unbranded set which were half the price but a 10th of the quality. If you’re on a budget and don’t expect studio quality sound, these are worth a fo

productTitle: EarFun Wireless Earbuds, [What Hi-Fi Awards] Air Bluetooth In-Ear Headphones with 4 Mics ENC, Sweatshield™ IPX7 Waterproof, Clear Sound, Deep Bass, Wireless Charge, Game Mode, App Customize EQ, 35H
reviewText: Amazing earbuds for the money. I was trying these to save money, on ridiculously overpriced earbuds which don’t last much past their guarantee period. It is just what I was looking for.

productTitle: EarFun Wireless Earbuds, [What Hi-Fi Awards] Air Bluetooth In-Ear Headphones with 4 Mics ENC, Sweatshield™ IPX7 Waterproof, Clear Sound, Deep Bass, Wireless Charge, Game Mode, App Customize EQ, 35H
reviewText: Well if youre reading reviews and thinking £26 must be no good well these buds punch way above their price and at rrp£50 would still be a steal.I read loads of reviews before taking the plunge and so glad i chose these set up in seconds and auto blutooth to phone everytime  and they sound amazing from spotify not using for phone calls only music but you will not be dosapointed and skipping tracks or adjusting volume via tap on left or right bud easy. Charging and battery life at 40hrs WOW. Very comfortable  and supplied with 5 different sets of ear pads. Why pay £££s when these 5star buds are just perfect.Highly recomend.

productTitle: EarFun Wireless Earbuds, [What Hi-Fi Awards] Air Bluetooth In-Ear Headphones with 4 Mics ENC, Sweatshield™ IPX7 Waterproof, Clear Sound, Deep Bass, Wireless Charge, Game Mode, App Customize EQ, 35H
reviewTitle: Constant problems but fast response from manufacturer
reviewText: I have had some kind of issue with these since day 1. The left earbud doesn't work very well. Most of the time when I try to use these in the morning I find that only the right headphone connects and though it is possible to solve this issue it is a frustrating 5 minutes of constantly attempting to pair them until it works.It also seems that once I've managed to connect to it, the left earbud wants to keep connecting even when it is in the charging case. Frustrating again when someone calls me and I miss it because the audio is only coming out of one earbud safely tucked away in its case where it is meant to be charging and not connected to my phone.Finally, they have recently become fully unusable thanks to the left earbud refusing to charge past about 10%. They last about 10 mins of my walk now before they start disrupting my music with "battery low... disconnected.... pairing... disconnected... pairing... battery low... disconnected... pairing... power off"UPDATE: The manufacturer was very responsive when I emailed and are sending a new pair. For now I am updating the review to 4 stars, which may further change depending on how the new pair perform.

productTitle: EarFun Wireless Earbuds, [What Hi-Fi Awards] Air Bluetooth In-Ear Headphones with 4 Mics ENC, Sweatshield™ IPX7 Waterproof, Clear Sound, Deep Bass, Wireless Charge, Game Mode, App Customize EQ, 35H
reviewText: Love these buds, very comfy fit great quality sound. They appear and look great. Agree with What Hi Fi said about them. Easy to operate excellent quality product.

productTitle: EarFun Wireless Earbuds, [What Hi-Fi Awards] Air Bluetooth In-Ear Headphones with 4 Mics ENC, Sweatshield™ IPX7 Waterproof, Clear Sound, Deep Bass, Wireless Charge, Game Mode, App Customize EQ, 35H
reviewTitle: Can’t believe how good these are for the money
reviewText: After misplacing my AirPods yet again, I decided to opt for a much cheaper alternative so I went with the whatHiFi recommendation and I genuinely can’t believe how good these sound. There easy to operate and the sound is superb for cheap earbuds. They also don’t fall out my ears the way the AirPods often did. Brilliant find for the money. Great sound and good bass.

productTitle: EarFun Wireless Earbuds, [What Hi-Fi Awards] Air Bluetooth In-Ear Headphones with 4 Mics ENC, Sweatshield™ IPX7 Waterproof, Clear Sound, Deep Bass, Wireless Charge, Game Mode, App Customize EQ, 35H
reviewText: These were recommended to me and as they are relatively cheap I decided to buy them. Easy to link to phone, comfortable, absolute bargain for the price.

productTitle: EarFun Wireless Earbuds, [What Hi-Fi Awards] Air Bluetooth In-Ear Headphones with 4 Mics ENC, Sweatshield™ IPX7 Waterproof, Clear Sound, Deep Bass, Wireless Charge, Game Mode, App Customize EQ, 35H
reviewText: Bought these after seeing their review in What HiFi. I needed to replace my running headphones that had given up after a couple of years of service and these sounded OK. Got a pair and I quite like them. But....I have found that they will not stay in my ears whilst running. Have been out a few times since getting them and they just won't stay in. The left one will pop out every hundred metres and the right every five hundred. It's annoying and frustrating. I've tried all of the ear tips but none of them help. I've even tried some from other headphones but I just cannot get them to fit well enough to stay in my ear. I am a bit of a sweaty runner so perhaps the added lubrication there is the cause. Maybe I have unusually large earholes. Who knows. I'd just say they are not really for running. For walking and less strenuous stuff they're perfectly fine. The sound is not the best, but I've paid much more for headphones that sound a lot worse. They're a bit heavy on the base but again, it's not like they're couple of hundred pounds and designed for audiophiles. I'd say they were great value for money and good enough for day-to-day use, just not for running.

productTitle: EarFun Wireless Earbuds, [What Hi-Fi Awards] Air Bluetooth In-Ear Headphones with 4 Mics ENC, Sweatshield™ IPX7 Waterproof, Clear Sound, Deep Bass, Wireless Charge, Game Mode, App Customize EQ, 35H
reviewTitle: Overall pretty decent for the money.
reviewText: You can certainly get cheaper and of course more expensive but these are good for the money, the touch controls work pretty well, something I had trouble with on a cheaper set and the sound quality is excellent, you may not think so if you're a bit of an audiophile.I've used them for an hour at the gym with no issues but early today, I was pottering around the house and they started to drop connection, I've got them paired to my Apple Watch so it wasn't a distance issue, and it hadn't been much more than and hour. I've popped them back on charge and I'll try them again, hopefully this isn't a sign of them starting to let me down as I'm otherwise very happy with them.

productTitle: EarFun Wireless Earbuds, [What Hi-Fi Awards] Air Bluetooth In-Ear Headphones with 4 Mics ENC, Sweatshield™ IPX7 Waterproof, Clear Sound, Deep Bass, Wireless Charge, Game Mode, App Customize EQ, 35H
reviewTitle: Awkward fitting and sensitivity and a terrible app
reviewText: The sound quality is pretty good (not 5* What Hifi) good strong bass. Ive tried all sizes of buds. None fit that well. Once fitted the odd shape means that do not hang vertically but the stems point directly forwards, and quite frankly they look ugly. Others have mentioned the annoying over sensitive touch pads. It is impossible to insert them without turning on an unwanted feature (game mode on, game mode off - in an annoying Chinese voice). Also they pair too eagerly and turn back on your music track that you have just closed. Once you get used to them they perform reasonably well and the volume control works. Pause and skip are a little more tricky. Unless you have an equaliser on your phone you will be disappointed with the Earfun app - it is absolutely abysmal with 4 presets that make almost no difference. I will keep them however as the sound is good. But I will only use them indoors as they look awful and fall out easily. A wired pair work best outside. A good effort Earfun, but these are a work in progress.

productTitle: EarFun Wireless Earbuds, [What Hi-Fi Awards] Air Bluetooth In-Ear Headphones with 4 Mics ENC, Sweatshield™ IPX7 Waterproof, Clear Sound, Deep Bass, Wireless Charge, Game Mode, App Customize EQ, 35H

reviewTitle: Very durable! Solid sound for price
reviewText: I have been using these for a year now. reviewText: Reviews over time became so important that it now seems they are not very useful. A simple question is why recommend headphones (by what hifi) when they have a major flaw? How can these be 5/5 when they have a major flaw for everyone?The flaw is simple, when you touch the headphones to adjust them falling out of your ear slightly, which almost everyone will, the volume goes up or down. There is no way to stop this. If you are connected to a tv it also shoots up an incredible amount. Along with a beeping noise, it means you're always at odds adjusting the sound. It shouldn't be like this, they must have found this when testing? If they did any..There needs to be a way to disable it, looking at their online support there is not.Also one of the earphones had a scuff and scratch on it and amazon makes it impossible to get money off nowadays and you'd have to just return it.

productTitle: EarFun Wireless Earbuds, [What Hi-Fi Awards] Air Bluetooth In-Ear Headphones with 4 Mics ENC, Sweatshield™ IPX7 Waterproof, Clear Sound, Deep Bass, Wireless Charge, Game Mode, App Customize EQ, 35H
reviewText: I bought these with very little expectation (who are EarFun?) but have been quite impressed with the quality of the product. They're not heavily used but the battery life is good. The box can be charged that in turn keeps the pods charged which I guess is standard for this type of thing. Coloured LEDs aplenty. The bass isn't absolutely fantastic but is adequate. I would definitely recommend if you don't fancy paying for the high end fashionable equivalent.

reviewText: I paid a reasonable price for these headphones and they actually have  a really good sound quality for this price range. I am well pleased with them and feel like I got a good deal.

reviewTitle: Hard to beat at the price point.
reviewText: Brought my first pair 2 years ago as a cheap pair of headphones for the gym so I wouldn't be too bothered if I broke or lost them, Lasted quiet well in my opinion & ordered my second pair as one of the buds from the original pair has developed a problem where it chooses randomly when it wants to play sound but for the £20 something I paid for them & the 2 years of daily use, wear and tear & never being off full volume I can't be mad about them when they cost less than a takeaway in current times.While the sound isn't as good as the Bose pair I used before them, At the price point and how long they have lasted I really can't complain. If your a audiophile definitely not the pair for you but if your just an average Joe just wanting to listen to your music/podcast etc they hard to beat especially when they are on offer.Battery on these are excellent easily lasted me a 3+ weeks with my use before I had to plug in the carry case to charge.Overall really happy with them for the price, only issue I've ever had with them is when your sweating at the gym certain movements/exercises can cause them to slowly come out of your ears which mid set can be annoying but saying that they aren't uncomfortable & have never aggregate my ears like other pairs in the past have also the ear bud controls off the ear aren't the best imo but I just use my phone so isn't that much of a Biggie personally to me but other people might use that feature more.

reviewTitle: Quality product at pocket money price
reviewText: Got these to replace my JVC earbuds which were £25 on sale at Sainsburys [£50 before markdown]. Read the reviews in What HiFi and then saw them on offer. Was worried that the review might have been over enthusiastic. Needn't have worried, these earbuds are amazing  value for the money, and make the JVC buds sound inferior in every respect. These buds will only ever be used to listen to my audio collection via pc, so I can't comment on their functionality as a phone accessory. However, they perform brilliantly on a variety of music from my collection such as DSOTM, Tubular Bells, Big Big Train, Rolling Stones. Much better than the JVC's. The range of the buds is better as well, I can be anywhere on the ground and first floors and they don't lose signal. I apparently have small earholes, and these buds have the silicon earpieces that fit perfectly so that the sound is channelled into my ears. I would heartily recommend these earbuds and would consider purchasing other earfun products over some of the other Chinese brands that Amazon sells at the same price point.

productTitle: EarFun Wireless Earbuds, [What Hi-Fi Awards] Air Bluetooth In-Ear Headphones with 4 Mics ENC, Sweatshield™ IPX7 Waterproof, Clear Sound, Deep Bass, Wireless Charge, Game Mode, App Customize EQ, 35H

productTitle: EarFun Wireless Earbuds, [What Hi-Fi Awards] Air Bluetooth In-Ear Headphones with 4 Mics ENC, Sweatshield™ IPX7 Waterproof, Clear Sound, Deep Bass, Wireless Charge, Game Mode, App Customize EQ, 35H
reviewText: Pros:-- Fantastic sound quality for music, media and calls-- Holds charge well-- Left & Right clearly marked-- There's only one way to fit the buds back in the pod (hard to get it wrong)-- No wires to tangle-- Different sized tips included-- Cleaning wand included-- Thoughtfully designed all roundCons:-- The touch controls are a faff-- Potentially easy to loseI've been reluctant to get some of these because I'm great at losing things; I didn't think they'd sound better than wired headphones; and I'm annoyed with phone companies for removing the headphone jacks from their high end phones.But these EarFün buds sound soooo good that I'm a total convert!I got some OnePlus wireless buds for my OnePlus phone, but I kept seeing these in reviews coming up as top value, and great sound for the price.I can't resist a deal, so I took a risk, and I'm very, very happy. These sound better than any wired earphones I've experienced and better than the OnePlus ones I've got.They're not perfect; the touch controls are hard to memorize, and a bit of a faff; and I imagine they're easy to lose!But they're so good that, if I lose one, I'll re-buy this same pair rather than stick with my back-ups.

productTitle: EarFun Wireless Earbuds, [What Hi-Fi Awards] Air Bluetooth In-Ear Headphones with 4 Mics ENC, Sweatshield™ IPX7 Waterproof, Clear Sound, Deep Bass, Wireless Charge, Game Mode, App Customize EQ, 35H
reviewText: Very happy with these earbuds. Having auditioned several pairs of over-ear headphones around the £200 mark and been disappointed with the sound quality, I decided to try an inexpensive pair of earbuds to test the waters. I really like the sound quality and dynamic reproduction of these.For the money, I have no complaints, except to say that non of the supplied silicone ear tips suited my ears so I bought some others and found a foam pair that helps me enjoy the EarFun's even more.Very happy.

productTitle: EarFun Wireless Earbuds, [What Hi-Fi Awards] Air Bluetooth In-Ear Headphones with 4 Mics ENC, Sweatshield™ IPX7 Waterproof, Clear Sound, Deep Bass, Wireless Charge, Game Mode, App Customize EQ, 35H
reviewText: If you can live with the performance issues then these are a solid purchase at the price point. Since buying, I have endured several irritating problems, including the earbuds failing to connect necessitating forgetting/resetting the Bluetooth pairing, one earbud failing to connect while the other works fine, both earbuds disconnecting during use, the battery dropping off suddenly despite being at 60%, and others. While not deal-breaking on their own, these problems, combined, can be a serious annoyance.Despite this, the sound is great for the price. It lacks a bit of clarity at higher volumes, particularly listening to louder, more complex genres like metal, but the sound is full-bodied and punchy. It's a shame about the performance issues because without them, these would represent incredible value for money.

productTitle: EarFun Wireless Earbuds, [What Hi-Fi Awards] Air Bluetooth In-Ear Headphones with 4 Mics ENC, Sweatshield™ IPX7 Waterproof, Clear Sound, Deep Bass, Wireless Charge, Game Mode, App Customize EQ, 35H

productTitle: EarFun Wireless Earbuds, Air Pro Active Noise Cancelling Earbuds, Bluetooth Earphones with 6 Mics ENC Clear Call, 10 mm Big Drivers, Deep Bass, Fast USB-C Charge, 32Hrs, Ambient Mode for Office, Gym
reviewText: Amazing product. Seamless connectivity, reasonable noise cancellation, solid audio with decent bass. Can’t beat this for the money. I’ve no doubt that more expensive earphones are better but that’s not the point. Are they good enough for the daily commute? 100% yes.

productTitle: EarFun Wireless Earbuds, Air Pro Active Noise Cancelling Earbuds, Bluetooth Earphones with 6 Mics ENC Clear Call, 10 mm Big Drivers, Deep Bass, Fast USB-C Charge, 32Hrs, Ambient Mode for Office, Gym
reviewTitle: Powerful bass and clear sound. Inconvenient indication.
reviewText: This model is probably the first of EarFun list of crowdfunding company . The connection is very fast. The sound was amazing and clear. The bass is very powerful and it's amazing how such cheap headphones can have such an incredibly pleasant and clear bass!Control - 2 touches on the right - forward, etc.2 touches on the left - switch the ambient mode to normal mode and ANC.Of course, to expect some kind of noise reduction from the earbuds is ridiculous.However, the noise is slightly reduced in the subway.The headphones have very limited visible indicationThe charge indicator is on the back and very tiny.This is very, very inconvenient.When you open the box, you need to turn it and look at the indicator on the back - if it flashes 2-3 times, then the charge is enough. If it is 1 time, then you need to recharge it.This is the only disadvantage of this model.Conclusion - inexpensive, easy-to-use earbuds with powerful bass and crystal clear sound.A very inconvenient indication is the LED at the back and you need to get used to it.Recommend it to those who can get used to an inconvenient indication.

productTitle: EarFun Wireless Earbuds, Air Pro Active Noise Cancelling Earbuds, Bluetooth Earphones with 6 Mics ENC Clear Call, 10 mm Big Drivers, Deep Bass, Fast USB-C Charge, 32Hrs, Ambient Mode for Office, Gym
reviewText: Sound is quite good!Noise cancelling isn't great and I got them for the gym to drown out the crap music there.Wouldn't buy these for travelling on the plane of you're after good noise cancellation but if it's to walk around your area that isn't too loud, then you're ok.

productTitle: EarFun Wireless Earbuds, Air Pro Active Noise Cancelling Earbuds, Bluetooth Earphones with 6 Mics ENC Clear Call, 10 mm Big Drivers, Deep Bass, Fast USB-C Charge, 32Hrs, Ambient Mode for Office, Gym

productTitle: EarFun Wireless Earbuds, Air Pro Active Noise Cancelling Earbuds, Bluetooth Earphones with 6 Mics ENC Clear Call, 10 mm Big Drivers, Deep Bass, Fast USB-C Charge, 32Hrs, Ambient Mode for Office, Gym
reviewText: Pros:Sound quality pretty good for the priceDidn't break/or fail over 2 years medium usage.Well buildCons:Very average battery life plus left hand one always dies a lot faster than right oneRubbish tap function to switch modes. Almost always turns on Google assistant for some reason.Noise reduction only 'so so'Conclusion.If you can spend a bit more, go for it and buy something better.If super tight on budget, these earphones will still do the job in at least acceptable level. For this price can't really expect miracles.

productTitle: EarFun Wireless Earbuds, Air Pro Active Noise Cancelling Earbuds, Bluetooth Earphones with 6 Mics ENC Clear Call, 10 mm Big Drivers, Deep Bass, Fast USB-C Charge, 32Hrs, Ambient Mode for Office, Gym
reviewText: Had these less than a month and I’ve already reverted to my old earphones (which are a couple of years old and were about half the price brand new). Not satisfied at all.I initially bought these to replace my old ones and because I wanted something with noise cancelling. Unfortunately the shape and fit of these mean they keep wriggling out of my ears and are just really uncomfortable. There’s no volume controls on them which I think is pretty stingy in 2023/4. The noise cancelling has stopped working more than once (where it only switches between ambient and normal modes) and had to be reset to fix. The battery has also completely drained despite it being on charge throughout the previous night. I don’t think the product is faulty, I just think it’s bad quality.Ideally, I wish I could return them but since some of these issues weren’t immediate (and thus, I threw away the packaging), I’m not able too. Kind of feels like a waste of £50.

productTitle: EarFun Air Pro 3 Noise Cancelling Wireless Earbuds, Qualcomm® aptX™ Adaptive Sound, 6 Mics CVC 8.0 ENC, Bluetooth 5.3 Earbuds, Multipoint Connection, 45H Playtime, App Customize EQ, Pink
reviewText: I have had these earphones for about 3 months.I have had galaxy buds+ and galaxy buds pro 2 in the past before this and ended up losing one of my buds 2 pro's, decided to try and find something relatively cheap to replace it but still wanted features like dual connectivity and ANC.These earbuds have not let me down, the case is well made, the earbuds are well made, the sound quality is on par with the galaxy buds of not a little bit better making them great value for money.They're easy to use, the app has a lot of features such as your dual connectivity, charging levels and ANC.I have found that if I charge them once at the beginning of the week the battery life easily lasts all week with a few hours of listening each day.Honestly if you're considering these and are worried they'll be a downgrade in audio quality from your now aging galaxy buds or airpods then I can assure you that you will not be let down.Worth every penny.

productTitle: EarFun Air Pro 3 Noise Cancelling Wireless Earbuds, Qualcomm® aptX™ Adaptive Sound, 6 Mics CVC 8.0 ENC, Bluetooth 5.3 Earbuds, Multipoint Connection, 45H Playtime, App Customize EQ, Pink
reviewTitle: These are good little ear buds for the price!
reviewText: I was sceptical about the performance of these Earfun Air Pods Pro 3’s. I’m an Apple user and use mainly the AirPod pros and Powerbeats pro (for the gym).I wanted these mostly for the ANC function.My first impressions of these were overall very good.The ambient setting is not great - it causes a lot of hiss and does not give a very realistic impression of outside sounds. Needs improvement.The ANC really surprised me though how good it is. Plus the overall sound quality, once tweaked using the EarFun app is very good.So many quality features including wireless charging case, for such a great price, without really having to compromise on listening quality and performance.The battery life with ANC on, is in my real life use around 6 hours - which is acceptable. It says a lot when many reviews show these standing up against the Apple AirPod pro, that are in a completely different price bracket.The EarFun app could do with a revamp but that’s minor really. I prefer over ear buds for working out, but others may find that these work 0k - they seem a very good tight fit when you get the right size tips. They are also very comfortable for me.It would also be nice to have wear detection so that they turn off when you take them out.I guess some will be put off by the name (it’s not the greatest name is it?) - written on the buds showing the world that you like Earfun!If you can live with not having Apple kudos, these are pretty good earbuds, especially for the price.

productTitle: EarFun Air Pro 3 Noise Cancelling Wireless Earbuds, Qualcomm® aptX™ Adaptive Sound, 6 Mics CVC 8.0 ENC, Bluetooth 5.3 Earbuds, Multipoint Connection, 45H Playtime, App Customize EQ, Pink
reviewText: I have been searching for decently priced, good quality, noise cancelling earbuds since like, forever and, at last,  I think I have found them....Hallelujah!! I have resisted paying through the nose for the high end brands because, in my heart, I knew there had to be a company out there that was beavering away trying  to match the big guys for a fraction of the cost and, in my opinon, the Earfun Air Pro 3's come pretty damn close!The minute I put them in my ears and the music started I actually said, " Wow!" Clear sound and vocals and the base is brilliant. They are so comfortable, just make sure you fit the correct sized earplugs to make a good seal. Noise cancelling is good, I feel totally immersed in the sound..Honestly, they are worth every penny - I'm just amazed  for the amount I paid the quality of them.Do your ears a favour and go get them - You won't be sorry!

productTitle: EarFun Air Pro 3 Noise Cancelling Wireless Earbuds, Qualcomm® aptX™ Adaptive Sound, 6 Mics CVC 8.0 ENC, Bluetooth 5.3 Earbuds, Multipoint Connection, 45H Playtime, App Customize EQ, Pink
reviewText: I love bluetooth earbuds. They are so convenient, amazing tech and can have great sound in such a delicate package.I have some high end models from Sony and Bose, which are very good indeed.When I read the reviews of this Ear Fun model I was intrigued and decided to take the plunge and see for myself what these are like, for approx 20% of the price of my last Bose's.First impressions were good, a neat carry case, the alternative bud tips you would expect and a USB Connector.  You need to download the phone app if you want to change any control functions or equaliser settings, then ready to go. Straightforward.After opening this review by saying that I love bluetooth earbuds, I do accept that the sound quality will be compromised when compared to a proper Hi Fi or over ear earphones.  My Bose QC's are probably the best sound from a compact earbud - I've never experienced better, but these EarFuns, for a fraction of the price, are amazing.  Sound is clear and crisp.  Most of the time we wear earbuds we are on the move and there is extraneous sounds, movement , etc, and the EarFuns handled this well.Now, for the Noise Cancellation.  it is difficult to beat the Bose's for that too, sorry Sony.  Now, for 20% of the price , the EarFuns provide perfectly acceptable ANC, most annoying sounds are masked to an acceptable level.  I've tested them on planes and trains and they worked well. They do not rival high end products - but I don't expect that at this price point.All in all I think the Ear Fun Air Pro 3 is a bargain, a great all round product.

productTitle: EarFun Air Pro 3 Noise Cancelling Wireless Earbuds, Qualcomm® aptX™ Adaptive Sound, 6 Mics CVC 8.0 ENC, Bluetooth 5.3 Earbuds, Multipoint Connection, 45H Playtime, App Customize EQ, Pink
reviewTitle: Great Ear Buds that actually stay in your ears.
reviewText: I have always had problems keeping ear buds in place in my right ear. I’ve tried loads, including big A to no avail. I bought these to pair with my Apple watch. I have had them a week now, used them every day on an 8k run outside and they have been great. Pairing is a doddle as is setting up for individuality using the app on your phone. Once set up, pairing to the watch was a breeze. The fit is perfect, with a range of cups to choose from. Be aware to follow the instructions for inserting in the ear because the process is not the same as other brands. The controls are intuitive, based on a series of taps for different functions. Everything works well and is easy to remember. Sound cancelling and ambient sound features are impressive as is the sound quality. The sound equaliser in the app allows infinite flexibility to personalise your own settings. The charging case is a gem and is recharged via USB C connector or on a magsafe charger.I’m really chuffed to have finally found a set of earbuds that stay in my ears and function brilliantly.

productTitle: EarFun Air Pro 3 Noise Cancelling Wireless Earbuds, Qualcomm® aptX™ Adaptive Sound, 6 Mics CVC 8.0 ENC, Bluetooth 5.3 Earbuds, Multipoint Connection, 45H Playtime, App Customize EQ, Pink
reviewTitle: Great noise cancellation but audio is ok
reviewText: This is personally my second most liked pair of headphones. Deaccent hardware cursed with bad software.To start Bluetooth connection has been great and connects consistently and quickly. The connection stability has also been great as compared to other headphones its had very few dropped connections or patchy audio, even at a considerable distances.Noise cancelation, these are personally the best noise cancelling headphones as someone who commutes by bus which is very noise these have provided the best performance, even beating out more expensive options such as the soundcore Liberty 4 NC by a margin.The mic has been good, however robotic. What I mean by this calls sound fine but to people on the other side, voice sounds clear and isolated but robotic. There is probably some processing going but over all its not a bad thing as audio is clear despite sounding off.Sound, Unfortunately this is where it begins to falls short. The audio sounds ok, but in my opinion the bass is uncontrolled in the sense its a bit over the top. While rest sounds ok, with deaccent-ish clarity. With anc on vs off the audio does sound very different. The audio out of the box in my option is leaning on the inadequate side compared to what it is really capable of. A few firmware updates and messing in the eq it sounds good. Over all would recommend if your willing to spend a good few mins adjusting things.App. This is where it begins to fall flat. The app was buggy and the connection to the app was not always there which made it a headache to use when trying to use the app. The app if anything was the worst bit about this headphone, Personally I would recommend just to get there to update and adjust eq and change what the buttons do (like anc modes) so you don't need to return back to the software.The Multi-point over all for me was the deal-breaker for me unfortunately. As I work mainly in IT I use a lot of devices. The problem I had with the feature was not stability but choosing which device is connected and this is in part thanks to the app again. So for regular people who will only use 2 devices at most its fine.Comfort, The headphones for me where ok but long periods of time was not very comfortable and the ear tips them selves not a fan but they are fine. The overall shape of the ear bud is probably more suited to larger ears and the stem design, for my ear, at least did not sit well as I thought. Again theses are personal preferences as the person I gifted these on to said the fit was fine. But compared to his ears mine are more flat and soft, this MAY have played a role.Over all this is a ok product as the product was fine, but with app related compilations and the over all lack of clarity in the audio was the deciding factor for me to move to the soundcore 4 nc which is as if the time of writing £20 more. So if your looking for headphones on a tight £50 budget then yes. How ever if your able to spend the additional the amount of great options increase. On a final note the battery life was not the best when anc is on, this is expected but to the extent this happened not good,Note: Firmware update sin my experience have made significant differences so updating is a must and it is possible the issues addressed could be fixed in the future.

productTitle: EarFun Air Pro 3 Noise Cancelling Wireless Earbuds, Qualcomm® aptX™ Adaptive Sound, 6 Mics CVC 8.0 ENC, Bluetooth 5.3 Earbuds, Multipoint Connection, 45H Playtime, App Customize EQ, Pink
reviewTitle: Good sound but quiet. Good anc until but only low end.
reviewText: After 3 weeks of having these it's time to review, however for 2 reasona I will be returning.The good:-SOUND QUALITY-CHARGING CASE (sturdy, black so can't see scratches that much)-BATTERY LIFE (charging every few days, using 2-3 hours daily)-CONTROLS (easy to feel indented touchpad; many control options to customise including switching between ANC, Ambient, Normal)-controls are customisable in the app.-GAMING MODE (latency is still there, but one of best I tested for that price - on par with Soundpeats H2)-CALL QUALITY (everyone praises how well they can hear me comparing to Soundpeats Q. And I often were talking in really noisy environment.-ANC noise cancelling (good at mid to low frequencies. I've used it at home with noisy extractor hood in kitchen, works brilliantly.Also had them on the airplane and they block a lot of low hum rumble noise. Although I needed full volume (they're not that loud) the ANC was brilliant to have on the plane and I could hear movie dialogs good enough.-AMBIENT SOUND/TRANSPARENCY MODE (this one is brilliant and really helps when I'm talking to someone, does what it says on the tin and it's easy to activate!THE BAD:-VOLUME (they're really quiet if you're going bit deaf. My hearing is still quite good and I wish they were a tad louder. If not for ANC they wouldn't be good for listening to podcasts or movies in noisy environment.-ANC CRACKLES AT HIGH FREQUENCY NOISE (this is the worst and weakest point of these. When I use my tape measure and it "snaps" it causes really unpleasant crackle (unless normal mode on).Also happens when grinder is on. It's not a totally deafening sound, but I think it rather has got something to do with average quality microphones that just don't take high pitched noise. It's better to turn ANC off then as it really misses it's purpose.The above is the reason I'm returning these, where I work this issue makes them unbearable to wear.I'm giving these 4 stars, only 1 star taken for low volume and ANC crackling and high pitched noise.

reviewTitle: EarFun Air Pro 3 Wireless Earbuds: Value-Packed with Some Drawbacks
reviewText: The EarFun Air Pro 3 Wireless Earbuds offer an impressive array of features and sound quality at an affordable price point, making them an attractive option for budget-conscious consumers. However, while they excel in many areas, there are some drawbacks to consider.On the positive side, these earbuds deliver exceptional sound quality thanks to their Hybrid Active Noise Cancelling technology and 11mm wool drivers. Whether you're listening to music, podcasts, or taking calls, you can expect rich, immersive sound with deep bass and crisp highs. Additionally, the Bluetooth 5.3 connectivity ensures a stable and seamless wireless connection, allowing you to enjoy your audio content without interruption.Despite these strengths, one area where the EarFun Air Pro 3 earbuds fall short is in their microphone performance, particularly in open environments. While the 6 built-in microphones and CVC8.0 noise reduction technology excel in quiet settings, they struggle to deliver clear call quality in noisy environments. This can be particularly frustrating for users who rely on their earbuds for phone calls while on the go or in crowded spaces.However, it's essential to consider that these limitations are somewhat expected given the earbuds' affordable price point. For users primarily interested in high-quality audio playback and ANC performance, the EarFun Air Pro 3 earbuds offer excellent value for money.In conclusion, while the EarFun Air Pro 3 Wireless Earbuds have some drawbacks, such as subpar microphone performance in open environments, they still offer a compelling package of features and sound quality at an affordable price. If you can overlook this minor flaw, these earbuds provide a solid option for budget-conscious consumers looking for a reliable audio solution.

productTitle: EarFun Air Pro 3 Noise Cancelling Wireless Earbuds, Qualcomm® aptX™ Adaptive Sound, 6 Mics CVC 8.0 ENC, Bluetooth 5.3 Earbuds, Multipoint Connection, 45H Playtime, App Customize EQ, Pink
reviewText: Just wow is all I can say. I took 2 hours overall when I finally made the decision to buy this product.I had been looking at Denons technics and sonys as they are all well known brands, didn't like the look of them as they seemed really big and clunky, even so much as the samsung earpods.The advert for these kept popping up throughout my search, and each time I looked at the advert, as something about them was clearly drawing me to it.Everything in the advert was positive, even boasting about the fact that it had won a what hifi award, and a gold award in something else, so I thought they have to be worth a punt, given they were a decent price Compared to others.Well, if like me, you are browsing these earpods deliberating wether to buy them, or for example some Denons, and now reading my review, don't waste your money on ANY other earpods, these are exactly what you need, and the sound is really really impressive truly unreal, and very immersive.Being the type of person I am, I tried out lots of different genres, from dubstep to classical, each of which truly put them to the test. The sound is so crisp and clear, and the bass is clear, not like a fart in a baked bean tin.I wouldn't say I am an audiophile, but I do appreciate good sound music playing devices, that can carry a bit of bass, just to make it sound better, and I wasn't disappointed with these bad boys.Easy to pair to your phone, just open the case and then find them on Bluetooth. Noise cancelling feature seems OK, but not had a chance to properly test that yet, or clarity of calls yet, so unable to comment on those functions. As my main use will be for listening to music I am very happy with my purchase and you will be too if you buy them.

reviewText: ITEM BOUGHT RECEIVED: 12/08/2023Quality of it in terms of sound and features are amazing. The only problem with these earbuds which seems to be a common problem is the right ear bud. productTitle: EarFun Air Pro 3 Noise Cancelling Wireless Earbuds, Qualcomm® aptX™ Adaptive Sound, 6 Mics CVC 8.0 ENC, Bluetooth 5.3 Earbuds, Multipoint Connection, 45H Playtime, App Customize EQ, Pink
reviewText: I bought these after returning some Soundcore Liberty 4 NC earbuds that were great generally except they irritated my ears. These have a different design that I hoped meant they would not irritate my ears.I must admit I was sceptical about earbuds made by a company called 'EarFun' but they have impressed me hugely and don't irritate my ears like the Liberty 4's.What has impressed me:1. Sound - I like a 'deep V' shaped music response - strong bass response, a dip in the mid frequencies and then a strong treble response - these have this naturally as well as onboard 10-band EQ (most earbuds only have 5-band EQ). This allows me to get the strong bass thump I want.2. Noise cancelling - like the Liberty 4's, they have good noise cancelling for the price point, doesn't get rid of everything but gets rid of a lot. I've not tried them in a noisy environment properly yet.3. Multi-point - I can connect these to my laptop and my phone at the same time and they switch automatically depending upon what's playing audio. Useful for switching between watching TV on my laptop and listening to music on my phone. The app also allows me to explicitly disconnect from my other device easily.4. Comfort - no issues with irritation, they're very light and very comfortable. I can easily fall asleep with them in (useful for naps just using the noise cancelling to quieten the natural noises in my house).5. Controls - they are fully configurable with single-, double- and triple- taps plus hold configurable on both sides.Not really tested battery life yet or how well they work in an electrically noisy environment either but so far no issues on either front.I got these when they were on offer at 50 quid and at that price they are an absolute bargain!Highly recommended.

productTitle: EarFun Air Pro 3 Noise Cancelling Wireless Earbuds, Qualcomm® aptX™ Adaptive Sound, 6 Mics CVC 8.0 ENC, Bluetooth 5.3 Earbuds, Multipoint Connection, 45H Playtime, App Customize EQ, Pink
reviewTitle: If you like clear loud sound these are your buds!
reviewText: I was a bit skeptic to buy these as I’ve had Beats by Dre and AirPods etc and was disappointed with both.So I bought these because of the good reviews on Google as well as here plus they were reduced and I took the chance- they are the clearest and loudest earbuds I’ve ever had!EarFun do have an app you can install which I did and was pleasantly surprised that you can change or customise your sound experience, ie more bass or more treble etcI customised them to be the loudest and sharpest and it was well over my iPhone 15 decibels!They’re very easy to use and you can also customise the touch controls on the earbuds to either switch them around or just disable them plus they’re very comfortable for small awkward ears. :)Connectivity is fantastic I could have them blasting whilst my phone is away at the other side of the house, the charging is great also and they last for hours, I’ve fell asleep with them in and woke up next morning and they still had charge.Only thing I can think of is if you’re someone who LOVES a very heavy heavy bass then I don’t think they could give you it but I highly recommend these and I’m so glad I bought them!

reviewText: Considering these are 1/5 the price of AirPods I wasn’t expecting equal performance but I wasn’t disappointed. The noise cancellation is very good.
