productTitle: Air S Noise Cancelling Wireless Earbuds, Qualcomm® aptX™, 4 Mics CVC 8.0 Call, Multipoint Connection, Wireless Charging, 10mm Wool Composite Drivers, App with Custom EQ, Game Mode, White
reviewTitle: Delivers premium features at an amazing price!
reviewText: If you want an immediate "should i get these?" simply theyre now my daily earbud replacing ones costing triple.So the earbuds come in a nice simple package with a pleasant unboxing experiance. The case is made from the worst material for picking up finger prints but that means so little to how much they deliver. The earbuds are a little larger than usual but its because they've packed so much into it.Multi-point connection for pc/phone or phone/switch etcANC that works with ambient mode for being able to talk and listenAn app that lets you customise everything you need or turn off the touch function to use it fully through the app incase of accidental touches.fast charge option so 10 mins gives 2hrs and they last roughly 6hrs each capping at 30 with the casespeaking of charging, they also charge wirelessly!Gaming mode (although this turns off the skip back option for whatever they do to make it low latency)Using the qualcomm chip it has hands down the best voice quality i've ever heard on earbuds (simply check the video)the 2023 meme of "hows the snap?" solid, again, in the video.But first and foremost they have just such exceptional sound quality. There is a minor amount of sound bleed at full but anything under 80% and your fine. Mids and lows are exceptional, they are a touch bassy as their selling point with the treble a tiny bit off at stock but once its knocked down a touch in the app its clear strong and at all 3 levels theres no distortion.I really have to keep saying how much the sound quality has blown me away, the sound stage feels so wide and the instrumental separation is just the best i've heard on an earbud, theyre to the level of my philip studio headphones. Vs my older earbuds i simply hear parts so much clearer, notes i didnt hear before, the stereo mix from right to left feels almost 3D with some songs, i literally cant praise these enough.The only negative i could "kinda, sorta" find is more and more ear buds have more than just a dot to indicate how much battery they have. LCD screens with indicators etc even on the super budget priced but this is a small complaint vs what they deliver.

productTitle: EarFun Wireless Earbuds, Air Pro 3 Hybrid Active Noise Cancelling Qualcomm® aptX™ Adaptive Sound, 6 Mics CVC8.0 Calls, Bluetooth 5.3, 11mm Wool Drivers, Multi-Connection, App Customize EQ,45H
reviewText: I have had these earphones for about 3 months.I have had galaxy buds+ and galaxy buds pro 2 in the past before this and ended up losing one of my buds 2 pro's, decided to try and find something relatively cheap to replace it but still wanted features like dual connectivity and ANC.These earbuds have not let me down, the case is well made, the earbuds are well made, the sound quality is on par with the galaxy buds of not a little bit better making them great value for money.They're easy to use, the app has a lot of features such as your dual connectivity, charging levels and ANC.I have found that if I charge them once at the beginning of the week the battery life easily lasts all week with a few hours of listening each day.Honestly if you're considering these and are worried they'll be a downgrade in audio quality from your now aging galaxy buds or airpods then I can assure you that you will not be let down.Worth every penny.

productTitle: EarFun Wireless Earbuds, Air Pro 3 Hybrid Active Noise Cancelling Qualcomm® aptX™ Adaptive Sound, 6 Mics CVC8.0 Calls, Bluetooth 5.3, 11mm Wool Drivers, Multi-Connection, App Customize EQ,45H
reviewTitle: These are good little ear buds for the price!
reviewText: I was sceptical about the performance of these Earfun Air Pods Pro 3’s. I’m an Apple user and use mainly the AirPod pros and Powerbeats pro (for the gym).I wanted these mostly for the ANC function.My first impressions of these were overall very good.The ambient setting is not great - it causes a lot of hiss and does not give a very realistic impression of outside sounds. Needs improvement.The ANC really surprised me though how good it is. Plus the overall sound quality, once tweaked using the EarFun app is very good.So many quality features including wireless charging case, for such a great price, without really having to compromise on listening quality and performance.The battery life with ANC on, is in my real life use around 6 hours - which is acceptable. It says a lot when many reviews show these standing up against the Apple AirPod pro, that are in a completely different price bracket.The EarFun app could do with a revamp but that’s minor really. I prefer over ear buds for working out, but others may find that these work 0k - they seem a very good tight fit when you get the right size tips. They are also very comfortable for me.It would also be nice to have wear detection so that they turn off when you take them out.I guess some will be put off by the name (it’s not the greatest name is it?) - written on the buds showing the world that you like Earfun!If you can live with not having Apple kudos, these are pretty good earbuds, especially for the price.

productTitle: EarFun Wireless Earbuds, Air Pro 3 Hybrid Active Noise Cancelling Qualcomm® aptX™ Adaptive Sound, 6 Mics CVC8.0 Calls, Bluetooth 5.3, 11mm Wool Drivers, Multi-Connection, App Customize EQ,45H
reviewText: I have been searching for decently priced, good quality, noise cancelling earbuds since like, forever and, at last,  I think I have found them....Hallelujah!! I have resisted paying through the nose for the high end brands because, in my heart, I knew there had to be a company out there that was beavering away trying  to match the big guys for a fraction of the cost and, in my opinon, the Earfun Air Pro 3's come pretty damn close!The minute I put them in my ears and the music started I actually said, " Wow!" Clear sound and vocals and the base is brilliant. They are so comfortable, just make sure you fit the correct sized earplugs to make a good seal. Noise cancelling is good, I feel totally immersed in the sound..Honestly, they are worth every penny - I'm just amazed  for the amount I paid the quality of them.Do your ears a favour and go get them - You won't be sorry!

productTitle: EarFun Wireless Earbuds, Air Pro 3 Hybrid Active Noise Cancelling Qualcomm® aptX™ Adaptive Sound, 6 Mics CVC8.0 Calls, Bluetooth 5.3, 11mm Wool Drivers, Multi-Connection, App Customize EQ,45H
reviewText: I love bluetooth earbuds. They are so convenient, amazing tech and can have great sound in such a delicate package.I have some high end models from Sony and Bose, which are very good indeed.When I read the reviews of this Ear Fun model I was intrigued and decided to take the plunge and see for myself what these are like, for approx 20% of the price of my last Bose's.First impressions were good, a neat carry case, the alternative bud tips you would expect and a USB Connector.  You need to download the phone app if you want to change any control functions or equaliser settings, then ready to go. Straightforward.After opening this review by saying that I love bluetooth earbuds, I do accept that the sound quality will be compromised when compared to a proper Hi Fi or over ear earphones.  My Bose QC's are probably the best sound from a compact earbud - I've never experienced better, but these EarFuns, for a fraction of the price, are amazing.  Sound is clear and crisp.  Most of the time we wear earbuds we are on the move and there is extraneous sounds, movement , etc, and the EarFuns handled this well.Now, for the Noise Cancellation.  it is difficult to beat the Bose's for that too, sorry Sony.  Now, for 20% of the price , the EarFuns provide perfectly acceptable ANC, most annoying sounds are masked to an acceptable level.  I've tested them on planes and trains and they worked well. They do not rival high end products - but I don't expect that at this price point.All in all I think the Ear Fun Air Pro 3 is a bargain, a great all round product.

productTitle: EarFun Wireless Earbuds, Air Pro 3 Hybrid Active Noise Cancelling Qualcomm® aptX™ Adaptive Sound, 6 Mics CVC8.0 Calls, Bluetooth 5.3, 11mm Wool Drivers, Multi-Connection, App Customize EQ,45H
reviewTitle: Good sound but quiet. Good anc until but only low end.
reviewText: After 3 weeks of having these it's time to review, however for 2 reasona I will be returning.The good:-SOUND QUALITY-CHARGING CASE (sturdy, black so can't see scratches that much)-BATTERY LIFE (charging every few days, using 2-3 hours daily)-CONTROLS (easy to feel indented touchpad; many control options to customise including switching between ANC, Ambient, Normal)-controls are customisable in the app.-GAMING MODE (latency is still there, but one of best I tested for that price - on par with Soundpeats H2)-CALL QUALITY (everyone praises how well they can hear me comparing to Soundpeats Q. And I often were talking in really noisy environment.-ANC noise cancelling (good at mid to low frequencies. I've used it at home with noisy extractor hood in kitchen, works brilliantly.Also had them on the airplane and they block a lot of low hum rumble noise. Although I needed full volume (they're not that loud) the ANC was brilliant to have on the plane and I could hear movie dialogs good enough.-AMBIENT SOUND/TRANSPARENCY MODE (this one is brilliant and really helps when I'm talking to someone, does what it says on the tin and it's easy to activate!THE BAD:-VOLUME (they're really quiet if you're going bit deaf. My hearing is still quite good and I wish they were a tad louder. If not for ANC they wouldn't be good for listening to podcasts or movies in noisy environment.-ANC CRACKLES AT HIGH FREQUENCY NOISE (this is the worst and weakest point of these. When I use my tape measure and it "snaps" it causes really unpleasant crackle (unless normal mode on).Also happens when grinder is on. It's not a totally deafening sound, but I think it rather has got something to do with average quality microphones that just don't take high pitched noise. It's better to turn ANC off then as it really misses it's purpose.The above is the reason I'm returning these, where I work this issue makes them unbearable to wear.I'm giving these 4 stars, only 1 star taken for low volume and ANC crackling and high pitched noise.

productTitle: EarFun Wireless Earbuds, Air Pro 3 Hybrid Active Noise Cancelling Qualcomm® aptX™ Adaptive Sound, 6 Mics CVC8.0 Calls, Bluetooth 5.3, 11mm Wool Drivers, Multi-Connection, App Customize EQ,45H
reviewTitle: Great noise cancellation but audio is ok
reviewText: This is personally my second most liked pair of headphones. Deaccent hardware cursed with bad software.To start Bluetooth connection has been great and connects consistently and quickly. The connection stability has also been great as compared to other headphones its had very few dropped connections or patchy audio, even at a considerable distances.Noise cancelation, these are personally the best noise cancelling headphones as someone who commutes by bus which is very noise these have provided the best performance, even beating out more expensive options such as the soundcore Liberty 4 NC by a margin.The mic has been good, however robotic. What I mean by this calls sound fine but to people on the other side, voice sounds clear and isolated but robotic. There is probably some processing going but over all its not a bad thing as audio is clear despite sounding off.Sound, Unfortunately this is where it begins to falls short. The audio sounds ok, but in my opinion the bass is uncontrolled in the sense its a bit over the top. While rest sounds ok, with deaccent-ish clarity. With anc on vs off the audio does sound very different. The audio out of the box in my option is leaning on the inadequate side compared to what it is really capable of. A few firmware updates and messing in the eq it sounds good. Over all would recommend if your willing to spend a good few mins adjusting things.App. This is where it begins to fall flat. The app was buggy and the connection to the app was not always there which made it a headache to use when trying to use the app. The app if anything was the worst bit about this headphone, Personally I would recommend just to get there to update and adjust eq and change what the buttons do (like anc modes) so you don't need to return back to the software.The Multi-point over all for me was the deal-breaker for me unfortunately. As I work mainly in IT I use a lot of devices. The problem I had with the feature was not stability but choosing which device is connected and this is in part thanks to the app again. So for regular people who will only use 2 devices at most its fine.Comfort, The headphones for me where ok but long periods of time was not very comfortable and the ear tips them selves not a fan but they are fine. The overall shape of the ear bud is probably more suited to larger ears and the stem design, for my ear, at least did not sit well as I thought. Again theses are personal preferences as the person I gifted these on to said the fit was fine. But compared to his ears mine are more flat and soft, this MAY have played a role.Over all this is a ok product as the product was fine, but with app related compilations and the over all lack of clarity in the audio was the deciding factor for me to move to the soundcore 4 nc which is as if the time of writing £20 more. So if your looking for headphones on a tight £50 budget then yes. How ever if your able to spend the additional the amount of great options increase. On a final note the battery life was not the best when anc is on, this is expected but to the extent this happened not good,Note: Firmware update sin my experience have made significant differences so updating is a must and it is possible the issues addressed could be fixed in the future.

productTitle: EarFun Wireless Earbuds, Air Pro 3 Hybrid Active Noise Cancelling Qualcomm® aptX™ Adaptive Sound, 6 Mics CVC8.0 Calls, Bluetooth 5.3, 11mm Wool Drivers, Multi-Connection, App Customize EQ,45H

reviewTitle: EarFun Air Pro 3 Wireless Earbuds: Value-Packed with Some Drawbacks
reviewText: The EarFun Air Pro 3 Wireless Earbuds offer an impressive array of features and sound quality at an affordable price point, making them an attractive option for budget-conscious consumers. However, while they excel in many areas, there are some drawbacks to consider.On the positive side, these earbuds deliver exceptional sound quality thanks to their Hybrid Active Noise Cancelling technology and 11mm wool drivers. Whether you're listening to music, podcasts, or taking calls, you can expect rich, immersive sound with deep bass and crisp highs. Additionally, the Bluetooth 5.3 connectivity ensures a stable and seamless wireless connection, allowing you to enjoy your audio content without interruption.Despite these strengths, one area where the EarFun Air Pro 3 earbuds fall short is in their microphone performance, particularly in open environments. While the 6 built-in microphones and CVC8.0 noise reduction technology excel in quiet settings, they struggle to deliver clear call quality in noisy environments. This can be particularly frustrating for users who rely on their earbuds for phone calls while on the go or in crowded spaces.However, it's essential to consider that these limitations are somewhat expected given the earbuds' affordable price point. For users primarily interested in high-quality audio playback and ANC performance, the EarFun Air Pro 3 earbuds offer excellent value for money.In conclusion, while the EarFun Air Pro 3 Wireless Earbuds have some drawbacks, such as subpar microphone performance in open environments, they still offer a compelling package of features and sound quality at an affordable price. If you can overlook this minor flaw, these earbuds provide a solid option for budget-conscious consumers looking for a reliable audio solution.

productTitle: EarFun Wireless Earbuds, Air Pro 3 Hybrid Active Noise Cancelling Qualcomm® aptX™ Adaptive Sound, 6 Mics CVC8.0 Calls, Bluetooth 5.3, 11mm Wool Drivers, Multi-Connection, App Customize EQ,45H
reviewText: Just wow is all I can say. I took 2 hours overall when I finally made the decision to buy this product.I had been looking at Denons technics and sonys as they are all well known brands, didn't like the look of them as they seemed really big and clunky, even so much as the samsung earpods.The advert for these kept popping up throughout my search, and each time I looked at the advert, as something about them was clearly drawing me to it.Everything in the advert was positive, even boasting about the fact that it had won a what hifi award, and a gold award in something else, so I thought they have to be worth a punt, given they were a decent price Compared to others.Well, if like me, you are browsing these earpods deliberating wether to buy them, or for example some Denons, and now reading my review, don't waste your money on ANY other earpods, these are exactly what you need, and the sound is really really impressive truly unreal, and very immersive.Being the type of person I am, I tried out lots of different genres, from dubstep to classical, each of which truly put them to the test. The sound is so crisp and clear, and the bass is clear, not like a fart in a baked bean tin.I wouldn't say I am an audiophile, but I do appreciate good sound music playing devices, that can carry a bit of bass, just to make it sound better, and I wasn't disappointed with these bad boys.Easy to pair to your phone, just open the case and then find them on Bluetooth. Noise cancelling feature seems OK, but not had a chance to properly test that yet, or clarity of calls yet, so unable to comment on those functions. As my main use will be for listening to music I am very happy with my purchase and you will be too if you buy them.

reviewTitle: Great Ear Buds that actually stay in your ears.
reviewText: I have always had problems keeping ear buds in place in my right ear. I’ve tried loads, including big A to no avail. I bought these to pair with my Apple watch. I have had them a week now, used them every day on an 8k run outside and they have been great. Pairing is a doddle as is setting up for individuality using the app on your phone. Once set up, pairing to the watch was a breeze. The fit is perfect, with a range of cups to choose from. Be aware to follow the instructions for inserting in the ear because the process is not the same as other brands. The controls are intuitive, based on a series of taps for different functions. Everything works well and is easy to remember. Sound cancelling and ambient sound features are impressive as is the sound quality. The sound equaliser in the app allows infinite flexibility to personalise your own settings. The charging case is a gem and is recharged via USB C connector or on a magsafe charger.I’m really chuffed to have finally found a set of earbuds that stay in my ears and function brilliantly.

productTitle: EarFun Wireless Earbuds, Air Pro 3 Hybrid Active Noise Cancelling Qualcomm® aptX™ Adaptive Sound, 6 Mics CVC8.0 Calls, Bluetooth 5.3, 11mm Wool Drivers, Multi-Connection, App Customize EQ,45H
reviewText: Great value. Does everything you need.  The sound quality is good for the price point. The fit is not bad, the touch functions to increase volume and decrease volume is good sometimes I get confused and disconnect calls but I think that is a matter of getting used to it.  I like the battery life, I listen to music for an hour and talk on it for 2 and charge it once every 2-3 days.  But it's been less than a month of using it will be good to see if the performance is consistent over the longer term.  The only issue I have is that the microphone is not as sensitive as some of the higher end ones and I need to be a bit louder when speaking that I normally would be but only marginally so.  Great product I think.

reviewText: ITEM BOUGHT RECEIVED: 12/08/2023Quality of it in terms of sound and features are amazing. The only problem with these earbuds which seems to be a common problem is the right ear bud. productTitle: EarFun Wireless Earbuds, Air Pro 3 Hybrid Active Noise Cancelling Qualcomm® aptX™ Adaptive Sound, 6 Mics CVC8.0 Calls, Bluetooth 5.3, 11mm Wool Drivers, Multi-Connection, App Customize EQ,45H
reviewText: I bought these after returning some Soundcore Liberty 4 NC earbuds that were great generally except they irritated my ears. These have a different design that I hoped meant they would not irritate my ears.I must admit I was sceptical about earbuds made by a company called 'EarFun' but they have impressed me hugely and don't irritate my ears like the Liberty 4's.What has impressed me:1. Sound - I like a 'deep V' shaped music response - strong bass response, a dip in the mid frequencies and then a strong treble response - these have this naturally as well as onboard 10-band EQ (most earbuds only have 5-band EQ). This allows me to get the strong bass thump I want.2. Noise cancelling - like the Liberty 4's, they have good noise cancelling for the price point, doesn't get rid of everything but gets rid of a lot. I've not tried them in a noisy environment properly yet.3. Multi-point - I can connect these to my laptop and my phone at the same time and they switch automatically depending upon what's playing audio. Useful for switching between watching TV on my laptop and listening to music on my phone. The app also allows me to explicitly disconnect from my other device easily.4. Comfort - no issues with irritation, they're very light and very comfortable. I can easily fall asleep with them in (useful for naps just using the noise cancelling to quieten the natural noises in my house).5. Controls - they are fully configurable with single-, double- and triple- taps plus hold configurable on both sides.Not really tested battery life yet or how well they work in an electrically noisy environment either but so far no issues on either front.I got these when they were on offer at 50 quid and at that price they are an absolute bargain!Highly recommended.

productTitle: EarFun Wireless Earbuds, Air Pro 3 Hybrid Active Noise Cancelling Qualcomm® aptX™ Adaptive Sound, 6 Mics CVC8.0 Calls, Bluetooth 5.3, 11mm Wool Drivers, Multi-Connection, App Customize EQ,45H
reviewTitle: If you like clear loud sound these are your buds!
reviewText: I was a bit skeptic to buy these as I’ve had Beats by Dre and AirPods etc and was disappointed with both.So I bought these because of the good reviews on Google as well as here plus they were reduced and I took the chance- they are the clearest and loudest earbuds I’ve ever had!EarFun do have an app you can install which I did and was pleasantly surprised that you can change or customise your sound experience, ie more bass or more treble etcI customised them to be the loudest and sharpest and it was well over my iPhone 15 decibels!They’re very easy to use and you can also customise the touch controls on the earbuds to either switch them around or just disable them plus they’re very comfortable for small awkward ears. :)Connectivity is fantastic I could have them blasting whilst my phone is away at the other side of the house, the charging is great also and they last for hours, I’ve fell asleep with them in and woke up next morning and they still had charge.Only thing I can think of is if you’re someone who LOVES a very heavy heavy bass then I don’t think they could give you it but I highly recommend these and I’m so glad I bought them!

reviewText: The Airfun 3 earbuds live up to the description. The ANC is very good, the sound quality is superb, they are comfortable to wear for extended periods, and the battery life is impressive.The available phone software allows you to 'tune' the frequencies to your hearing, something that even my Apple Airpod Pro 2 doesn't have.30% of the cost of Airpods, 80% of the quality. reviewText: Considering these are 1/5 the price of AirPods I wasn’t expecting equal performance but I wasn’t disappointed. The noise cancellation is very good. Would buy again when I inevitably lose these!Update: I’ve finally had to charge them, nearly a month after purchase! I’m so impressed with them. If I had to find fault I’d like there to be more than 3 charge indicator lights so you have a better idea of when it’ll run out of battery but that’s just nitpicking. I can honestly say they’re the best earbuds I’ve ever had.

reviewText: I only had one complaint when I got these but they amazingly updated it so it is now perfect. For eg one or two taps for pause or Siri etc. you can also change the EQ. The headphones are comfortable for in-ears and they are sturdy. I had a problem and the customer service was quick and very generous with 18 month warranty! It’s nice to support business who care. Very different from what u get with Apple with is expensive and you already know it’s a hassle if something is wrong.

productTitle: Air Pro 3 Noise Cancelling Wireless Earbuds, Qualcomm® aptX™ Adaptive Sound, 6 Mics CVC 8.0 ENC, Bluetooth 5.3 Earbuds, Multipoint Connection, 45H Playtime, App Customize EQ, Oat White
reviewText: I have had these earphones for about 3 months.I have had galaxy buds+ and galaxy buds pro 2 in the past before this and ended up losing one of my buds 2 pro's, decided to try and find something relatively cheap to replace it but still wanted features like dual connectivity and ANC.These earbuds have not let me down, the case is well made, the earbuds are well made, the sound quality is on par with the galaxy buds of not a little bit better making them great value for money.They're easy to use, the app has a lot of features such as your dual connectivity, charging levels and ANC.I have found that if I charge them once at the beginning of the week the battery life easily lasts all week with a few hours of listening each day.Honestly if you're considering these and are worried they'll be a downgrade in audio quality from your now aging galaxy buds or airpods then I can assure you that you will not be let down.Worth every penny.

productTitle: Air Pro 3 Noise Cancelling Wireless Earbuds, Qualcomm® aptX™ Adaptive Sound, 6 Mics CVC 8.0 ENC, Bluetooth 5.3 Earbuds, Multipoint Connection, 45H Playtime, App Customize EQ, Oat White
reviewTitle: These are good little ear buds for the price!
reviewText: I was sceptical about the performance of these Earfun Air Pods Pro 3’s. I’m an Apple user and use mainly the AirPod pros and Powerbeats pro (for the gym).I wanted these mostly for the ANC function.My first impressions of these were overall very good.The ambient setting is not great - it causes a lot of hiss and does not give a very realistic impression of outside sounds. Needs improvement.The ANC really surprised me though how good it is. Plus the overall sound quality, once tweaked using the EarFun app is very good.So many quality features including wireless charging case, for such a great price, without really having to compromise on listening quality and performance.The battery life with ANC on, is in my real life use around 6 hours - which is acceptable. It says a lot when many reviews show these standing up against the Apple AirPod pro, that are in a completely different price bracket.The EarFun app could do with a revamp but that’s minor really. I prefer over ear buds for working out, but others may find that these work 0k - they seem a very good tight fit when you get the right size tips. They are also very comfortable for me.It would also be nice to have wear detection so that they turn off when you take them out.I guess some will be put off by the name (it’s not the greatest name is it?) - written on the buds showing the world that you like Earfun!If you can live with not having Apple kudos, these are pretty good earbuds, especially for the price.

productTitle: Air Pro 3 Noise Cancelling Wireless Earbuds, Qualcomm® aptX™ Adaptive Sound, 6 Mics CVC 8.0 ENC, Bluetooth 5.3 Earbuds, Multipoint Connection, 45H Playtime, App Customize EQ, Oat White
reviewText: I have been searching for decently priced, good quality, noise cancelling earbuds since like, forever and, at last,  I think I have found them....Hallelujah!! I have resisted paying through the nose for the high end brands because, in my heart, I knew there had to be a company out there that was beavering away trying  to match the big guys for a fraction of the cost and, in my opinon, the Earfun Air Pro 3's come pretty damn close!The minute I put them in my ears and the music started I actually said, " Wow!" Clear sound and vocals and the base is brilliant. They are so comfortable, just make sure you fit the correct sized earplugs to make a good seal. Noise cancelling is good, I feel totally immersed in the sound..Honestly, they are worth every penny - I'm just amazed  for the amount I paid the quality of them.Do your ears a favour and go get them - You won't be sorry!

productTitle: Air Pro 3 Noise Cancelling Wireless Earbuds, Qualcomm® aptX™ Adaptive Sound, 6 Mics CVC 8.0 ENC, Bluetooth 5.3 Earbuds, Multipoint Connection, 45H Playtime, App Customize EQ, Oat White
reviewText: I love bluetooth earbuds. They are so convenient, amazing tech and can have great sound in such a delicate package.I have some high end models from Sony and Bose, which are very good indeed.When I read the reviews of this Ear Fun model I was intrigued and decided to take the plunge and see for myself what these are like, for approx 20% of the price of my last Bose's.First impressions were good, a neat carry case, the alternative bud tips you would expect and a USB Connector.  You need to download the phone app if you want to change any control functions or equaliser settings, then ready to go. Straightforward.After opening this review by saying that I love bluetooth earbuds, I do accept that the sound quality will be compromised when compared to a proper Hi Fi or over ear earphones.  My Bose QC's are probably the best sound from a compact earbud - I've never experienced better, but these EarFuns, for a fraction of the price, are amazing.  Sound is clear and crisp.  Most of the time we wear earbuds we are on the move and there is extraneous sounds, movement , etc, and the EarFuns handled this well.Now, for the Noise Cancellation.  it is difficult to beat the Bose's for that too, sorry Sony.  Now, for 20% of the price , the EarFuns provide perfectly acceptable ANC, most annoying sounds are masked to an acceptable level.  I've tested them on planes and trains and they worked well. They do not rival high end products - but I don't expect that at this price point.All in all I think the Ear Fun Air Pro 3 is a bargain, a great all round product.

productTitle: Air Pro 3 Noise Cancelling Wireless Earbuds, Qualcomm® aptX™ Adaptive Sound, 6 Mics CVC 8.0 ENC, Bluetooth 5.3 Earbuds, Multipoint Connection, 45H Playtime, App Customize EQ, Oat White
reviewTitle: Great Ear Buds that actually stay in your ears.
reviewText: I have always had problems keeping ear buds in place in my right ear. I’ve tried loads, including big A to no avail. I bought these to pair with my Apple watch. I have had them a week now, used them every day on an 8k run outside and they have been great. Pairing is a doddle as is setting up for individuality using the app on your phone. Once set up, pairing to the watch was a breeze. The fit is perfect, with a range of cups to choose from. Be aware to follow the instructions for inserting in the ear because the process is not the same as other brands. The controls are intuitive, based on a series of taps for different functions. Everything works well and is easy to remember. Sound cancelling and ambient sound features are impressive as is the sound quality. The sound equaliser in the app allows infinite flexibility to personalise your own settings. The charging case is a gem and is recharged via USB C connector or on a magsafe charger.I’m really chuffed to have finally found a set of earbuds that stay in my ears and function brilliantly.

productTitle: Air Pro 3 Noise Cancelling Wireless Earbuds, Qualcomm® aptX™ Adaptive Sound, 6 Mics CVC 8.0 ENC, Bluetooth 5.3 Earbuds, Multipoint Connection, 45H Playtime, App Customize EQ, Oat White
reviewTitle: Great noise cancellation but audio is ok
reviewText: This is personally my second most liked pair of headphones. Deaccent hardware cursed with bad software.To start Bluetooth connection has been great and connects consistently and quickly. The connection stability has also been great as compared to other headphones its had very few dropped connections or patchy audio, even at a considerable distances.Noise cancelation, these are personally the best noise cancelling headphones as someone who commutes by bus which is very noise these have provided the best performance, even beating out more expensive options such as the soundcore Liberty 4 NC by a margin.The mic has been good, however robotic. What I mean by this calls sound fine but to people on the other side, voice sounds clear and isolated but robotic. There is probably some processing going but over all its not a bad thing as audio is clear despite sounding off.Sound, Unfortunately this is where it begins to falls short. The audio sounds ok, but in my opinion the bass is uncontrolled in the sense its a bit over the top. While rest sounds ok, with deaccent-ish clarity. With anc on vs off the audio does sound very different. The audio out of the box in my option is leaning on the inadequate side compared to what it is really capable of. A few firmware updates and messing in the eq it sounds good. Over all would recommend if your willing to spend a good few mins adjusting things.App. This is where it begins to fall flat. The app was buggy and the connection to the app was not always there which made it a headache to use when trying to use the app. The app if anything was the worst bit about this headphone, Personally I would recommend just to get there to update and adjust eq and change what the buttons do (like anc modes) so you don't need to return back to the software.The Multi-point over all for me was the deal-breaker for me unfortunately. As I work mainly in IT I use a lot of devices. The problem I had with the feature was not stability but choosing which device is connected and this is in part thanks to the app again. So for regular people who will only use 2 devices at most its fine.Comfort, The headphones for me where ok but long periods of time was not very comfortable and the ear tips them selves not a fan but they are fine. The overall shape of the ear bud is probably more suited to larger ears and the stem design, for my ear, at least did not sit well as I thought. Again theses are personal preferences as the person I gifted these on to said the fit was fine. But compared to his ears mine are more flat and soft, this MAY have played a role.Over all this is a ok product as the product was fine, but with app related compilations and the over all lack of clarity in the audio was the deciding factor for me to move to the soundcore 4 nc which is as if the time of writing £20 more. So if your looking for headphones on a tight £50 budget then yes. How ever if your able to spend the additional the amount of great options increase. On a final note the battery life was not the best when anc is on, this is expected but to the extent this happened not good,Note: Firmware update sin my experience have made significant differences so updating is a must and it is possible the issues addressed could be fixed in the future.

productTitle: Air Pro 3 Noise Cancelling Wireless Earbuds, Qualcomm® aptX™ Adaptive Sound, 6 Mics CVC 8.0 ENC, Bluetooth 5.3 Earbuds, Multipoint Connection, 45H Playtime, App Customize EQ, Oat White

reviewText: These are my second pair of Earfun headphones coming previously from the Earfun Free Pros which lasted me a couple of years so decided to stay with the same brand. productTitle: Air Pro 3 Noise Cancelling Wireless Earbuds, Qualcomm® aptX™ Adaptive Sound, 6 Mics CVC 8.0 ENC, Bluetooth 5.3 Earbuds, Multipoint Connection, 45H Playtime, App Customize EQ, Oat White
reviewTitle: Good sound but quiet. Good anc until but only low end.
reviewText: After 3 weeks of having these it's time to review, however for 2 reasona I will be returning.The good:-SOUND QUALITY-CHARGING CASE (sturdy, black so can't see scratches that much)-BATTERY LIFE (charging every few days, using 2-3 hours daily)-CONTROLS (easy to feel indented touchpad; many control options to customise including switching between ANC, Ambient, Normal)-controls are customisable in the app.-GAMING MODE (latency is still there, but one of best I tested for that price - on par with Soundpeats H2)-CALL QUALITY (everyone praises how well they can hear me comparing to Soundpeats Q. And I often were talking in really noisy environment.-ANC noise cancelling (good at mid to low frequencies. I've used it at home with noisy extractor hood in kitchen, works brilliantly.Also had them on the airplane and they block a lot of low hum rumble noise. Although I needed full volume (they're not that loud) the ANC was brilliant to have on the plane and I could hear movie dialogs good enough.-AMBIENT SOUND/TRANSPARENCY MODE (this one is brilliant and really helps when I'm talking to someone, does what it says on the tin and it's easy to activate!THE BAD:-VOLUME (they're really quiet if you're going bit deaf. My hearing is still quite good and I wish they were a tad louder. If not for ANC they wouldn't be good for listening to podcasts or movies in noisy environment.-ANC CRACKLES AT HIGH FREQUENCY NOISE (this is the worst and weakest point of these. When I use my tape measure and it "snaps" it causes really unpleasant crackle (unless normal mode on).Also happens when grinder is on. It's not a totally deafening sound, but I think it rather has got something to do with average quality microphones that just don't take high pitched noise. It's better to turn ANC off then as it really misses it's purpose.The above is the reason I'm returning these, where I work this issue makes them unbearable to wear.I'm giving these 4 stars, only 1 star taken for low volume and ANC crackling and high pitched noise.

productTitle: Air Pro 3 Noise Cancelling Wireless Earbuds, Qualcomm® aptX™ Adaptive Sound, 6 Mics CVC 8.0 ENC, Bluetooth 5.3 Earbuds, Multipoint Connection, 45H Playtime, App Customize EQ, Oat White
reviewText: ...but VERY IMPRESSED so far!

productTitle: Air Pro 3 Noise Cancelling Wireless Earbuds, Qualcomm® aptX™ Adaptive Sound, 6 Mics CVC 8.0 ENC, Bluetooth 5.3 Earbuds, Multipoint Connection, 45H Playtime, App Customize EQ, Oat White
reviewTitle: EarFun Air Pro 3 Wireless Earbuds: Value-Packed with Some Drawbacks
reviewText: The EarFun Air Pro 3 Wireless Earbuds offer an impressive array of features and sound quality at an affordable price point, making them an attractive option for budget-conscious consumers. However, while they excel in many areas, there are some drawbacks to consider.On the positive side, these earbuds deliver exceptional sound quality thanks to their Hybrid Active Noise Cancelling technology and 11mm wool drivers. Whether you're listening to music, podcasts, or taking calls, you can expect rich, immersive sound with deep bass and crisp highs. Additionally, the Bluetooth 5.3 connectivity ensures a stable and seamless wireless connection, allowing you to enjoy your audio content without interruption.Despite these strengths, one area where the EarFun Air Pro 3 earbuds fall short is in their microphone performance, particularly in open environments. While the 6 built-in microphones and CVC8.0 noise reduction technology excel in quiet settings, they struggle to deliver clear call quality in noisy environments. This can be particularly frustrating for users who rely on their earbuds for phone calls while on the go or in crowded spaces.However, it's essential to consider that these limitations are somewhat expected given the earbuds' affordable price point. For users primarily interested in high-quality audio playback and ANC performance, the EarFun Air Pro 3 earbuds offer excellent value for money.In conclusion, while the EarFun Air Pro 3 Wireless Earbuds have some drawbacks, such as subpar microphone performance in open environments, they still offer a compelling package of features and sound quality at an affordable price. If you can overlook this minor flaw, these earbuds provide a solid option for budget-conscious consumers looking for a reliable audio solution.

productTitle: Air Pro 3 Noise Cancelling Wireless Earbuds, Qualcomm® aptX™ Adaptive Sound, 6 Mics CVC 8.0 ENC, Bluetooth 5.3 Earbuds, Multipoint Connection, 45H Playtime, App Customize EQ, Oat White
reviewText: Just wow is all I can say. I took 2 hours overall when I finally made the decision to buy this product.I had been looking at Denons technics and sonys as they are all well known brands, didn't like the look of them as they seemed really big and clunky, even so much as the samsung earpods.The advert for these kept popping up throughout my search, and each time I looked at the advert, as something about them was clearly drawing me to it.Everything in the advert was positive, even boasting about the fact that it had won a what hifi award, and a gold award in something else, so I thought they have to be worth a punt, given they were a decent price Compared to others.Well, if like me, you are browsing these earpods deliberating wether to buy them, or for example some Denons, and now reading my review, don't waste your money on ANY other earpods, these are exactly what you need, and the sound is really really impressive truly unreal, and very immersive.Being the type of person I am, I tried out lots of different genres, from dubstep to classical, each of which truly put them to the test. The sound is so crisp and clear, and the bass is clear, not like a fart in a baked bean tin.I wouldn't say I am an audiophile, but I do appreciate good sound music playing devices, that can carry a bit of bass, just to make it sound better, and I wasn't disappointed with these bad boys.Easy to pair to your phone, just open the case and then find them on Bluetooth. Noise cancelling feature seems OK, but not had a chance to properly test that yet, or clarity of calls yet, so unable to comment on those functions. As my main use will be for listening to music I am very happy with my purchase and you will be too if you buy them.

reviewTitle: Great Value - Actual fits my ears
reviewText: Brought these when they were on sale for £49.99, and so far they have been excellent. I have always had issues with fit and comfort when it comes to earbuds/earphones, everything from the super cheap wired earphones, to the classic ipod/iphone earphones to the samsung galaxy buds+, I could never wear anything for longer than a few minutes without them falling out or just hurting my ears. I've had no such issues with these, I've been able to wear them fine while walking, washing the car, mowing the lawn, for hours at a time and not had any issues at all.In terms of sound they are alright, I have managed to get them to sound closer to my liking with the EQ, without any changes to the EQ they are too bass and mid heavy, and not enough highs.Noise cancelling is also acceptable for the price, but isn't as good as my sony wh-1000xm3, but I would expect it to be as they are 4-6 times the price when they were new.

reviewTitle: Excellent Customer Service & Resolved Earbud Issue!
reviewText: ITEM BOUGHT RECEIVED: 12/08/2023Quality of it in terms of sound and features are amazing. The only problem with these earbuds which seems to be a common problem is the right ear bud. It stops working after 6 months (~28/02/2024).UPDATE 12/03/2024:I’m back with an update on my earbuds situation. After my initial review, customer service stepped up and sorted everything out. They replaced my headset with a brand new one, and I’ve got to say, the sound and features are still top-notch. The previous issue with the right earbud cutting out seems to be resolved with this replacement. Props to the support team for handling my warranty claim without a hitch.

productTitle: Air Pro 3 Noise Cancelling Wireless Earbuds, Qualcomm® aptX™ Adaptive Sound, 6 Mics CVC 8.0 ENC, Bluetooth 5.3 Earbuds, Multipoint Connection, 45H Playtime, App Customize EQ, Oat White
reviewText: I bought these after returning some Soundcore Liberty 4 NC earbuds that were great generally except they irritated my ears. These have a different design that I hoped meant they would not irritate my ears.I must admit I was sceptical about earbuds made by a company called 'EarFun' but they have impressed me hugely and don't irritate my ears like the Liberty 4's.What has impressed me:1. Sound - I like a 'deep V' shaped music response - strong bass response, a dip in the mid frequencies and then a strong treble response - these have this naturally as well as onboard 10-band EQ (most earbuds only have 5-band EQ). This allows me to get the strong bass thump I want.2. Noise cancelling - like the Liberty 4's, they have good noise cancelling for the price point, doesn't get rid of everything but gets rid of a lot. I've not tried them in a noisy environment properly yet.3. Multi-point - I can connect these to my laptop and my phone at the same time and they switch automatically depending upon what's playing audio. Useful for switching between watching TV on my laptop and listening to music on my phone. The app also allows me to explicitly disconnect from my other device easily.4. Comfort - no issues with irritation, they're very light and very comfortable. I can easily fall asleep with them in (useful for naps just using the noise cancelling to quieten the natural noises in my house).5. Controls - they are fully configurable with single-, double- and triple- taps plus hold configurable on both sides.Not really tested battery life yet or how well they work in an electrically noisy environment either but so far no issues on either front.I got these when they were on offer at 50 quid and at that price they are an absolute bargain!Highly recommended.

productTitle: Air Pro 3 Noise Cancelling Wireless Earbuds, Qualcomm® aptX™ Adaptive Sound, 6 Mics CVC 8.0 ENC, Bluetooth 5.3 Earbuds, Multipoint Connection, 45H Playtime, App Customize EQ, Oat White
reviewTitle: If you like clear loud sound these are your buds!
reviewText: I was a bit skeptic to buy these as I’ve had Beats by Dre and AirPods etc and was disappointed with both.So I bought these because of the good reviews on Google as well as here plus they were reduced and I took the chance- they are the clearest and loudest earbuds I’ve ever had!EarFun do have an app you can install which I did and was pleasantly surprised that you can change or customise your sound experience, ie more bass or more treble etcI customised them to be the loudest and sharpest and it was well over my iPhone 15 decibels!They’re very easy to use and you can also customise the touch controls on the earbuds to either switch them around or just disable them plus they’re very comfortable for small awkward ears. :)Connectivity is fantastic I could have them blasting whilst my phone is away at the other side of the house, the charging is great also and they last for hours, I’ve fell asleep with them in and woke up next morning and they still had charge.Only thing I can think of is if you’re someone who LOVES a very heavy heavy bass then I don’t think they could give you it but I highly recommend these and I’m so glad I bought them!

reviewText: The product was good to begunah nd my daughter loved it.  She was wanted these for her vacation trip and now they don't work. They were extremely helpful with the support and provided me with instructions to try and resolve the existing issue.  unfortunately the product still had the issue.  Hence Earfun immediately sent out a new device which has been working without any issue.  Earfun has been extremely helpful in resolving the issue and hence have updated the scores. Hope

reviewText: After doing a bit of research and thinking these earphones were the best value for money...Unfortunately, I was let down, they arrived promptly and as they were a present for my daughter, I was looking forward to giving them to her.She was really pleased when she got them and really liked the look of them and continued to open them so she could connect to her phone.They connected very easily and quickly but the left earphone did not work!We tried with my phone, but the same thing happened :( We thought it might be a question that they had not been charged 100% so we charged them overnight hoping that this might be the solution...That did not work so we had no choice but to return it.We gave it 2 ⭐ because of the design, the way it fitted in our ears and although only sound coming out of 1 of the earfones it was good sound quality.UPDATEI was approached by EarFun and was kindly offered a replacement which I accepted and they work very well, as I said they are good value for money.It was kind of the company to reach out and rectify this issue for this reason I have adjusted my rating.Thank you guys.

reviewText: These are my first earbuds so I have nothing to compare them too as far as audio quality goes.The people at earfun are good. Not helpful as such. My first buds were faulty and I messaged them for advice. Instead they offered me 50% off. Great but it didn't fix the outfit issue so I reset the buds. The fault still happened so I messaged for advice again but instead they said 'we'll send you a new pair instead of the 50% back'. Great. productTitle: EarFun Air Pro 3 Noise Cancelling Wireless Earbuds, Qualcomm® aptX™ Adaptive Sound, 6 Mics CVC 8.0 ENC, Bluetooth 5.3 Earbuds, Multipoint Connection, 45H Playtime, App Customize EQ, Blue
reviewText: I have had these earphones for about 3 months.I have had galaxy buds+ and galaxy buds pro 2 in the past before this and ended up losing one of my buds 2 pro's, decided to try and find something relatively cheap to replace it but still wanted features like dual connectivity and ANC.These earbuds have not let me down, the case is well made, the earbuds are well made, the sound quality is on par with the galaxy buds of not a little bit better making them great value for money.They're easy to use, the app has a lot of features such as your dual connectivity, charging levels and ANC.I have found that if I charge them once at the beginning of the week the battery life easily lasts all week with a few hours of listening each day.Honestly if you're considering these and are worried they'll be a downgrade in audio quality from your now aging galaxy buds or airpods then I can assure you that you will not be let down.Worth every penny.

productTitle: EarFun Air Pro 3 Noise Cancelling Wireless Earbuds, Qualcomm® aptX™ Adaptive Sound, 6 Mics CVC 8.0 ENC, Bluetooth 5.3 Earbuds, Multipoint Connection, 45H Playtime, App Customize EQ, Blue
reviewTitle: These are good little ear buds for the price!
reviewText: I was sceptical about the performance of these Earfun Air Pods Pro 3’s. I’m an Apple user and use mainly the AirPod pros and Powerbeats pro (for the gym).I wanted these mostly for the ANC function.My first impressions of these were overall very good.The ambient setting is not great - it causes a lot of hiss and does not give a very realistic impression of outside sounds. Needs improvement.The ANC really surprised me though how good it is. Plus the overall sound quality, once tweaked using the EarFun app is very good.So many quality features including wireless charging case, for such a great price, without really having to compromise on listening quality and performance.The battery life with ANC on, is in my real life use around 6 hours - which is acceptable. It says a lot when many reviews show these standing up against the Apple AirPod pro, that are in a completely different price bracket.The EarFun app could do with a revamp but that’s minor really. I prefer over ear buds for working out, but others may find that these work 0k - they seem a very good tight fit when you get the right size tips. They are also very comfortable for me.It would also be nice to have wear detection so that they turn off when you take them out.I guess some will be put off by the name (it’s not the greatest name is it?) - written on the buds showing the world that you like Earfun!If you can live with not having Apple kudos, these are pretty good earbuds, especially for the price.

productTitle: EarFun Air Pro 3 Noise Cancelling Wireless Earbuds, Qualcomm® aptX™ Adaptive Sound, 6 Mics CVC 8.0 ENC, Bluetooth 5.3 Earbuds, Multipoint Connection, 45H Playtime, App Customize EQ, Blue
reviewText: I have been searching for decently priced, good quality, noise cancelling earbuds since like, forever and, at last,  I think I have found them....Hallelujah!! I have resisted paying through the nose for the high end brands because, in my heart, I knew there had to be a company out there that was beavering away trying  to match the big guys for a fraction of the cost and, in my opinon, the Earfun Air Pro 3's come pretty damn close!The minute I put them in my ears and the music started I actually said, " Wow!" Clear sound and vocals and the base is brilliant. They are so comfortable, just make sure you fit the correct sized earplugs to make a good seal. Noise cancelling is good, I feel totally immersed in the sound..Honestly, they are worth every penny - I'm just amazed  for the amount I paid the quality of them.Do your ears a favour and go get them - You won't be sorry!

productTitle: EarFun Air Pro 3 Noise Cancelling Wireless Earbuds, Qualcomm® aptX™ Adaptive Sound, 6 Mics CVC 8.0 ENC, Bluetooth 5.3 Earbuds, Multipoint Connection, 45H Playtime, App Customize EQ, Blue
reviewText: I love bluetooth earbuds. They are so convenient, amazing tech and can have great sound in such a delicate package.I have some high end models from Sony and Bose, which are very good indeed.When I read the reviews of this Ear Fun model I was intrigued and decided to take the plunge and see for myself what these are like, for approx 20% of the price of my last Bose's.First impressions were good, a neat carry case, the alternative bud tips you would expect and a USB Connector.  You need to download the phone app if you want to change any control functions or equaliser settings, then ready to go. Straightforward.After opening this review by saying that I love bluetooth earbuds, I do accept that the sound quality will be compromised when compared to a proper Hi Fi or over ear earphones.  My Bose QC's are probably the best sound from a compact earbud - I've never experienced better, but these EarFuns, for a fraction of the price, are amazing.  Sound is clear and crisp.  Most of the time we wear earbuds we are on the move and there is extraneous sounds, movement , etc, and the EarFuns handled this well.Now, for the Noise Cancellation.  it is difficult to beat the Bose's for that too, sorry Sony.  Now, for 20% of the price , the EarFuns provide perfectly acceptable ANC, most annoying sounds are masked to an acceptable level.  I've tested them on planes and trains and they worked well. They do not rival high end products - but I don't expect that at this price point.All in all I think the Ear Fun Air Pro 3 is a bargain, a great all round product.

productTitle: EarFun Air Pro 3 Noise Cancelling Wireless Earbuds, Qualcomm® aptX™ Adaptive Sound, 6 Mics CVC 8.0 ENC, Bluetooth 5.3 Earbuds, Multipoint Connection, 45H Playtime, App Customize EQ, Blue
reviewTitle: Great Ear Buds that actually stay in your ears.
reviewText: I have always had problems keeping ear buds in place in my right ear. I’ve tried loads, including big A to no avail. I bought these to pair with my Apple watch. I have had them a week now, used them every day on an 8k run outside and they have been great. Pairing is a doddle as is setting up for individuality using the app on your phone. Once set up, pairing to the watch was a breeze. The fit is perfect, with a range of cups to choose from. Be aware to follow the instructions for inserting in the ear because the process is not the same as other brands. The controls are intuitive, based on a series of taps for different functions. Everything works well and is easy to remember. Sound cancelling and ambient sound features are impressive as is the sound quality. The sound equaliser in the app allows infinite flexibility to personalise your own settings. The charging case is a gem and is recharged via USB C connector or on a magsafe charger.I’m really chuffed to have finally found a set of earbuds that stay in my ears and function brilliantly.

productTitle: EarFun Air Pro 3 Noise Cancelling Wireless Earbuds, Qualcomm® aptX™ Adaptive Sound, 6 Mics CVC 8.0 ENC, Bluetooth 5.3 Earbuds, Multipoint Connection, 45H Playtime, App Customize EQ, Blue
reviewTitle: Great noise cancellation but audio is ok
reviewText: This is personally my second most liked pair of headphones. Deaccent hardware cursed with bad software.To start Bluetooth connection has been great and connects consistently and quickly. The connection stability has also been great as compared to other headphones its had very few dropped connections or patchy audio, even at a considerable distances.Noise cancelation, these are personally the best noise cancelling headphones as someone who commutes by bus which is very noise these have provided the best performance, even beating out more expensive options such as the soundcore Liberty 4 NC by a margin.The mic has been good, however robotic. What I mean by this calls sound fine but to people on the other side, voice sounds clear and isolated but robotic. There is probably some processing going but over all its not a bad thing as audio is clear despite sounding off.Sound, Unfortunately this is where it begins to falls short. The audio sounds ok, but in my opinion the bass is uncontrolled in the sense its a bit over the top. While rest sounds ok, with deaccent-ish clarity. With anc on vs off the audio does sound very different. The audio out of the box in my option is leaning on the inadequate side compared to what it is really capable of. A few firmware updates and messing in the eq it sounds good. Over all would recommend if your willing to spend a good few mins adjusting things.App. This is where it begins to fall flat. The app was buggy and the connection to the app was not always there which made it a headache to use when trying to use the app. The app if anything was the worst bit about this headphone, Personally I would recommend just to get there to update and adjust eq and change what the buttons do (like anc modes) so you don't need to return back to the software.The Multi-point over all for me was the deal-breaker for me unfortunately. As I work mainly in IT I use a lot of devices. The problem I had with the feature was not stability but choosing which device is connected and this is in part thanks to the app again. So for regular people who will only use 2 devices at most its fine.Comfort, The headphones for me where ok but long periods of time was not very comfortable and the ear tips them selves not a fan but they are fine. The overall shape of the ear bud is probably more suited to larger ears and the stem design, for my ear, at least did not sit well as I thought. Again theses are personal preferences as the person I gifted these on to said the fit was fine. But compared to his ears mine are more flat and soft, this MAY have played a role.Over all this is a ok product as the product was fine, but with app related compilations and the over all lack of clarity in the audio was the deciding factor for me to move to the soundcore 4 nc which is as if the time of writing £20 more. So if your looking for headphones on a tight £50 budget then yes. How ever if your able to spend the additional the amount of great options increase. On a final note the battery life was not the best when anc is on, this is expected but to the extent this happened not good,Note: Firmware update sin my experience have made significant differences so updating is a must and it is possible the issues addressed could be fixed in the future.

productTitle: EarFun Air Pro 3 Noise Cancelling Wireless Earbuds, Qualcomm® aptX™ Adaptive Sound, 6 Mics CVC 8.0 ENC, Bluetooth 5.3 Earbuds, Multipoint Connection, 45H Playtime, App Customize EQ, Blue
reviewTitle: Good sound but quiet. Good anc until but only low end.
reviewText: After 3 weeks of having these it's time to review, however for 2 reasona I will be returning.The good:-SOUND QUALITY-CHARGING CASE (sturdy, black so can't see scratches that much)-BATTERY LIFE (charging every few days, using 2-3 hours daily)-CONTROLS (easy to feel indented touchpad; many control options to customise including switching between ANC, Ambient, Normal)-controls are customisable in the app.-GAMING MODE (latency is still there, but one of best I tested for that price - on par with Soundpeats H2)-CALL QUALITY (everyone praises how well they can hear me comparing to Soundpeats Q. And I often were talking in really noisy environment.-ANC noise cancelling (good at mid to low frequencies. I've used it at home with noisy extractor hood in kitchen, works brilliantly.Also had them on the airplane and they block a lot of low hum rumble noise. Although I needed full volume (they're not that loud) the ANC was brilliant to have on the plane and I could hear movie dialogs good enough.-AMBIENT SOUND/TRANSPARENCY MODE (this one is brilliant and really helps when I'm talking to someone, does what it says on the tin and it's easy to activate!THE BAD:-VOLUME (they're really quiet if you're going bit deaf. My hearing is still quite good and I wish they were a tad louder. If not for ANC they wouldn't be good for listening to podcasts or movies in noisy environment.-ANC CRACKLES AT HIGH FREQUENCY NOISE (this is the worst and weakest point of these. When I use my tape measure and it "snaps" it causes really unpleasant crackle (unless normal mode on).Also happens when grinder is on. It's not a totally deafening sound, but I think it rather has got something to do with average quality microphones that just don't take high pitched noise. It's better to turn ANC off then as it really misses it's purpose.The above is the reason I'm returning these, where I work this issue makes them unbearable to wear.I'm giving these 4 stars, only 1 star taken for low volume and ANC crackling and high pitched noise.

productTitle: EarFun Air Pro 3 Noise Cancelling Wireless Earbuds, Qualcomm® aptX™ Adaptive Sound, 6 Mics CVC 8.0 ENC, Bluetooth 5.3 Earbuds, Multipoint Connection, 45H Playtime, App Customize EQ, Blue

productTitle: EarFun Air Pro 3 Noise Cancelling Wireless Earbuds, Qualcomm® aptX™ Adaptive Sound, 6 Mics CVC 8.0 ENC, Bluetooth 5.3 Earbuds, Multipoint Connection, 45H Playtime, App Customize EQ, Blue
reviewTitle: EarFun Air Pro 3 Wireless Earbuds: Value-Packed with Some Drawbacks
reviewText: The EarFun Air Pro 3 Wireless Earbuds offer an impressive array of features and sound quality at an affordable price point, making them an attractive option for budget-conscious consumers. However, while they excel in many areas, there are some drawbacks to consider.On the positive side, these earbuds deliver exceptional sound quality thanks to their Hybrid Active Noise Cancelling technology and 11mm wool drivers. Whether you're listening to music, podcasts, or taking calls, you can expect rich, immersive sound with deep bass and crisp highs. Additionally, the Bluetooth 5.3 connectivity ensures a stable and seamless wireless connection, allowing you to enjoy your audio content without interruption.Despite these strengths, one area where the EarFun Air Pro 3 earbuds fall short is in their microphone performance, particularly in open environments. While the 6 built-in microphones and CVC8.0 noise reduction technology excel in quiet settings, they struggle to deliver clear call quality in noisy environments. This can be particularly frustrating for users who rely on their earbuds for phone calls while on the go or in crowded spaces.However, it's essential to consider that these limitations are somewhat expected given the earbuds' affordable price point. For users primarily interested in high-quality audio playback and ANC performance, the EarFun Air Pro 3 earbuds offer excellent value for money.In conclusion, while the EarFun Air Pro 3 Wireless Earbuds have some drawbacks, such as subpar microphone performance in open environments, they still offer a compelling package of features and sound quality at an affordable price. If you can overlook this minor flaw, these earbuds provide a solid option for budget-conscious consumers looking for a reliable audio solution.

productTitle: EarFun Air Pro 3 Noise Cancelling Wireless Earbuds, Qualcomm® aptX™ Adaptive Sound, 6 Mics CVC 8.0 ENC, Bluetooth 5.3 Earbuds, Multipoint Connection, 45H Playtime, App Customize EQ, Blue
reviewText: Just wow is all I can say. I took 2 hours overall when I finally made the decision to buy this product.I had been looking at Denons technics and sonys as they are all well known brands, didn't like the look of them as they seemed really big and clunky, even so much as the samsung earpods.The advert for these kept popping up throughout my search, and each time I looked at the advert, as something about them was clearly drawing me to it.Everything in the advert was positive, even boasting about the fact that it had won a what hifi award, and a gold award in something else, so I thought they have to be worth a punt, given they were a decent price Compared to others.Well, if like me, you are browsing these earpods deliberating wether to buy them, or for example some Denons, and now reading my review, don't waste your money on ANY other earpods, these are exactly what you need, and the sound is really really impressive truly unreal, and very immersive.Being the type of person I am, I tried out lots of different genres, from dubstep to classical, each of which truly put them to the test. The sound is so crisp and clear, and the bass is clear, not like a fart in a baked bean tin.I wouldn't say I am an audiophile, but I do appreciate good sound music playing devices, that can carry a bit of bass, just to make it sound better, and I wasn't disappointed with these bad boys.Easy to pair to your phone, just open the case and then find them on Bluetooth. Noise cancelling feature seems OK, but not had a chance to properly test that yet, or clarity of calls yet, so unable to comment on those functions. As my main use will be for listening to music I am very happy with my purchase and you will be too if you buy them.

reviewTitle: Great Value - Actual fits my ears
reviewText: Brought these when they were on sale for £49.99, and so far they have been excellent. I have always had issues with fit and comfort when it comes to earbuds/earphones, everything from the super cheap wired earphones, to the classic ipod/iphone earphones to the samsung galaxy buds+, I could never wear anything for longer than a few minutes without them falling out or just hurting my ears. I've had no such issues with these, I've been able to wear them fine while walking, washing the car, mowing the lawn, for hours at a time and not had any issues at all.In terms of sound they are alright, I have managed to get them to sound closer to my liking with the EQ, without any changes to the EQ they are too bass and mid heavy, and not enough highs.Noise cancelling is also acceptable for the price, but isn't as good as my sony wh-1000xm3, but I would expect it to be as they are 4-6 times the price when they were new.

reviewTitle: Excellent Customer Service & Resolved Earbud Issue!
reviewText: ITEM BOUGHT RECEIVED: 12/08/2023Quality of it in terms of sound and features are amazing. The only problem with these earbuds which seems to be a common problem is the right ear bud. It stops working after 6 months (~28/02/2024).UPDATE 12/03/2024:I’m back with an update on my earbuds situation. After my initial review, customer service stepped up and sorted everything out. They replaced my headset with a brand new one, and I’ve got to say, the sound and features are still top-notch. The previous issue with the right earbud cutting out seems to be resolved with this replacement. Props to the support team for handling my warranty claim without a hitch.

productTitle: EarFun Air Pro 3 Noise Cancelling Wireless Earbuds, Qualcomm® aptX™ Adaptive Sound, 6 Mics CVC 8.0 ENC, Bluetooth 5.3 Earbuds, Multipoint Connection, 45H Playtime, App Customize EQ, Blue
reviewText: I bought these after returning some Soundcore Liberty 4 NC earbuds that were great generally except they irritated my ears. These have a different design that I hoped meant they would not irritate my ears.I must admit I was sceptical about earbuds made by a company called 'EarFun' but they have impressed me hugely and don't irritate my ears like the Liberty 4's.What has impressed me:1. Sound - I like a 'deep V' shaped music response - strong bass response, a dip in the mid frequencies and then a strong treble response - these have this naturally as well as onboard 10-band EQ (most earbuds only have 5-band EQ). This allows me to get the strong bass thump I want.2. Noise cancelling - like the Liberty 4's, they have good noise cancelling for the price point, doesn't get rid of everything but gets rid of a lot. I've not tried them in a noisy environment properly yet.3. Multi-point - I can connect these to my laptop and my phone at the same time and they switch automatically depending upon what's playing audio. Useful for switching between watching TV on my laptop and listening to music on my phone. The app also allows me to explicitly disconnect from my other device easily.4. Comfort - no issues with irritation, they're very light and very comfortable. I can easily fall asleep with them in (useful for naps just using the noise cancelling to quieten the natural noises in my house).5. Controls - they are fully configurable with single-, double- and triple- taps plus hold configurable on both sides.Not really tested battery life yet or how well they work in an electrically noisy environment either but so far no issues on either front.I got these when they were on offer at 50 quid and at that price they are an absolute bargain!Highly recommended.

productTitle: EarFun Air Pro 3 Noise Cancelling Wireless Earbuds, Qualcomm® aptX™ Adaptive Sound, 6 Mics CVC 8.0 ENC, Bluetooth 5.3 Earbuds, Multipoint Connection, 45H Playtime, App Customize EQ, Blue
reviewTitle: If you like clear loud sound these are your buds!
reviewText: I was a bit skeptic to buy these as I’ve had Beats by Dre and AirPods etc and was disappointed with both.So I bought these because of the good reviews on Google as well as here plus they were reduced and I took the chance- they are the clearest and loudest earbuds I’ve ever had!EarFun do have an app you can install which I did and was pleasantly surprised that you can change or customise your sound experience, ie more bass or more treble etcI customised them to be the loudest and sharpest and it was well over my iPhone 15 decibels!They’re very easy to use and you can also customise the touch controls on the earbuds to either switch them around or just disable them plus they’re very comfortable for small awkward ears. :)Connectivity is fantastic I could have them blasting whilst my phone is away at the other side of the house, the charging is great also and they last for hours, I’ve fell asleep with them in and woke up next morning and they still had charge.Only thing I can think of is if you’re someone who LOVES a very heavy heavy bass then I don’t think they could give you it but I highly recommend these and I’m so glad I bought them!

reviewText: Considering these are 1/5 the price of AirPods I wasn’t expecting equal performance but I wasn’t disappointed. The noise cancellation is very good. My other half bought earbuds of similar value that claim to be noise cancelling but don’t at all. The sound quality is good. If you want good performance but don’t want to spend ridiculous amounts on something that you’d be so worried about losing you wouldn’t take them out of the house then these are a great option and certainly the best performance in this price band. Would buy again when I inevitably lose these!Update: I’ve finally had to charge them, nearly a month after purchase! I’m so impressed with them. If I had to find fault I’d like there to be more than 3 charge indicator lights so you have a better idea of when it’ll run out of battery but that’s just nitpicking.
