reviewText: I only had one complaint when I got these but they amazingly updated it so it is now perfect. The app that you download where you can control the headphones gives you the option to decide how many taps and on what ear they should be assigned. For eg one or two taps for pause or Siri etc. you can also change the EQ. The headphones are comfortable for in-ears and they are sturdy. I had a problem and the customer service was quick and very generous with 18 month warranty! It’s nice to support business who care. Very different from what u get with Apple with is expensive and you already know it’s a hassle if something is wrong.

productTitle: Free Pro 3 Noise Cancelling Earbuds, Snapdragon Sound™ with Qualcomm aptX™ Adaptive, 6 Mics ENC, Multipoint Connection, Bluetooth 5.3 Wireless Earbuds, Cozy Fit, Upgraded App Customization,33H
reviewText: In the box: 5 sets of ear pieces in rubber style, and 1 earpiece of memory foam + rubber style, which i was using while writing this review. After 2 days of use I am comparing them to previously used CX True Wireless and Outlier Air V3.Good sound, nice extended bass without tiring your ear, especially nice low end for heavy metal tracks, mildly boosted bass, which was good for my 1-2 hours street walking and listening, i enjoy using these earbuds more than using Sennheiser's CX True Wireless or Creative's Outlier Air v3.Fits snuggly in my ear, better than CX TW & OA V3, with memory foam earpieces i can shake my head more confidently and not keep losing them on the street pavement than when using rubber earpieces (with rubber pieces i lose 1 earbud of the 2 too often, quite a disturbing listening experience).Battery is ok, what expected for this price point, didn't have to charge the case for 2 days until it went down to 41% and earpieces 30% + 30%. With more features on in the app, i suspected battery went down much quicker.If the audio recordings are of older release date (like 1991's heavy metal) APTX Adaptive mode will result into an interrupted sound, don't think this is because earbuds are failing just after 1 or 2 days and you want to return them and write a low score feedback, it's better to turn off APTX Adaptive on your smartphone, then during listening to older recordings it will not give interrupted glitchy soundI even tried to play some FPS game like CS2 on steam, it was obviously with some delay, i tried to turn on Game Mode on the smartphone in the EarFun app, apparently it had to reduce delay, it was acceptable experience, we won a competitive match, still had some kind of delay in transmission and i heard my teammates talking a tad later.ANC function is better when the music is playing, otherwise: when music stops playing it's just an ok ANC, you can still hear surroundings and keyboard taps.Got it at the time when it was 59.99, well spent, felt like i should have bought these first, instead of trying to find that one cheap budget set of eardbuds (like i did with CX TW and OA v3). Probably would rebuy Earfun Free Pro 3 or Earfun 4 in the future, and not the budget Creative or Sennheiser

productTitle: Free Pro 3 Noise Cancelling Earbuds, Snapdragon Sound™ with Qualcomm aptX™ Adaptive, 6 Mics ENC, Multipoint Connection, Bluetooth 5.3 Wireless Earbuds, Cozy Fit, Upgraded App Customization,33H
reviewTitle: Compact, good sounding earbuds that fit securely in the ear.
reviewText: I have a large selection of reasonably priced Bluetooth earbuds. I keep trying to find my ideal pair.  I think these are the closest so far. I'm no audiophile so take what I say with that in mind.I did have a problem using them with the PlayerPro audio player app I use, at first. It turned out to be an incompatibility between the EQ on the app and the EQ on the earbuds. There was a lot of distortion on some songs and lack of depth on others. Switching off the EQ on the PlayerPro app solved the problem. Even I'd you switch off the EQ on the earbuds the EQ on the PlayerPro app causes distortion, yet it works fine with all my other Bluetooth earbuds.I tried all the preset EQ settings on the Earfun app and Pop seems the best for most of the music I listen to ( mostly pop, EDM, easy listening).I was unimpressed with the noise cancelling when I tried it first with a fan running in the room I was in. I could still hear it quite clearly. On a train, the noise was only reduced slightly.  I WAS impressed when I tried it on a plane and the engine noise dropped significantly. I then tried it when on a cruise ship but it didn't seem to reduce the constant noise of the ships generator at all, all ANC settings sounded the same to me as did the other two modes ambient and normal. I'm guessing the ANC has been tuned for frequencies produced by plane engines.Assessing sound quality is personal. I like the sound they produce but it is still lacking. Songs that give me chills when I listen to them on my Bluetooth speaker don't do the same when I use the earbuds. As I said, I'm not an audiophile and I can't say what is missing but something is. They are certainly better in sound quality some of my other earbuds and they are a comfortable and secure fit.Maybe I need to change to over ear headphones to get the sound quality I want rather than keep using earbuds but they are convenient to carry and I these ones are certainly good value for all the built-in functionality.

productTitle: Free Pro 3 Noise Cancelling Earbuds, Snapdragon Sound™ with Qualcomm aptX™ Adaptive, 6 Mics ENC, Multipoint Connection, Bluetooth 5.3 Wireless Earbuds, Cozy Fit, Upgraded App Customization,33H
reviewText: Been using for just over a week now and I have to say these are pretty good for the price. I legitimately haven't charged the case once since I bought them and have gotten at least 25 hours so far no problem.Most importantly, NO HISSING!!! With other earbuds at this price range I'd get a constant "hissing" sound whenever the audio would be engaged and people would just blame it on me "having a high range of hearing" or something, but I'm happy to say that these have no hissing.The app is fine. It asks for an "account" when you open it but it's easy to skip. Touch controls again but I just disabled them since I don't really like them on any earbuds. The audio passthrough and active noise cancellation seem to be completely useless for me though, but it shouldn't be something at this price range anyway.The fit is fine. I had some issues initially but they seem to stay in fine now. They have a nice shape to them that fills the ear. My old earbuds (galaxy buds) really jutted out but these are very snug.I love the material on the case. The feel of it is just amazing.The only problems I've had with these is that sometimes one side would randomly stop playing audio for a bit, but it seems to happen really rarely, and the app can be a bit slow sometimes. The ear tips that come with it are also of a noticeably low quality, as well as the box being hideous for some reason lol.I was skeptical to buy a brand I didn't know at this price range but it's turned out to be great. I can't say much to their durability yet but I'll update my review if anything happens in the next few years.Edit: After using for over a month the only problem is that very rarely the connection drops. We're talking once or twice in a week, and it's usually only one side and it's easily fixed by just disabling and enabling Bluetooth on my phone. The app is okay; a bit slow but I basically never have to open it since all the settings are properly saved

productTitle: Free Pro 3 Noise Cancelling Earbuds, Snapdragon Sound™ with Qualcomm aptX™ Adaptive, 6 Mics ENC, Multipoint Connection, Bluetooth 5.3 Wireless Earbuds, Cozy Fit, Upgraded App Customization,33H
reviewTitle: Almost perfect, but one HUGE flaw!
reviewText: Bought these as I am a big fan of Ear Fun products. They are comfortable, terrific sound and battery life and they synch perfectly with the Ear Fun app. However, after a few hours wear I had a terrible allergic reaction to them, my ears were red and sore then itching followed by clear discharge from my ears. Its so incredibly annoying, I really don't know what is in them that causes this? So along with these the Samsung Pros, Bose Ultra and Soundcore Liberty 4 not the NC 4 for some reason are now no go's for me. I am sure that the majority of customers will not have these issues but for some reason they affect me. Luckily Amazon have graciously accepted the return of them.

productTitle: Free Pro 3 Noise Cancelling Earbuds, Snapdragon Sound™ with Qualcomm aptX™ Adaptive, 6 Mics ENC, Multipoint Connection, Bluetooth 5.3 Wireless Earbuds, Cozy Fit, Upgraded App Customization,33H

reviewText: Love these ear buds but they're very sensitive to touch if you need to adjust them and will skip a song or shut off.Haven't mastered what the touches do yet so still answer the phone on my phone.The band's also need to have the holes lined up perfectly for sound to come through. I love the sound and having noise cancelling and ambient sound too. There's also settings to change sound to your own preference. (I got lost in that bit so went with presets.Took a while to get them to feel comfortable but now they're good. Has different size silicone ear pieces so plenty to choose from.Battery life is max 3 hours without recharging.Haven't lost them outside yet,  so as long as they're fitted correctly I think they'll be fine.All in all I like them and would recommend as the sound is great.

reviewTitle: Very good sound quality with excellent bass. Fit a bit fiddly as first.
reviewText: Very good sound quality. The optimal fitting took quite some time, but it's supplied with several sizes of fittings, so you should find a good fit. The instruction booklet seems to show a completely impossible angle for fitting. The only secure fit is essentially with the logo horizontal, photo attached. Once that's sorted, I found the fit to be pretty good, with no falling out when walking . I'm not a jogger, but I think they'd stay in. The size is nice and small, with the ear bud not sticking out much at all. You can definitely have them in with your head against a pillow with no discomfort. A couple of photos attached, one of the EarFun and one of my old Echo buds: big difference. The battery life is good and the app lets you change tapping options for both left and right. The noise cancelling is reasonably OK, however, that is more than compensated for by the very good sound quality, so no complaints. I bought these a couple of weeks ago (Feb 2024) and so far I'm very happy with them.

productTitle: Free Pro 3 Noise Cancelling Earbuds, Snapdragon Sound™ with Qualcomm aptX™ Adaptive, 6 Mics ENC, Multipoint Connection, Bluetooth 5.3 Wireless Earbuds, Cozy Fit, Upgraded App Customization,33H
reviewTitle: Good sound but spoilt by weak noise cancelling and glitchy Bluetooth
reviewText: First the positives. They fit well and don't move or fall out when running. They sound good especially using one of the Oluv equaliser settings. But the noise cancelling isn't good at all. Compared to Bose earbuds they cancel no noise at all. I've used all the settings for noise cancelling and none really reduce external noise by much. The Bluetooth is a bit glitchy (a bit like Bose earbuds) and initial connection is a bit hit and miss. They sometimes connect but then sound a bit like a bad phone connection. This has always gone away after disconnecting and reconnecting though. I've gone though 4 sets of earbuds now and the only ones with noticeable noise cancelling are Bose.

productTitle: Free Pro 3 Noise Cancelling Earbuds, Snapdragon Sound™ with Qualcomm aptX™ Adaptive, 6 Mics ENC, Multipoint Connection, Bluetooth 5.3 Wireless Earbuds, Cozy Fit, Upgraded App Customization,33H
reviewText: I wasn't too sure what to expect. It's been a long time since I used earbuds as I prefer over ear headphones, but sometimes the headphones are too unwieldy, so I thought I'd give these a try.I chose these purely based on online reviews. I didn't want stem style buds as I don't like the look of them, I have fairly small ears and an even smaller budget. These seemed to be the ones most recommended in my price range along with Soundcore A40 and a couple of others. The aptx support swung it for me.Once I received them, I played around with different sized tips and wings to get the best fit. Then downloaded the app and played around with the equalizer - the Jazz preset was my preferred choice. I left the default controls alone.Pairing to 2 devices is very easy, and when you don't want to use them put them back in the charging case, close the lid and all devices will disconnect.I liked that the chosen EQ not only worked on my phone, but also when connected to my laptop (I thought it would just EQ the sound played by the phone, but it must download the EQ to the buds).  I could play around with the app on my phone whilst playing music via my laptop.Sound quality is very good once you've achieved a snug fit. Plenty loud enough with good clarity and good bass. Can't really detect any difference when using Aptx on my phone, but my hearing is definitely not what it used to be 30 years ago.Looking forward to getting a lot of use out of these.

reviewText: Having previously owned a Samsung S21 Ultra phone and the Buds Pro 2 I was very happy with the sound quality.However since changing to an Oppo Find X5 Pro the Samsung pro buds too sounded terrible.This was down to the Samsung codec not being available on my new phone.From the first listen of these new Earfun buds I was blown away.Before I purchased I went into my developer settings to see what codecs were available before I made the purchase.I found that not only did my phone have the relevant codecs it had the exact same one that this set of earbuds worked the best with.My own lit gripe is that the earphones don't turn off as soon as you take them from your ear.That is the only thing that bothers me.I am very impressed with the same quality and Bill quality of these earphones at this price point.If you have a compatible phone don't hesitate you won't regret it

productTitle: Free Pro 3 Noise Cancelling Earbuds, Snapdragon Sound™ with Qualcomm aptX™ Adaptive, 6 Mics ENC, Multipoint Connection, Bluetooth 5.3 Wireless Earbuds, Cozy Fit, Upgraded App Customization, 33H
reviewText: In the box: 5 sets of ear pieces in rubber style, and 1 earpiece of memory foam + rubber style, which i was using while writing this review. After 2 days of use I am comparing them to previously used CX True Wireless and Outlier Air V3.Good sound, nice extended bass without tiring your ear, especially nice low end for heavy metal tracks, mildly boosted bass, which was good for my 1-2 hours street walking and listening, i enjoy using these earbuds more than using Sennheiser's CX True Wireless or Creative's Outlier Air v3.Fits snuggly in my ear, better than CX TW & OA V3, with memory foam earpieces i can shake my head more confidently and not keep losing them on the street pavement than when using rubber earpieces (with rubber pieces i lose 1 earbud of the 2 too often, quite a disturbing listening experience).Battery is ok, what expected for this price point, didn't have to charge the case for 2 days until it went down to 41% and earpieces 30% + 30%. With more features on in the app, i suspected battery went down much quicker.If the audio recordings are of older release date (like 1991's heavy metal) APTX Adaptive mode will result into an interrupted sound, don't think this is because earbuds are failing just after 1 or 2 days and you want to return them and write a low score feedback, it's better to turn off APTX Adaptive on your smartphone, then during listening to older recordings it will not give interrupted glitchy soundI even tried to play some FPS game like CS2 on steam, it was obviously with some delay, i tried to turn on Game Mode on the smartphone in the EarFun app, apparently it had to reduce delay, it was acceptable experience, we won a competitive match, still had some kind of delay in transmission and i heard my teammates talking a tad later.ANC function is better when the music is playing, otherwise: when music stops playing it's just an ok ANC, you can still hear surroundings and keyboard taps.Got it at the time when it was 59.99, well spent, felt like i should have bought these first, instead of trying to find that one cheap budget set of eardbuds (like i did with CX TW and OA v3). Probably would rebuy Earfun Free Pro 3 or Earfun 4 in the future, and not the budget Creative or Sennheiser

productTitle: Free Pro 3 Noise Cancelling Earbuds, Snapdragon Sound™ with Qualcomm aptX™ Adaptive, 6 Mics ENC, Multipoint Connection, Bluetooth 5.3 Wireless Earbuds, Cozy Fit, Upgraded App Customization, 33H
reviewTitle: Compact, good sounding earbuds that fit securely in the ear.
reviewText: I have a large selection of reasonably priced Bluetooth earbuds. I keep trying to find my ideal pair.  I think these are the closest so far. I'm no audiophile so take what I say with that in mind.I did have a problem using them with the PlayerPro audio player app I use, at first. It turned out to be an incompatibility between the EQ on the app and the EQ on the earbuds. There was a lot of distortion on some songs and lack of depth on others. Switching off the EQ on the PlayerPro app solved the problem. Even I'd you switch off the EQ on the earbuds the EQ on the PlayerPro app causes distortion, yet it works fine with all my other Bluetooth earbuds.I tried all the preset EQ settings on the Earfun app and Pop seems the best for most of the music I listen to ( mostly pop, EDM, easy listening).I was unimpressed with the noise cancelling when I tried it first with a fan running in the room I was in. I could still hear it quite clearly. On a train, the noise was only reduced slightly.  I WAS impressed when I tried it on a plane and the engine noise dropped significantly. I then tried it when on a cruise ship but it didn't seem to reduce the constant noise of the ships generator at all, all ANC settings sounded the same to me as did the other two modes ambient and normal. I'm guessing the ANC has been tuned for frequencies produced by plane engines.Assessing sound quality is personal. I like the sound they produce but it is still lacking. Songs that give me chills when I listen to them on my Bluetooth speaker don't do the same when I use the earbuds. As I said, I'm not an audiophile and I can't say what is missing but something is. They are certainly better in sound quality some of my other earbuds and they are a comfortable and secure fit.Maybe I need to change to over ear headphones to get the sound quality I want rather than keep using earbuds but they are convenient to carry and I these ones are certainly good value for all the built-in functionality.

productTitle: Free Pro 3 Noise Cancelling Earbuds, Snapdragon Sound™ with Qualcomm aptX™ Adaptive, 6 Mics ENC, Multipoint Connection, Bluetooth 5.3 Wireless Earbuds, Cozy Fit, Upgraded App Customization, 33H
reviewText: Been using for just over a week now and I have to say these are pretty good for the price. I legitimately haven't charged the case once since I bought them and have gotten at least 25 hours so far no problem.Most importantly, NO HISSING!!! With other earbuds at this price range I'd get a constant "hissing" sound whenever the audio would be engaged and people would just blame it on me "having a high range of hearing" or something, but I'm happy to say that these have no hissing.The app is fine. It asks for an "account" when you open it but it's easy to skip. Touch controls again but I just disabled them since I don't really like them on any earbuds. The audio passthrough and active noise cancellation seem to be completely useless for me though, but it shouldn't be something at this price range anyway.The fit is fine. I had some issues initially but they seem to stay in fine now. They have a nice shape to them that fills the ear. My old earbuds (galaxy buds) really jutted out but these are very snug.I love the material on the case. The feel of it is just amazing.The only problems I've had with these is that sometimes one side would randomly stop playing audio for a bit, but it seems to happen really rarely, and the app can be a bit slow sometimes. The ear tips that come with it are also of a noticeably low quality, as well as the box being hideous for some reason lol.I was skeptical to buy a brand I didn't know at this price range but it's turned out to be great. I can't say much to their durability yet but I'll update my review if anything happens in the next few years.Edit: After using for over a month the only problem is that very rarely the connection drops. We're talking once or twice in a week, and it's usually only one side and it's easily fixed by just disabling and enabling Bluetooth on my phone. The app is okay; a bit slow but I basically never have to open it since all the settings are properly saved

Has different size silicone ear pieces so plenty to choose from.Battery life is max 3 hours without recharging.Haven't lost them outside yet,  so as long as they're fitted correctly I think they'll be fine.All in all I like them and would recommend as the sound is great.

reviewTitle: Very good sound quality with excellent bass. Fit a bit fiddly as first.
reviewText: Very good sound quality. The optimal fitting took quite some time, but it's supplied with several sizes of fittings, so you should find a good fit. The instruction booklet seems to show a completely impossible angle for fitting. The only secure fit is essentially with the logo horizontal, photo attached. Once that's sorted, I found the fit to be pretty good, with no falling out when walking . I'm not a jogger, but I think they'd stay in. The size is nice and small, with the ear bud not sticking out much at all. You can definitely have them in with your head against a pillow with no discomfort. A couple of photos attached, one of the EarFun and one of my old Echo buds: big difference. The battery life is good and the app lets you change tapping options for both left and right. The noise cancelling is reasonably OK, however, that is more than compensated for by the very good sound quality, so no complaints. I bought these a couple of weeks ago (Feb 2024) and so far I'm very happy with them.

productTitle: Free Pro 3 Noise Cancelling Earbuds, Snapdragon Sound™ with Qualcomm aptX™ Adaptive, 6 Mics ENC, Multipoint Connection, Bluetooth 5.3 Wireless Earbuds, Cozy Fit, Upgraded App Customization, 33H
reviewTitle: Good sound but spoilt by weak noise cancelling and glitchy Bluetooth
reviewText: First the positives. They fit well and don't move or fall out when running. They sound good especially using one of the Oluv equaliser settings. But the noise cancelling isn't good at all. Compared to Bose earbuds they cancel no noise at all. I've used all the settings for noise cancelling and none really reduce external noise by much. The Bluetooth is a bit glitchy (a bit like Bose earbuds) and initial connection is a bit hit and miss. They sometimes connect but then sound a bit like a bad phone connection. This has always gone away after disconnecting and reconnecting though. I've gone though 4 sets of earbuds now and the only ones with noticeable noise cancelling are Bose.

productTitle: Free Pro 3 Noise Cancelling Earbuds, Snapdragon Sound™ with Qualcomm aptX™ Adaptive, 6 Mics ENC, Multipoint Connection, Bluetooth 5.3 Wireless Earbuds, Cozy Fit, Upgraded App Customization, 33H
reviewText: I wasn't too sure what to expect. It's been a long time since I used earbuds as I prefer over ear headphones, but sometimes the headphones are too unwieldy, so I thought I'd give these a try.I chose these purely based on online reviews. I didn't want stem style buds as I don't like the look of them, I have fairly small ears and an even smaller budget. These seemed to be the ones most recommended in my price range along with Soundcore A40 and a couple of others. The aptx support swung it for me.Once I received them, I played around with different sized tips and wings to get the best fit. Then downloaded the app and played around with the equalizer - the Jazz preset was my preferred choice. I left the default controls alone.Pairing to 2 devices is very easy, and when you don't want to use them put them back in the charging case, close the lid and all devices will disconnect.I liked that the chosen EQ not only worked on my phone, but also when connected to my laptop (I thought it would just EQ the sound played by the phone, but it must download the EQ to the buds).  I could play around with the app on my phone whilst playing music via my laptop.Sound quality is very good once you've achieved a snug fit. Plenty loud enough with good clarity and good bass. Can't really detect any difference when using Aptx on my phone, but my hearing is definitely not what it used to be 30 years ago.Looking forward to getting a lot of use out of these.

reviewText: Having previously owned a Samsung S21 Ultra phone and the Buds Pro 2 I was very happy with the sound quality.However since changing to an Oppo Find X5 Pro the Samsung pro buds too sounded terrible.This was down to the Samsung codec not being available on my new phone.From the first listen of these new Earfun buds I was blown away.Before I purchased I went into my developer settings to see what codecs were available before I made the purchase.I found that not only did my phone have the relevant codecs it had the exact same one that this set of earbuds worked the best with.My own lit gripe is that the earphones don't turn off as soon as you take them from your ear.That is the only thing that bothers me.I am very impressed with the same quality and Bill quality of these earphones at this price point.If you have a compatible phone don't hesitate you won't regret it

reviewText: Good quality Noise Cancelling Earbuds from Earfun made from good materials.I have to say I am impressed with these earbuds they are far better than I thought they were going to be. reviewText: After my buds giving up on me I decided to try these ones and I am really impressed with them. productTitle: Free Pro 3 Noise Cancelling Earbuds, Snapdragon Sound™ with Qualcomm aptX™ Adaptive, 6 Mics ENC, Multipoint Connection, Bluetooth 5.3 Wireless Earbuds, Cozy Fit, Upgraded App Customization,33H
reviewText: In the box: 5 sets of ear pieces in rubber style, and 1 earpiece of memory foam + rubber style, which i was using while writing this review. After 2 days of use I am comparing them to previously used CX True Wireless and Outlier Air V3.Good sound, nice extended bass without tiring your ear, especially nice low end for heavy metal tracks, mildly boosted bass, which was good for my 1-2 hours street walking and listening, i enjoy using these earbuds more than using Sennheiser's CX True Wireless or Creative's Outlier Air v3.Fits snuggly in my ear, better than CX TW & OA V3, with memory foam earpieces i can shake my head more confidently and not keep losing them on the street pavement than when using rubber earpieces (with rubber pieces i lose 1 earbud of the 2 too often, quite a disturbing listening experience).Battery is ok, what expected for this price point, didn't have to charge the case for 2 days until it went down to 41% and earpieces 30% + 30%. With more features on in the app, i suspected battery went down much quicker.If the audio recordings are of older release date (like 1991's heavy metal) APTX Adaptive mode will result into an interrupted sound, don't think this is because earbuds are failing just after 1 or 2 days and you want to return them and write a low score feedback, it's better to turn off APTX Adaptive on your smartphone, then during listening to older recordings it will not give interrupted glitchy soundI even tried to play some FPS game like CS2 on steam, it was obviously with some delay, i tried to turn on Game Mode on the smartphone in the EarFun app, apparently it had to reduce delay, it was acceptable experience, we won a competitive match, still had some kind of delay in transmission and i heard my teammates talking a tad later.ANC function is better when the music is playing, otherwise: when music stops playing it's just an ok ANC, you can still hear surroundings and keyboard taps.Got it at the time when it was 59.99, well spent, felt like i should have bought these first, instead of trying to find that one cheap budget set of eardbuds (like i did with CX TW and OA v3). Probably would rebuy Earfun Free Pro 3 or Earfun 4 in the future, and not the budget Creative or Sennheiser

productTitle: Free Pro 3 Noise Cancelling Earbuds, Snapdragon Sound™ with Qualcomm aptX™ Adaptive, 6 Mics ENC, Multipoint Connection, Bluetooth 5.3 Wireless Earbuds, Cozy Fit, Upgraded App Customization,33H
reviewTitle: Compact, good sounding earbuds that fit securely in the ear.
reviewText: I have a large selection of reasonably priced Bluetooth earbuds. I keep trying to find my ideal pair.  I think these are the closest so far. I'm no audiophile so take what I say with that in mind.I did have a problem using them with the PlayerPro audio player app I use, at first. It turned out to be an incompatibility between the EQ on the app and the EQ on the earbuds. There was a lot of distortion on some songs and lack of depth on others. Switching off the EQ on the PlayerPro app solved the problem. Even I'd you switch off the EQ on the earbuds the EQ on the PlayerPro app causes distortion, yet it works fine with all my other Bluetooth earbuds.I tried all the preset EQ settings on the Earfun app and Pop seems the best for most of the music I listen to ( mostly pop, EDM, easy listening).I was unimpressed with the noise cancelling when I tried it first with a fan running in the room I was in. I could still hear it quite clearly. On a train, the noise was only reduced slightly.  I WAS impressed when I tried it on a plane and the engine noise dropped significantly. I then tried it when on a cruise ship but it didn't seem to reduce the constant noise of the ships generator at all, all ANC settings sounded the same to me as did the other two modes ambient and normal. I'm guessing the ANC has been tuned for frequencies produced by plane engines.Assessing sound quality is personal. I like the sound they produce but it is still lacking. Songs that give me chills when I listen to them on my Bluetooth speaker don't do the same when I use the earbuds. As I said, I'm not an audiophile and I can't say what is missing but something is. They are certainly better in sound quality some of my other earbuds and they are a comfortable and secure fit.Maybe I need to change to over ear headphones to get the sound quality I want rather than keep using earbuds but they are convenient to carry and I these ones are certainly good value for all the built-in functionality.

productTitle: Free Pro 3 Noise Cancelling Earbuds, Snapdragon Sound™ with Qualcomm aptX™ Adaptive, 6 Mics ENC, Multipoint Connection, Bluetooth 5.3 Wireless Earbuds, Cozy Fit, Upgraded App Customization,33H
reviewText: Been using for just over a week now and I have to say these are pretty good for the price. I legitimately haven't charged the case once since I bought them and have gotten at least 25 hours so far no problem.Most importantly, NO HISSING!!! With other earbuds at this price range I'd get a constant "hissing" sound whenever the audio would be engaged and people would just blame it on me "having a high range of hearing" or something, but I'm happy to say that these have no hissing.The app is fine. It asks for an "account" when you open it but it's easy to skip. Touch controls again but I just disabled them since I don't really like them on any earbuds. The audio passthrough and active noise cancellation seem to be completely useless for me though, but it shouldn't be something at this price range anyway.The fit is fine. I had some issues initially but they seem to stay in fine now. They have a nice shape to them that fills the ear. My old earbuds (galaxy buds) really jutted out but these are very snug.I love the material on the case. The feel of it is just amazing.The only problems I've had with these is that sometimes one side would randomly stop playing audio for a bit, but it seems to happen really rarely, and the app can be a bit slow sometimes. The ear tips that come with it are also of a noticeably low quality, as well as the box being hideous for some reason lol.I was skeptical to buy a brand I didn't know at this price range but it's turned out to be great. I can't say much to their durability yet but I'll update my review if anything happens in the next few years.Edit: After using for over a month the only problem is that very rarely the connection drops. We're talking once or twice in a week, and it's usually only one side and it's easily fixed by just disabling and enabling Bluetooth on my phone. The app is okay; a bit slow but I basically never have to open it since all the settings are properly saved

productTitle: Free Pro 3 Noise Cancelling Earbuds, Snapdragon Sound™ with Qualcomm aptX™ Adaptive, 6 Mics ENC, Multipoint Connection, Bluetooth 5.3 Wireless Earbuds, Cozy Fit, Upgraded App Customization,33H

reviewTitle: Very good sound quality with excellent bass. Fit a bit fiddly as first.
reviewText: Very good sound quality. The optimal fitting took quite some time, but it's supplied with several sizes of fittings, so you should find a good fit. The instruction booklet seems to show a completely impossible angle for fitting. The only secure fit is essentially with the logo horizontal, photo attached. Once that's sorted, I found the fit to be pretty good, with no falling out when walking . I'm not a jogger, but I think they'd stay in. The size is nice and small, with the ear bud not sticking out much at all. You can definitely have them in with your head against a pillow with no discomfort. A couple of photos attached, one of the EarFun and one of my old Echo buds: big difference. The battery life is good and the app lets you change tapping options for both left and right. The noise cancelling is reasonably OK, however, that is more than compensated for by the very good sound quality, so no complaints. I bought these a couple of weeks ago (Feb 2024) and so far I'm very happy with them.

reviewText: First the positives. They fit well and don't move or fall out when running. They sound good especially using one of the Oluv equaliser settings. But the noise cancelling isn't good at all. Compared to Bose earbuds they cancel no noise at all. I've used all the settings for noise cancelling and none really reduce external noise by much. The Bluetooth is a bit glitchy (a bit like Bose earbuds) and initial connection is a bit hit and miss. They sometimes connect but then sound a bit like a bad phone connection. This has always gone away after disconnecting and reconnecting though. I've gone though 4 sets of earbuds now and the only ones with noticeable noise cancelling are Bose.

productTitle: Free Pro 3 Noise Cancelling Earbuds, Snapdragon Sound™ with Qualcomm aptX™ Adaptive, 6 Mics ENC, Multipoint Connection, Bluetooth 5.3 Wireless Earbuds, Cozy Fit, Upgraded App Customization,33H
reviewText: I wasn't too sure what to expect. It's been a long time since I used earbuds as I prefer over ear headphones, but sometimes the headphones are too unwieldy, so I thought I'd give these a try.I chose these purely based on online reviews. I didn't want stem style buds as I don't like the look of them, I have fairly small ears and an even smaller budget. These seemed to be the ones most recommended in my price range along with Soundcore A40 and a couple of others. The aptx support swung it for me.Once I received them, I played around with different sized tips and wings to get the best fit. Then downloaded the app and played around with the equalizer - the Jazz preset was my preferred choice. I left the default controls alone.Pairing to 2 devices is very easy, and when you don't want to use them put them back in the charging case, close the lid and all devices will disconnect.I liked that the chosen EQ not only worked on my phone, but also when connected to my laptop (I thought it would just EQ the sound played by the phone, but it must download the EQ to the buds).  I could play around with the app on my phone whilst playing music via my laptop.Sound quality is very good once you've achieved a snug fit. Plenty loud enough with good clarity and good bass. Can't really detect any difference when using Aptx on my phone, but my hearing is definitely not what it used to be 30 years ago.Looking forward to getting a lot of use out of these.

reviewText: Good quality Noise Cancelling Earbuds from Earfun made from good materials.I have to say I am impressed with these earbuds they are far better than I thought they were going to be. They come with enough earhooks to meet any size.I have been using the earbuds with an iPhone 14 Pro Max and it is very easy to setup it is just a case of pairing it using bluetooth this will be the same for any smartphone/bluetooth device.The sound quality and noise cancellation is very good it is not excellent but comes very close my only minor grumble is the sound level is not that great BUT I do like my music to be loud so this might be more than loud enough for most peopleOverall I am happy with the noise cancelling earbuds.

productTitle: Free Pro 3 Noise Cancelling Earbuds, Snapdragon Sound™ with Qualcomm aptX™ Adaptive, 6 Mics ENC, Multipoint Connection, Bluetooth 5.3 Wireless Earbuds, Cozy Fit, Upgraded App Customization,33H
reviewText: After my buds giving up on me I decided to try these ones and I am really impressed with them. They look sleeker and feel a lot more comfortable in the ear too and are generally a lot smaller and discreet which is a bonus.The set up was so simple and quick just pair with your Bluetooth and off you go!The battery lasts well I only charge them every 3-4 days so this is an improvement on my previous ones. productTitle: Air 2 Wireless Earbuds, Bluetooth 5.3 Earphones with Hi-Res Sound, LDAC Codec, 10mm Wool Drivers, Multipoint Connection, 4 Mics ENC Clear Calls, IPX7 Waterproof, App Customization, 40H Playtime
reviewText: I have to be honest, I was pleasantly surprised by these earphones. The sound quality from them is brilliant. I consider myself an audiophile and am quite fussy over how I like my music to sound and these really didn't disappoint. I listen to all sorts of music from pop, easy listening through to punk and prog rock and these didn't struggle with any genre.These are louder than most other earphones I've purchased over the years which I think is a good thing as most are too quiet. You can tweak the sound however you like using the EQ settings within the app. The bass is very deep and the treble quite bright but not overly so on default settings but that is how I like my music to sound so I was quite happy. The detail portrayed is very good and I didn't struggle to hear little nuances in the music that I have struggles with on full size headphones costing 3 times more.The pebble shaped case is quite small which is a good thing for those that like to keep it in their pocket. It has a charging point on the base along with a bluetooth sync button.The phones slotted into my ears very well with the set of silicon pads already fitted but they come with an additional 3 size sets which should fit most ear types. These things are comfortable, I didn't have to push or twist to get them to fit snugly, they just fell straight into place in my ears and once in weren't uncomfortable at all and didn't work loose.Battery life is very good and in general use I've only had to charge the case once in a week with the earphones being charged by the case once put away.Earfun even include a cleaning bud for keeping the connections clean and you get a charging cable included along with full instructions.The app is very good and allows you to adjust the tap controls for each earphone separately. You can even use the app to send a beeping sound to help find your earphones. You can update firmware, control duel device settings, adjust which microphone to use for calls and even adjust the voice prompt volume. It also shows the battery life of both the case and earphones separately which is very handy.Overall I'm very happy with these and think they are a bit of a bargain. This coupled with the fact that Earfun customer service is brilliant means you can buy these with confidence and if any issues arise, they will strive to put it right.5+ stars.

productTitle: Air 2 Wireless Earbuds, Bluetooth 5.3 Earphones with Hi-Res Sound, LDAC Codec, 10mm Wool Drivers, Multipoint Connection, 4 Mics ENC Clear Calls, IPX7 Waterproof, App Customization, 40H Playtime
reviewText: Well all I can say is that I am super impressed with these earbuds especially for the cost.They have a beautiful deep range of sound that really allows you to feel the music.The buds sit very nicely in ear and create an excellent natural noise cancellation and I find the sound clear and uninterrupted except when I'm out walking with heavy traffic going passed.The battery life is good enough for most use, I listen to them all day in work and although the buds do run out, the case offers several charges and charged them really quickly as well.They aren't going to match your top of the range buds but for a fraction on the cost I would say the offer at least 90% of the quality.The Earfun app allows you to play around with the equalizer and other features so you can fine tune the buds to your taste.The only minor complaint I have is the touch controls which can be a bit hit or miss so I often end up manually changing songs. Not a deal breaker for me given all the other plus points at this price range.I have buds that cost far more that are now redundant as I want good quality sound and these do the business.All in all I would highly recommend unless your caught up in wanting all the features you get with buds that cost 7 or 8 times as much.

productTitle: Air 2 Wireless Earbuds, Bluetooth 5.3 Earphones with Hi-Res Sound, LDAC Codec, 10mm Wool Drivers, Multipoint Connection, 4 Mics ENC Clear Calls, IPX7 Waterproof, App Customization, 40H Playtime
reviewText: As an avid fan of EarFun products I was looking to test these against both my Airpods 2’s, Airpods’s Pro, EarFun Air Pro 3’s and EarFun Air S’s (which I do hold in regard as my favourite earphones).Being honest, I did not hold much hope of these fending of the later. However, they are fantastic and I have to say, my favourite pair to date.I always felt something was lacking when comparing the EarFun Pro 3’s to my EarFun Air S’s. They were a quality pair of earphones but the Air’s always managed to steal the show and had me going back to them.The EarFun Air 2’s are better than them all. If you like the ability to adjust your equaliser settings and make your earphones louder, you won’t get better than this pair. They come packaged with extra ear tips, charging cable, manual and cleaning tools, as well as an 18 month warranty if you register them with the company.As always, I can say that the warranty definitely holds up. When a sudden fault occurred in my Air S’s, the company had a replacement set to me within 3 days. I can’t say the same for other companies on Amazon when things have developed a fault.If you are looking for a good pair of earphones with excellent sound quality, customisable app options and a long warranty look no further.Can’t wait for the next release.
