productTitle: Air 2 Wireless Earbuds, Bluetooth 5.3 Earphones with Hi-Res Sound, LDAC Codec, 10mm Wool Drivers, Multipoint Connection, 4 Mics ENC Clear Calls, IPX7 Waterproof, App Customization, 40H Playtime
reviewTitle: Impressive sound at this price point
reviewText: The sound is impressive, the case is good (not Apple quality but good for the price point). The EarPods feature tap controls, which is useful in any situation.The sound is really good considering price and size, even some bass is there.Battery life isn’t great and will drop with time.The fit is good the pods themselves are very light, thus comfortable and versatile to wear. Instructions are clear enough to get started quickly.

productTitle: Air 2 Wireless Earbuds, Bluetooth 5.3 Earphones with Hi-Res Sound, LDAC Codec, 10mm Wool Drivers, Multipoint Connection, 4 Mics ENC Clear Calls, IPX7 Waterproof, App Customization, 40H Playtime
reviewText: I recently upgraded to these wireless earbuds, and they have truly impressed me with their outstanding performance and features. The Bluetooth 5.3 connectivity ensures a stable and seamless connection, making pairing with multiple devices a breeze. The hi-res sound quality is phenomenal, thanks to the LDAC support and 10mm wool drivers that deliver rich, immersive audio with excellent clarity and depth.The multi-connection capability is a standout feature, allowing me to switch effortlessly between my phone, tablet, and laptop. This convenience is further enhanced by the app customization options, which let me fine-tune the sound profile and control settings to my preference.Call quality is top-notch with the four mics and ENC (Environmental Noise Cancellation) technology, ensuring my voice is clear and free from background noise during calls. The IPX7 waterproof rating gives me peace of mind, knowing that these earbuds can withstand sweat and rain, making them perfect for workouts and outdoor activities.Battery life is impressive, with up to 40 hours of playtime on a single charge. This longevity means I can enjoy my music and podcasts throughout the day without worrying about frequent recharging. The white colour adds a sleek and stylish touch, making these earbuds not only functional but also visually appealing.Overall, these wireless earbuds have exceeded my expectations in every aspect. They offer exceptional sound quality, robust features, and reliable performance, making them a fantastic choice for anyone seeking high-quality audio and versatility in their daily listening experience. Highly recommended!

productTitle: Air 2 Wireless Earbuds, Bluetooth 5.3 Earphones with Hi-Res Sound, LDAC Codec, 10mm Wool Drivers, Multipoint Connection, 4 Mics ENC Clear Calls, IPX7 Waterproof, App Customization, 40H Playtime
reviewText: These earbuds come boxed in a charging case together with instructions and USB-C charging cable. The earbuds are the silicone-tipped variety, which I have to push into my ears to get any kind of decent sound quality out of - if the fit is loose they sound tinny, but these are a good size for me. The design of the case makes the earbuds relatively easy to remove and replace. The case features a LED power light but no percentage display.I had no trouble pairing the earbuds to my phone using Bluetooth. Sound quality is very good, I listen to podcasts and mp3s of varying bit rates anyway so for casual listening these are impressive given the price. I find touch controls fiddly so was pleased to find that they could be disabled via the app - most of my earbuds don't have app support so this is a nice premium feature.Battery life is decent and they have been working well so overall I'm very happy with these earbuds.

productTitle: Air 2 Wireless Earbuds, Bluetooth 5.3 Earphones with Hi-Res Sound, LDAC Codec, 10mm Wool Drivers, Multipoint Connection, 4 Mics ENC Clear Calls, IPX7 Waterproof, App Customization, 40H Playtime
reviewTitle: A great pair of earbuds with plenty of features
reviewText: These are my second pair of EarFun earbuds, I was very impressed with my first set and I'm glad to say this pair continues in the same vein.They come in a very professional looking box, making them ideal to give as a gift, containing the earbuds in their case along with several different ear pieces, a charging cable and an excellent instruction booklet.The supplied ear pieces mean that you have plenty of options to customise the fit so they are perfect for your ears.The earbuds are charged by placing them in the supplied case which holds them in place using magnets, this can make it a little tricky to get them out of case the first few times you use, them but you soon get the hang of removing them without firing them across the room.The case also provides a couple of ways of charging, either using USB-C with the supplied cable or with wireless charging for complete convenience.The case is very compact and easily slips into a pocket so you can take the earbuds with you wherever you travel.Right out of the box they are a little bit bass rich, not overpowering just slightly pronounced, if you are into dance or electronic music this is ideal.But, if you want to rein the bass in a little the great software has plenty of pre-set equalisers or you can create your own. There is even a function to tune the earbuds to your own hearing response.Aside from the bass the rest of the sound spectrum is clear and detailed revealing individual instruments and vocals with great clarity.Once you have found the best ear piece for you the earbuds are comfortable to listen too for extended periods with no listening fatigue.Overall, a great pair of earbuds which you won't be disappointed with.

productTitle: Air 2 Wireless Earbuds, Bluetooth 5.3 Earphones with Hi-Res Sound, LDAC Codec, 10mm Wool Drivers, Multipoint Connection, 4 Mics ENC Clear Calls, IPX7 Waterproof, App Customization, 40H Playtime
reviewTitle: Decent quality sound but fiddly controls
reviewText: These are pretty good earphones for comfort and sound quality. They are very clear and there is even a hint of bass (I never expect any bass worth talking about with earphones).They fit very snugly in my ear canal and make a good seal which gets rid of a lot of background noise. As far as I can tell these don't have any active noise cancelling.There is a companion phone app which can allow you do change the EQ, but I didn't try it.Where these earbuds fall down for me is with the controls. In theory you single tap left for volume down, right for volume up, double tap either for pause and triple tap left/right for previous/next track. The volume controls work ok, but pause/track change controls are randomly done when I double/triple tap, it is very imprecise.In addition to the tapping, you have to tap on the part above your ear hole, and as the buds are snug in the ear, it isn't pleasant to be tapping and causing compression of the air in your ear canal.Something else missing is for the buds to auto pause when you remove them from your ears like some other buds do.If you don't need to pause/change tracks often, these are a decent buy, but for me the controls make them hard to give an easy recommendation.

productTitle: Air 2 Wireless Earbuds, Bluetooth 5.3 Earphones with Hi-Res Sound, LDAC Codec, 10mm Wool Drivers, Multipoint Connection, 4 Mics ENC Clear Calls, IPX7 Waterproof, App Customization, 40H Playtime
reviewTitle: A very nice set of earbuds
reviewText: These are very nice earbuds that support SBC, AAC and LDAC Bluetooth codecs However you can only use LDAC by turning it on in the app, and this will turn off multipoint connections.The case is well built with a nice hinge, it's also quite small. As for the buds they are also well made and have XS size ear buds, which is great if you have smaller ears.Media controls by default are:1 tap - volume control. (Left = down, right = up)2 taps - play/pause (both)3 taps - track skip (left = previous, right = next)Hold - voice assistant (both)Though they can be changed via the app to what you prefer. You can also add Game Mode to the buds controls which is missing by default.Game Mode significantly lowers the Bluetooth lag when gaming. You can turn it on via the app or and a control to the ear bud.The buds fit in my ears well and didn't fall out even with aggressive head shaking.Note: Battery life runs out much faster with LDAC used.

productTitle: Air 2 Wireless Earbuds, Bluetooth 5.3 Earphones with Hi-Res Sound, LDAC Codec, 10mm Wool Drivers, Multipoint Connection, 4 Mics ENC Clear Calls, IPX7 Waterproof, App Customization, 40H Playtime
reviewText: Finding these wireless earbuds has genuinely enhanced my audio experience. The sound quality is impressive, with deep bass and clear treble. The fit is comfortable, making them ideal for long listening sessions. The battery life is excellent, and the charging case is very convenient. Pairing with multiple devices has been seamless, and the call clarity is superb, even in noisy environments. Waterproofing adds extra confidence when using them during workouts.

productTitle: Air 2 Wireless Earbuds, Bluetooth 5.3 Earphones with Hi-Res Sound, LDAC Codec, 10mm Wool Drivers, Multipoint Connection, 4 Mics ENC Clear Calls, IPX7 Waterproof, App Customization, 40H Playtime
reviewText: These wireless earbuds have exceeded my expectations in terms of sound quality and comfort. Their Hi-Res audio and 10mm wool drivers deliver rich, immersive sound with crisp highs and deep bass. The multi-connection feature and ENC clear calls ensure seamless connectivity and crystal-clear voice quality during calls. Plus, the IPX7 waterproof rating and 40-hour playtime make them perfect for workouts and outdoor activities. Whether I'm listening to music or taking calls on the go, these earbuds deliver exceptional performance. Overall, I'm highly satisfied with these earbuds, and they've become my go-to choice for everyday use.

productTitle: Air 2 Wireless Earbuds, Bluetooth 5.3 Earphones with Hi-Res Sound, LDAC Codec, 10mm Wool Drivers, Multipoint Connection, 4 Mics ENC Clear Calls, IPX7 Waterproof, App Customization, 40H Playtime
reviewText: This set of earbuds from EarFun are an excellent value for money and offer so many features, including their own app!Right from the start the impression you get from these earbuds are that they are much more premium that the price tag would suggest.  The are really well made, a nice size & fit, and are aesthetically pleasing too.  The come with their own charging pod as well as a total of 4 different sized tips that can be changed over to best fit your ear size and shape.  A USB-C charge cable is also included as-is a cleaning stick.Once paired with your device they work straight away and the sound quality is excellent - I was genuinely impressed as to how good they were.  The base levels are great and the spoken word / call quality is brilliant too.  The side panels on the earbuds can be used to adjust the volume, answer call and switch tracks with ease.However all of the default settings such as the sound profile, and functions of the side panels can be customized in the EarFun Audio app which can be downloaded for free.  This is a fantastic add-extra and there is no need to create an account or provide any personal details in order to use the app - perfect!At the time of writing this review the price of these EarFun earbuds is just £39.99 after the 20% discount voucher has been applied, making them excellent value for money and highly recommended.

productTitle: Air 2 Wireless Earbuds, Bluetooth 5.3 Earphones with Hi-Res Sound, LDAC Codec, 10mm Wool Drivers, Multipoint Connection, 4 Mics ENC Clear Calls, IPX7 Waterproof, App Customization, 40H Playtime
reviewTitle: Superior Sound and Versatility with EarFun Wireless Earbuds Air 2.
reviewText: The EarFun Wireless Earbuds Air 2 have exceeded my expectations with their impressive features and performance. Equipped with Bluetooth 5.3 and Hi-Res sound quality, these earbuds deliver crystal-clear audio, enhanced by the LDAC technology and 10mm wool drivers for rich, immersive sound.The multi-connection capability allows seamless switching between devices, which is incredibly convenient for multitasking. The four ENC mics ensure clear calls even in noisy environments, making communication effortless.These earbuds are designed for durability with an IPX7 waterproof rating, perfect for workouts and outdoor activities. The app customization feature adds a personal touch, allowing me to tailor the sound to my preferences. With a remarkable 40-hour playtime, I can enjoy uninterrupted music and calls throughout the day.Overall, the EarFun Wireless Earbuds Air 2 offer excellent value for money with their superior sound quality, versatility, and robust design. Highly recommended for anyone seeking high-performance wireless earbuds.

productTitle: Air 2 Wireless Earbuds, Bluetooth 5.3 Earphones with Hi-Res Sound, LDAC Codec, 10mm Wool Drivers, Multipoint Connection, 4 Mics ENC Clear Calls, IPX7 Waterproof, App Customization, 40H Playtime
reviewText: Easy to use and great value for money, would recommend.

productTitle: Air 2 Wireless Earbuds, Bluetooth 5.3 Earphones with Hi-Res Sound, LDAC Codec, 10mm Wool Drivers, Multipoint Connection, 4 Mics ENC Clear Calls, IPX7 Waterproof, App Customization, 40H Playtime
reviewText: Decent earbuds with surprisingly good sound. Four stars.

productTitle: Air 2 Wireless Earbuds, Bluetooth 5.3 Earphones with Hi-Res Sound, LDAC Codec, 10mm Wool Drivers, Multipoint Connection, 4 Mics ENC Clear Calls, IPX7 Waterproof, App Customization, 40H Playtime
reviewTitle: Pleasantly surprised by the quality. A great gift.
reviewText: I've only had these EarFun earbuds for a few days but they have had a dramatic effect on my appreciation of music, especially classical guitar. I will certainly use them a lot more than I ever used my expensive headphones.They paired instantly with my phone and I was impressed by the build quality, battery life and comfort.The stand-out EarFun feature is probably the ability to tweak the sound with the app-based equaliser. This is superb if you have a little top-end hearing loss or, conversely, you find some styles of music a little harsh. I don't normally listen to heavy music but these stood up very well and the improved clarity was quite noticeable.Audio tests are always highly subjective but on the basis of my elevated enjoyment, I have to say that I cannot fault these earbuds and look forward to many hours of pleasure. Highly recommended.

productTitle: Air 2 Wireless Earbuds, Bluetooth 5.3 Earphones with Hi-Res Sound, LDAC Codec, 10mm Wool Drivers, Multipoint Connection, 4 Mics ENC Clear Calls, IPX7 Waterproof, App Customization, 40H Playtime
reviewText: These earbuds are very nicely presented in a well made box that includes the earbuds with case, a set of 3 different sized ear gels, USB C charging cable and well written instructions. They are very light and comfortable to wear. They paired easily to my Android phone and there’s also an app that I haven’t tried which apparently enables adjustment of equalisation.The main question is how do they sound? The answer is the sound quality is excellent, especially considering the price. I also tried making calls and the audio performance from the four microphones is also really good.I haven’t yet run down the battery but the claim is for up to 9 hours for the buds and another 40 hours with the case. The case itself, which is very nicely made, can be charged vis USB C or wirelessly.Overall, this is a great set of wireless ear buds at a reasonable price.

productTitle: Air 2 Wireless Earbuds, Bluetooth 5.3 Earphones with Hi-Res Sound, LDAC Codec, 10mm Wool Drivers, Multipoint Connection, 4 Mics ENC Clear Calls, IPX7 Waterproof, App Customization, 40H Playtime
reviewText: I am by no means an audiophile, but I know what I like and what sounds good to me and I am seriously impressed by these Earfun Air 2 earphones. I've now been using these for 4 weeks as my daily earbuds, they're that good and I own more premium and expensive ones from some big brands.The design of the earbuds is very much like that of the Apple AirPods with the stems, which is the first pair of this style I've owned as all my other pairs are of the in ear design. I found these extremely comfortable to wear even for prolonged use and I didn't suffer from any ear fatigue after 4 hours of constant use. I used the pre installed medium silicone tips, but there are also other sizes bundles in the box if these aren't right for you. The earbuds are gloss white in colour with a silver strip with the Earfun branding on, this has a slightly matt finish to it giving slightly more grip when you handle them.To get the most out of these earbuds the first thing you need to do is download the Earfun Audio App available from both the Google Play store or the Apple App store. Once you have downloaded the app you can connect it to the earbuds where it opens up a world of customisation, from EQ setting to what you want to assign to the touch controls, which is a great feature and not something that is always available on other brands. The app also has a find my earbuds feature again something many rivals don't have.The touch controls work well, although as the touch sensors are at the top of the buds I did on occasions accidentally activate them when just moving them in my ears.There is no ANC here, which isn't a surprise given the price, but there is a good amount of passive noise cancellation due to the excellent fit of these earbuds. But what they lack in ANC they make up for in their ability to play Hi-Res Audio and LDAC codecs which produce outstanding sound quality from supported sources.There is also a game mode which can be turned on via the app, this worked well in my test of playing games like pubg mobile, I could certainly identify where enemies were coming from easier and although not groundbreaking as lots of competitors have similar features it's a nice addition.The call clarity from these earbuds is much better than on some of my other pairs, with clear, crisp voices both received and transmitted.Battery life is fantastic, I've only charged these once so far in 4 weeks of mixed usage and charging is on par with many others.Value wise these are amazing and how Earfun has crammed so much tech into these and kept the price so low is astonishing as at the moment the black version can be snapped up for £35 and the white for £40 which is an absolute bargain and makes these so easy to recommend.

productTitle: Air 2 Wireless Earbuds, Bluetooth 5.3 Earphones with Hi-Res Sound, LDAC Codec, 10mm Wool Drivers, Multipoint Connection, 4 Mics ENC Clear Calls, IPX7 Waterproof, App Customization, 40H Playtime
reviewText: I've got a cheaper pair which have been fine but don't last long on the charge but you can tell these are a better quality in terms of just the feel and the sound quality. The battery lasts for ages as well.I like that they come with alternative sizes buds because I have really small ear canals and find the larger ones hurt which is a great addition.I've not used the app with it and won't likely do that but I can imagine that would be good for someone really into their music.The presentation box is also lovely which is nice if you are gifting them.It takes a while to get used to the volume controls but that's the same with these type - especially because you can't see them to change them. Might just be my age though, ha ha.So yes, there are so many choices out there but this set feels good quality, with good sound and a great battery life.

productTitle: Air 2 Wireless Earbuds, Bluetooth 5.3 Earphones with Hi-Res Sound, LDAC Codec, 10mm Wool Drivers, Multipoint Connection, 4 Mics ENC Clear Calls, IPX7 Waterproof, App Customization, 40H Playtime
reviewText: Experience audio excellence with the Bluetooth Earphones. Boasting LDAC codec and Hi-Res Audio certification, these earbuds deliver rich, detailed sound with minimal signal degradation. The 4-mic ENC technology ensures exceptional call clarity, making conversations transparent and realistic. With a total playtime of 40 hours and Bluetooth 5.3 multipoint connection, these earbuds offer convenience and versatility. Their lightweight design and ergonomic fit ensure all-day comfort, while the EarFun App allows for personalized audio settings and features like 'Find Headphones.' Whether for music or calls, they  provides an unmatched listening experience.

productTitle: Air 2 Wireless Earbuds, Bluetooth 5.3 Earphones with Hi-Res Sound, LDAC Codec, 10mm Wool Drivers, Multipoint Connection, 4 Mics ENC Clear Calls, IPX7 Waterproof, App Customization, 40H Playtime
reviewTitle: Sound good, shame about the lack of noise cancelling.
reviewText: The headline says it all really. These come in a small case, which makes them easy to carry, and all you do to pair them is open the lid, and go to your phone (you'll have to remove all the little protective tabs first time). These came charged to 100%, which was great.They're easy to fit in the ears, and pretty comfortable, although they're a bit slippy to take out of the case. The touch controls work with no issues and I had no dropouts at all with my Hiby music player. Volume wasn't mega, but that's as much a fault of the player as anything so I wouldn't read too much into that.I listened to tracks by...if I can remember...Fleet Foxes, Bob Dylan, Led Zeppelin, Lonnie Donegan, Abba, Bruce Springsteen... you get the picture. None of them sounded anything less than very good, and oh yes, I played a bit of Billy Eilish with real bass, and that too was great.The only thing you might expect at this price these days is noise cancelling, but to be fair it's rarely all that good unless you pay a lot more so does it matter? I wouldn't use them on a long walk by the side of the main road where we live, but I'd take them in the park and not worry about noise levels.Yeah. I like them.

productTitle: Air 2 Wireless Earbuds, Bluetooth 5.3 Earphones with Hi-Res Sound, LDAC Codec, 10mm Wool Drivers, Multipoint Connection, 4 Mics ENC Clear Calls, IPX7 Waterproof, App Customization, 40H Playtime
reviewTitle: Crisp sound, long-lasting battery and customisable - a necessity in my life!
reviewText: I didn't realise just how great these would be when I first saw them. You can download an app as stated in the earphones manual, which helps set the sound quality and what kind of bass/sound you like boosted. You can also set things like, tap twice on your left earphone and it'll skip a song, which is so handy for handsfree use.Obviously they're on the mid-range price scale, nowhere near as expensive as Apple, but not as cheap as ones you could get. But for what it's worth, I think these are very worth the price and seen as I haven't even needed to charge them more than once since I got them (and have worn them for hours for several nights) I would say they are completely a necessity in my daily life now.

productTitle: Air 2 Wireless Earbuds, Bluetooth 5.3 Earphones with Hi-Res Sound, LDAC Codec, 10mm Wool Drivers, Multipoint Connection, 4 Mics ENC Clear Calls, IPX7 Waterproof, App Customization, 40H Playtime
reviewText: I have tested a LOT of TWS buds in my tester career, some are great, some not so much, and generally it depends mostly on the price point, the expensive one packing a punch, the midrange ones being well, less ambitious.The Aerfun Ari 2 buck that trend. At the time I'm testing them, they're less that 50 quids, with a 20pct voucher.And for that I would not expect such a great sound, not just punchy but clear as a bell, a great design (with buds that actually fit naturally in the ear, and a good build quality) and cherry on top, a really neat Android app (which are generally the downside as these cost quite a lot for small brands so they rarely bother with the niceties).Seriously, not going to write a long one. If you are on the market for great TWS and don't have the budget to go top of the range, grab these. Trust me, you won't regret it

productTitle: Air 2 Wireless Earbuds, Bluetooth 5.3 Earphones with Hi-Res Sound, LDAC Codec, 10mm Wool Drivers, Multipoint Connection, 4 Mics ENC Clear Calls, IPX7 Waterproof, App Customization, 40H Playtime
reviewText: These earbuds have elevated my listening experience tremendously. Sound quality is top-notch, with clear and detailed audio across various genres. Calls are crisp, thanks to the noise-canceling microphones, which filter out background sounds effectively. The fit is comfortable, even for long periods, making them perfect for extended use. The battery life is impressive, allowing me to use them throughout the day without frequent recharges. Connection is stable and quick, seamlessly switching between my phone and laptop. An excellent choice for both music lovers and professionals needing reliable audio gear.

productTitle: Air 2 Wireless Earbuds, Bluetooth 5.3 Earphones with Hi-Res Sound, LDAC Codec, 10mm Wool Drivers, Multipoint Connection, 4 Mics ENC Clear Calls, IPX7 Waterproof, App Customization, 40H Playtime
reviewText: These EarFun Air 2 wireless earbuds in a decent case are a good option if you want decent sound quality, long battery life, and a comfortable fit, all at a reasonable price. The sound quality is actually very good, especially for the price. They have a long battery life of up to 40 hours with the charging case. They are also nice and comfortable and have a lightweight designa and water resistance.  Once you downloas the app it allows EQ customization and other decent features. Now I am not saying these are as good as ones that cost three or four times as much, but one thing is for sure.  You are not going to be as completely devastated if you lose them.

productTitle: Air 2 Wireless Earbuds, Bluetooth 5.3 Earphones with Hi-Res Sound, LDAC Codec, 10mm Wool Drivers, Multipoint Connection, 4 Mics ENC Clear Calls, IPX7 Waterproof, App Customization, 40H Playtime
reviewText: My main question with this was 'will they work with an iPhone', as it didn't seem clear from the page. Well yes they do. - You can't use the LDAC part of them but they are perfectly usable as a pair of bluetooth earbuds.I've found the sound quality of them to be really very good and, for me, they are very comfortable and stay in my ears. They do come with three differing sizes of buds in the package also.The case is nicely made and looks smart and I've had over the nice hours of listed battery life from them. I think these are a very good pair of Bluetooth earbuds at a very fair price.

productTitle: Air 2 Wireless Earbuds, Bluetooth 5.3 Earphones with Hi-Res Sound, LDAC Codec, 10mm Wool Drivers, Multipoint Connection, 4 Mics ENC Clear Calls, IPX7 Waterproof, App Customization, 40H Playtime
reviewTitle: Look great, very fiddly to use
reviewText: These are really a triumph of form over function. Starting with the robust little box, which is almost impossible to open. The top part should have "thumb" cut-outs so you can grip the inner part whilst pulling the outer lid off, but it doesn't. A struggle just to get the box open, as the lid is very close-fitting.Inside, the holder/charger is a smooth flattened egg shape, nestled - quite tightly - inside a flattened egg shaped recess in the plastic moulding. There's a bit of a cut-out but the charger is smooth and unless you have sticky fingers, you won't get it out on the first attempt. Once out, the top flips open - but again it's completely smooth and nothing to grip. There is a knack to it and once learnt it's OK...  The individual buds are held in by quite a strong magnet, and again it's hard to get a grip to pull them out. There's a button on the bottom of the charger but again, totally smooth and virtually invisible; you certainly couldn't find it by touch alone or in dim light. There are volume / function "buttons" (touch areas) on the buds and again, not marked.So these take a bit of getting used to and can be fiddly, especially at first use.But they're great. Sound quality is great, volume control is great, battery life is ... OK.The associated phone app does virtually nothing as far as I can see. And the name - EarFun - is just cringeworthy.I'm a "newbie" to using ear buds, and still think that people walking down the street talking to an "invisible friend" look totally ridiculous. But I'm an ancient luddite.  Certainly these will be used - for instance when I'm working in the garage. I don't need to use them for phone calls; if I take a call, I stop what I'm doing to give the caller my full attention, so holding a phone is no problem.  What I am disappointed about - and it's not the buds' fault - is that they don't work well with my Windows laptop. I'd hoped I could listen to zoom webinars etc whilst in the same room as my partner watching TV. But the Bluetooth on my (quite old) laptop is such that unless my head is within 12" of the laptop keyboard, the bluetooth signal fades in and out every 1 - 2 seconds on each bud; much of the time either one or the other is working, but sometimes neither (and sometimes both).  A check of the bluetooth signal from the laptop confirms the signal is very weak, so can't blame these buds really.There are cheaper buds on the market, and certainly ones that don't challenge you just opening the box. But these certainly look good and are tactile (and comfy in the ear) and give very good sound reproduction.

productTitle: Air 2 Wireless Earbuds, Bluetooth 5.3 Earphones with Hi-Res Sound, LDAC Codec, 10mm Wool Drivers, Multipoint Connection, 4 Mics ENC Clear Calls, IPX7 Waterproof, App Customization, 40H Playtime
reviewText: The Bluetooth connection works seamlessly, which isn't surprising, even my old, cheaper earbuds works well wirelessly. However, the standout feature of these EarFun earbuds is the sound quality. It's completely different from my previous pair, and I'm amazed that such small earbuds can deliver such high quality sound. The audio is crisp, music is much more enjoyable. And phone calls come through clearly without any background noise.I also tested the app, it could be useful, especially the function for finding the earbuds.

productTitle: Air 2 Wireless Earbuds, Bluetooth 5.3 Earphones with Hi-Res Sound, LDAC Codec, 10mm Wool Drivers, Multipoint Connection, 4 Mics ENC Clear Calls, IPX7 Waterproof, App Customization, 40H Playtime
reviewTitle: Beautifully packaged work well but missing features
reviewText: It’s very difficult to be super critical of earbuds that cost £30 so this review is comparing the fears supplied with alternative earbuds of the same price.The packaging is definitely high end and it took a while to get into the box as it’s such a tight fit. But once opened it’s really nicely set out with the earbuds in their case and the accessories in a separate box.The charging cable is extremely short which isn’t a deal breaker but not that practical.The spare rubber grommets cope for all ear types. The instructions are well laid out and clear to follow.You also get a small cotton bud that can be used to clean the electrical charging contacts and wax buildup on the earbuds.From first setup the earbuds are quite difficult to remove from the case with my fat fingers but again that’s not a deal breaker.The contacts have tape that needs to be removed. Then reseat earbuds and they start flashing red and blue so are ready for connecting to your device.Very easy to pair with, pc, iPhone and ipad. Good range from the device once connected and very clean audio with a good range of frequencies.Noise cancellation works well.Overall they are a decent pair of earbuds and are comfortable to wear, great audio and work really well for listening to content like TV programs or films and music.The only real issue I have is the battery life of the case. So many cases now have clear indicators of battery life whether it’s a number of led lights or a 100% charging indicator that reduces with use. All have the same quality of sound as these and roughly 4 hours of play between charging. This just has a red or green LED on the front of the case. So when red it needs to be charged. Doesn’t give a clear indication of how much juice is left so if you were in a rush picked it up and it was red you wouldn’t know how long the earbuds would last.

productTitle: Air 2 Wireless Earbuds, Bluetooth 5.3 Earphones with Hi-Res Sound, LDAC Codec, 10mm Wool Drivers, Multipoint Connection, 4 Mics ENC Clear Calls, IPX7 Waterproof, App Customization, 40H Playtime
reviewText: Excellent earbuds are of impressive quality, they have a very wonderful sound, of a quality as good as good quality headsets. Headphone droplets soundproof you quite well.The volume is quite good, they sound loud enough to hurt beware of those reviews/critiques who say they don't have enough volume, they do.

productTitle: Air 2 Wireless Earbuds, Bluetooth 5.3 Earphones with Hi-Res Sound, LDAC Codec, 10mm Wool Drivers, Multipoint Connection, 4 Mics ENC Clear Calls, IPX7 Waterproof, App Customization, 40H Playtime
reviewText: Great quality earphones. Look and feel really quite premium. Something you’d expect to pick up from ie B or S companies.Case is smoothly finished without any tacky logos that would make it look mickey mouse.Sound is quite good when listening to music and once you download the app, you can customise a lot of the settings to suit your preference.On the topic of the app, it was seamless to get it up and running.Scanned the included QR code, installed, turned earphones on (after pairing which was also a breeze - bluetooth on, take both out and push the button on the bottom of the case for 3s, done!) and the app detected the earphones as soon as I selected the model.No need to register anywhere and create accounts like most places force you to - nice!Once you have the app, you can customise the touch control to your liking for both pieces.There is also an option to play beeping sound in case you misplace them or drop somewhere! Not the loudest to be heard from ie downstairs if left upstairs, but definitely loud enough within the same room if they fell behind a sofa or something.Call quality is very good and noise cancelling outstanding!I stood under extraction hood in the kitchen going full blast and the person on the other end of the call couldn’t hear it!Charged wirelessly without any issues on charging stations I have for Apple products.Good looking, well made, long battery life, decent app, great sound and call quality - and all for under £50 - 5*!

productTitle: Air 2 Wireless Earbuds, Bluetooth 5.3 Earphones with Hi-Res Sound, LDAC Codec, 10mm Wool Drivers, Multipoint Connection, 4 Mics ENC Clear Calls, IPX7 Waterproof, App Customization, 40H Playtime
reviewText: It has pros and cons but overall, I am happy to own it. My first impression is as follows:*** Good Points ***- The built quality is good and feels premium- Great sound clarity- Reasonable bass, so enjoy listening to music on it- Quite loud- Lightweight and easy to carry around- Nice design and comfortable wearing them- Has stable Bluetooth connection- No observable latency in audio call- Arrived well-packaged*** Bad Points ***- In WhatsApp, my partner complained that the volume reduces slightly for her when I use earbuds.- Price is slightly expensive in my opinionHowever, still these are fantastic, and will recommend.

productTitle: Air 2 Wireless Earbuds, Bluetooth 5.3 Earphones with Hi-Res Sound, LDAC Codec, 10mm Wool Drivers, Multipoint Connection, 4 Mics ENC Clear Calls, IPX7 Waterproof, App Customization, 40H Playtime
reviewTitle: I have found my earbuds for life!
reviewText: These earbuds are brilliant.  The sound quality is exceptional and beats anything that I have tried before.  Once fully charged they last a very long time, I haven't timed it, but I rarely have to recharge.  I can listen to my music on the bus going to and from work and escape into my own little world.I have found my earbuds for life!

productTitle: Air 2 Wireless Earbuds, Bluetooth 5.3 Earphones with Hi-Res Sound, LDAC Codec, 10mm Wool Drivers, Multipoint Connection, 4 Mics ENC Clear Calls, IPX7 Waterproof, App Customization, 40H Playtime
reviewText: I'll freely admit I am a bit of a fan of Earfun. I have a couple of sets of their wireless earphones, and in terms of sound, quality and value they are pretty hard to beat. So how do these Air 2's measure up to previous incarnations.The earphones come nicely packaged in a illustrated box with the trademark Earfun yellow. Inside the box you have the charging case with the ear pieces inside, a charging cable, some spare silicone earbuds and some documentation. The case is a fairly typical pebble design that hinges open to reveal the ear pieces inside. Overall the quality is pretty good and feels solid. The ear piece fit nicely into the ear and playback can be controlled via touch controls.Pairing is straightforward. If you a familiar with Bluetooth devices then you will know exactly what to do. Once connected the sound quality is actually really good. With a surprising amount of bass. There is definitely a V shaped EQ but it sounds pleasant and they had no issues when switching genre. Battery life seems decent and is extended by the charging case.Overall another excellent value and quality offering from Earfun.

productTitle: Air 2 Wireless Earbuds, Bluetooth 5.3 Earphones with Hi-Res Sound, LDAC Codec, 10mm Wool Drivers, Multipoint Connection, 4 Mics ENC Clear Calls, IPX7 Waterproof, App Customization, 40H Playtime
reviewTitle: Great sound, strong noise cancelling and works on 2 devices.
reviewText: These earpods fit me very well. They are very comfy to wear with no slippage with the base 'M' buds that are supplied on them. There's also S and L if you did need to size up or down.I wear these on my commute(on the Central Line) as well as in the gym. The sound quality is excellent in both environments, even with such a large amount of background noise. Some of my previous earpods have struggled when commuting due to the noise on the tube, but I have had no issues with these as they block out enough noise to still be able to hear my podcasts.Downloading the Earfun Audio app from the Play store, you are able to adjust the equalizer with the various presets for different music types, as well as create your own. You are also able to change each earpods preset 'tap settings'. e.g 1 tap is volume up, 2 is play/pause, 3 is skip. I have never had this option on any of my headphones previously, which I didn't think I would need, but its so incredibly handy.One of the biggest things for me when looking for new headphones/earpods is the multi device capability. Being able to connect to both my personal phone(Android/Pixel) and my works Iphone, is so handy. Not having to faff about swapping earpods when taking a work or personal call when listening to some music is so key.These are a great pair of earbuds, and I have already bought another pair for my partner who was trying to pinch these from me.

productTitle: Air 2 Wireless Earbuds, Bluetooth 5.3 Earphones with Hi-Res Sound, LDAC Codec, 10mm Wool Drivers, Multipoint Connection, 4 Mics ENC Clear Calls, IPX7 Waterproof, App Customization, 40H Playtime
reviewText: These earbuds are excellent value. I didn't have high expectations originally due to their price, but they surprised me. The sound quality is excellent and all frequencies sound great. They also feel comfortable to wear. The only thing is they don't have noise cancellation but for the price, you really can't go wrong.

productTitle: Air 2 Wireless Earbuds, Bluetooth 5.3 Earphones with Hi-Res Sound, LDAC Codec, 10mm Wool Drivers, Multipoint Connection, 4 Mics ENC Clear Calls, IPX7 Waterproof, App Customization, 40H Playtime
reviewTitle: I can't believe the quality of these £50 Earbuds, Playing 24bit/192Khz from iPhone yet W/out LDAC!
reviewText: Ordered via vine. As per title, these highly exceeded my expectations, and since I have an iPhone (Cannot stream LDAC), I did not even get to test these at these maximum 24bit 990kbps LDAC bluetooth Codec, the leading codec for sound quality by far.Disclaimer: I am reviewing this impartially, and I ONLY mention my existing comparison equipment as an absolute positive example of how great these sounded, far exceeding my expectations. So please bear this in mind, I know reviewing guidelines and i'm reviewing them impartially and honestly.But being an audiophile, I already have some wired Linsoul 7hz Timeless AE Edition (£270) and I use them with a fully balanced Fiio BTR7 ESS DAC (£170) Or sometimes even Via an headphone Preamp, I use them with my £500 Topping E70 Velvet AKM flagship DAC chip playing Tidal Hi-Fi Master FLACs at 24bit 192Khz. Although the iPhones SBC OR AAC sample rate may have only been 41khz, 48khz or 96khz I am not sure.Nonetheless, I mention all of the above only as even considering my iPhone could not stream the superior LDAC codec, these £50 TrueWireless Bluetooth EarBuds, sounded absolutely fantastic in comparison to the significantly more expensive combined value of my preexisting setup. So mods please do not delete this as an unfair review. On the contrary, these are remarkable value.After testing these whilst going for a walk, the ANC was great, but the sound quality was so surprisingly better than I had expected. That immediately when I got home I had to double check the aforementioned high end existing earphone setup I have. As although all audiophiles understand diminishing returns, I don't think I can honestly say these EarFun Bluetooth Earbuds are even 1/3rd as good as existing my higher end my wired earphones & DAC setup. In other words, these entry level Wireless Earphones made a mockery of my High end setup which are between 8x and almost 16x more expensive, depending which DAC I'm referring to.And thats without LDAC... So if I had an android phone that could support LDAC these would have sounded even better much better. No they're not quite as good as my higher end setup, in many ways but blimey, overall in terms of sound quality they are surprisingly not so far off. Surely not all £50 earphones are this good?Anyway, EarFun have done a remarkable job. I once owned some Sony-WF-1000XM5s (also £270), and I specifically bought a Fiio BTA30 Pro. They're discontinued now but @£150 itself, it was and still is the only way to get an iPhone to Stream LDAC, and even then you must use a USB OTG adapter and cable. But ultimately I returned them, primarily as they just didn't go loud enough. But these go loud enough for my general listing, even out of the box they must be easier to drive with the quite basic yet high performing drivers. I feel that they have more headroom than the Sony's did.As for the other features, the App and software has the typical features you'd expect and is intuitive. The battery life is comparable to more expensive midrange products. They're very easy to pair and setup, they look good enough and are comfortable for the price, they magnetically charge just like higher end products.Also, since i'm being 100% transparent. My first box came without any earphone's or case at all, only the yellow booklet and with no break in the sealed box. I contacted earfun, they were not obliged to replace them and I also notified Amazon vine who were copied in. The following day I was asked a few questions, and I provided a few photos. Then I received a response with an apology, that the quality control was being investigated and that a replacement set had been shipped, and they arrived the following day.Considering there was admittedly no signs of damage on both inner and outer box, and I vouched for the courier by clearly and honestly stating the plastic seal was unbroken, meaning it impossible these had been stolen in transit. EarFun only had my word on this, I could have been lying to get a 2nd set of free earphones. And to be honest I even mentioned that I would still be declaring the original missing item and my full experience, even if they were replaced. The lady at Earfun replaced the full product after just 3 days anyway, and I feel that Earfun have demonstrated that they go above and beyond to honour their warranty.For all the true and accurate reasons above, I feel these deserve 5/5 stars.

productTitle: Air 2 Wireless Earbuds, Bluetooth 5.3 Earphones with Hi-Res Sound, LDAC Codec, 10mm Wool Drivers, Multipoint Connection, 4 Mics ENC Clear Calls, IPX7 Waterproof, App Customization, 40H Playtime
reviewText: They are well made buds with plenty of tips to get the right fit. The small ones in the B bag worked best for me. Took me a few goes to get the fit right but thats usually the same with any earbuds. I dont think they wouldPairing is really easy, and once paired they connect really really fast. Open the box and they are paired bfore they get to my ear. I have used these together and separately and each way works perfectly fine. I have had some buds that can have connection issues if you use the one side only and then both.The touch button on the buds is very easy to use and find, and all the functions have worked fine so far.Battery life has been solid for me, easily lasting a day working and putting them in the case during breaks. Like i said before they connect so easily and quickly that it is easy to put away and take back out.1 item i am not so keen on, is I have noticed some lag every time i watch a you tube clip on the computer. I dont know if there is a way to fix this.

productTitle: Air 2 Wireless Earbuds, Bluetooth 5.3 Earphones with Hi-Res Sound, LDAC Codec, 10mm Wool Drivers, Multipoint Connection, 4 Mics ENC Clear Calls, IPX7 Waterproof, App Customization, 40H Playtime
reviewText: A well presented pack, containing as it does the buds in the charging case, a small USB-C cable, instructions and a selection of silicone fit items to get the best experience from you buds.I very much like that the buds themselves came almost security wrapped, that tells me they are clean, that matters a lot to me and is an important reassurance. The case and buds themselves are well made, the silicone inserts are soft and made nicely available from a holding card. I very much like that the bud in the charger can be wireless charged, I popped them on my bedside charger prior to sleep and they were good to go when I woke.I started by pairing with BT5.3 via my PC and they dropped in flawlessly, same with my phone and an MP3 player I use occasionally, so no problem with connectivity at all.I am not an audiophile but I can tell if something sounds "right" or good to me, I quite often pick up on headphones or buds that have too much high end, these are exceptionally good, rich in bass from the off and the great thing about the app is being able to tailor the experience to your own preference. I particularly like that you can send a "beep beep beep" sound to the buds if you misplace them within earshot.Not only can you customise the sound through the app abut also the controls, which I found responsive, even though they are of the "tap to..." type, something in the past I have found disappointing in other ear buds, these are excellent in this regard. While on the subject of the app, you can access it without having to register (I like that) and the number of options available is impressive when you have the app up and running.All in all, high sound quality, well made, responsive controls, very customisable via the app, flawless connectivity, well thought out design and app. Good item.

productTitle: Air 2 Wireless Earbuds, Bluetooth 5.3 Earphones with Hi-Res Sound, LDAC Codec, 10mm Wool Drivers, Multipoint Connection, 4 Mics ENC Clear Calls, IPX7 Waterproof, App Customization, 40H Playtime
reviewTitle: Easy to use and great sound
reviewText: So many earbuds are a let down in the volume department, but not these.  I like my music loud, and these definitely go all the way up to 10, which was a pleasant surprise.  They fit well, and are easy to connect.  I am not the least bit sure about the app, which seems largely pointless.  Yes, it can switch sound between Rock and Pop (and that’s it for the in-built choices), but any halfway decent phone can already do that, and the only other thing it can do is ‘gaming’.  I shall admit to having been unaware that anything special needed to be done if you were gaming with earbuds, but that’s not my scene (not on the phone, anyway).  Music was faithfully reproduced and voices clear, and the buds switched off when closed away in their charging case (unlike another pair of buds in my possession).  I would give these 4.6, so round up to 5.

productTitle: Air 2 Wireless Earbuds, Bluetooth 5.3 Earphones with Hi-Res Sound, LDAC Codec, 10mm Wool Drivers, Multipoint Connection, 4 Mics ENC Clear Calls, IPX7 Waterproof, App Customization, 40H Playtime
reviewText: Very good earphones. I've had this brand before and they are very good. Excellent quality. Noise cancelling. Brilliant!

productTitle: Air 2 Wireless Earbuds, Bluetooth 5.3 Earphones with Hi-Res Sound, LDAC Codec, 10mm Wool Drivers, Multipoint Connection, 4 Mics ENC Clear Calls, IPX7 Waterproof, App Customization, 40H Playtime
reviewText: Swapping out my usual earbuds for these was enlightening in terms of audio quality. The sound detail is noticeably superior, especially for someone with a keen ear for music nuances. Having the ability to connect to two devices seamlessly has been beneficial during my workday, juggling calls and music without missing a beat. The comfort level is exceptional; I often forget I'm wearing them throughout the day. However, the battery life, while adequate, did require midday charging during heavy use, which was a slight inconvenience.

productTitle: Air 2 Wireless Earbuds, Bluetooth 5.3 Earphones with Hi-Res Sound, LDAC Codec, 10mm Wool Drivers, Multipoint Connection, 4 Mics ENC Clear Calls, IPX7 Waterproof, App Customization, 40H Playtime
reviewText: These are excellent, well-made premium earbuds. Finish is high quality, with decent soft touch materials, and an equally good charge case.  A really good feature is that they can be wirelessly charged direct from the case (the case also is wireless charge compatible using a standard wireless charge pad, or via the included USB C charge cable). These are above average, and generally up there with the better earbuds I have tried, not quite as good though as the Jabra Elite buds I have. They are though definitely comparable with airpods.As you would expect, the earbuds come complete with additional (various size) silicone ear tips and USB C charging cable. The earbuds do look, and feel premium quality, and the case equally so. The earbuds are retained (magnetically) securely within the case.Setting up was a simple and easy process, straightforward, and hassle-free. They BT (5.3) registered / connected straight away, and the app was a simple download, allowing equalisation (and lots more)  setting to suit your preferences. Take a little time to optimally set, and sound quality is superb, with rich, full (above average) bass, crystal clear highs and mids, giving an overall very good, wide frequency range.These earbuds are comfortable. They don’t feel quite as secure as my Jabra buds, but to be fair they do stay secure in the ears, which is good. It isn’t easy to find earbuds that stay where they should, and don’t feel like they’re going to drop out, but these do stay put pretty well. Full bass is experienced without the need to push right in and holding them, which is often the case, and why I generally prefer over-ear type.I’ve tried tried with Android (mine) with no issues, but these are for my Grandon, and they work perfectly with his IPhone 13. As noted, the audio app is a brilliant addition, allowing you to change various settings easily. AN etc. The features and functions are excellent, especially ANC and the mic set-up.Sound is excellent as noted, with more than adequate volume, plenty of customisation settings, and a pretty decent 9 hours (which drops to 5.5h using LDAC) play time. A fully charged case will provide up to 40 hours (23h with LDAC) play time, which isn’t bad.Overall, these are really good quality earbuds. As noted, not as good as my Jabra ones, but they were a lot more expensive. These are good value, and a really good product at this price point. They have a broad range of features and functions, and excellent all-round quality. Definitely recommended.I hope that you have found this helpful.

productTitle: Air 2 Wireless Earbuds, Bluetooth 5.3 Earphones with Hi-Res Sound, LDAC Codec, 10mm Wool Drivers, Multipoint Connection, 4 Mics ENC Clear Calls, IPX7 Waterproof, App Customization, 40H Playtime
reviewText: These wireless earbuds are simply ideal for music lovers! Equipped with Bluetooth 5.3 technology for high-resolution sound and supporting LDAC codec, the sound quality is clear and pure. 10mm wool drivers create deep bass and clear treble, giving every song a more layered feel. The multi-point connection function is very convenient, and the ENC technology of 4 microphones ensures clear and interference-free calls.The waterproof level reaches IPX7, so you are not afraid of rain and sweat. App customization allows you to personalize settings to meet your individual needs. What’s amazing is that the playback time is up to 40 hours, ensuring you can enjoy music while traveling or using it for long periods of time. Overall, this is a powerful and full-featured wireless earbuds that is definitely worth the money.

productTitle: Air 2 Wireless Earbuds, Bluetooth 5.3 Earphones with Hi-Res Sound, LDAC Codec, 10mm Wool Drivers, Multipoint Connection, 4 Mics ENC Clear Calls, IPX7 Waterproof, App Customization, 40H Playtime
reviewText: When I opened these I was expecting another pair of budget wireless earbuds, but happily I was very mistaken! The case is well built, with a convenient slim size and rounded edges. The hinge is very solid and closes with a nice snap (this is where a lot of lower quality models fail). The buds sit in nice moulded cavities and stay in place even when inverted and shaken around!Pairing with my phone was effortless. In terms of comfort they are excellent, sitting firmly in my ears with little adjustment needed. The default tips fit me fine, but there are 4 other sizes included if you need them. I wore them for a good 4 hours straight without any noticeable discomfort.In terms of battery life I haven’t been able to run them dead, so can’t give figures, but I would definitely say they are equal to or better than the competition.On to audio quality. These use the LDAC codec for high fidelity streaming, a feature missing from many lower end headphones, and it definitely shows!In Rachid Taha’s Barra Barra the percussion wraps all around with a clear soundstage, I’m able to pinpoint the individual instruments clearly. The electric guitar is warm and central, coming straight down the middle like a freight train, and the vocal sits clearly on top. I was even able to pick out some atmospheric synth elements that I’ve never noticed before. When the drums come in later in the song the kick is tight and powerful, underpinning the track beautifully.In Armand Van Helden’s I Want Your Soul, the kick is once again powerful without being overwhelming. The hats skip around with plenty of space to hear the detail of the reverb. The main bass synth fills out the low end without overpowering the drums. When the chorus breaks things do get a little muddy, the vocal is clear, but there is so much going on in the lower mids that I don’t think the drivers can quite maintain separation.Honestly I was blown away by the performance, I did not expect quality like this at the price point. Are they reference headphones? No (but honestly would do the job in a pinch). Are they fun? Absolutely. Anyone would be happy with these as daily drivers.My only real issue (and a minor one at that) is that these things block pretty much all external sound and there is no passthrough, so for me are more suitable for home and office listening that walking on busy streets. I like to be able to hear my surroundings while out and about. To be clear, this is the nature of the beast and not a criticism, but something to be aware of.In short, these are excellent. Highly recommended.

productTitle: Air 2 Wireless Earbuds, Bluetooth 5.3 Earphones with Hi-Res Sound, LDAC Codec, 10mm Wool Drivers, Multipoint Connection, 4 Mics ENC Clear Calls, IPX7 Waterproof, App Customization, 40H Playtime
reviewText: These wireless earbuds are really well made and contain the  latest tech in regard to delivering superb range of sound .The  high-res functionality belies the inexpensive pricetag that these come with.Features like this are usually found in branded buds which come of course, at a much higher price!These connected effortlessly to my smartphone and produced a rich, vibrant sound that  gave my old branded buds a run for their money .All in all I was highly impressed with these particularly considering the price and would easily give these 5 stars.

productTitle: Air 2 Wireless Earbuds, Bluetooth 5.3 Earphones with Hi-Res Sound, LDAC Codec, 10mm Wool Drivers, Multipoint Connection, 4 Mics ENC Clear Calls, IPX7 Waterproof, App Customization, 40H Playtime
reviewText: I was very impressed by these neat little earbuds. I cannot tell you how they compare with the Apple ones, but they are definitely better than some similarly priced ones I ordered a while back that were disappointing on every level.In stark contrast these have a simple design and fit easily in the ear. In my case I removed the rubber ear pads it came with and fitted the smallest size alternatives instead, which fit comfortably and securely.Connecting to Bluetooth is dead easy. After charging, remove the stickers and place the earbuds back in the box. They automatically started pairing and it was all sorted in 5 seconds.Down to the key point though, the sound quality is clear with excellent tone and easy to listen for extended periods. I often listen to podcasts or Youtube videos while I work, but struggle to focus on one or the other, whereas these let me listen without distraction, while still being able to work.The battery is long lasting, they look good, fit well and the sound quality is very respectable. If I wonder out if distance for Bluetooth they disconnect, but then reconnect automatically when I enter range. In summary, they do everything right.

productTitle: Air 2 Wireless Earbuds, Bluetooth 5.3 Earphones with Hi-Res Sound, LDAC Codec, 10mm Wool Drivers, Multipoint Connection, 4 Mics ENC Clear Calls, IPX7 Waterproof, App Customization, 40H Playtime
reviewText: Price at review: £49.99So these in-ear bluetooth earbuds are just great! I don't even know if my android phone is sophisticated enough to support the fancy hi-res codec they support but the sound quality is amazing. I listened to a variety of music and the rendition was exactly how I like it on all of it. Plenty of bass and they can kick out some serious volume at maximum without distortingI used them for some calls and the callers could hear me no problem and I could hear them. Everything from pairing to adjusting the volume by tapping the headphones just worked flawlessly.They look great and stuck well in my ears without any threat of falling out. They were a bit like wearing earplugs when music wasn't playing but I think that just shows what a tight seal they make in the ear.The charger case is slimmer than the previous iteration of this product and fits easier in to my pocket.The only negative I could say is that at the current price this is more than I would like to pay for earbuds, as I always worry I'll lose them or break them and regret it later. These are really good though so maybe the price is justified.

productTitle: Air 2 Wireless Earbuds, Bluetooth 5.3 Earphones with Hi-Res Sound, LDAC Codec, 10mm Wool Drivers, Multipoint Connection, 4 Mics ENC Clear Calls, IPX7 Waterproof, App Customization, 40H Playtime
reviewText: Everything is relative when it comes to hi-fi and audio equipment, and when reviewing it, you really need to compare and rate products within discreet price bands.  The band that these buds fall into is budget, and as budget buds go, they perform very well indeed, and can be recommended.POSITIVES*  Hefty bass response (these go LOW)*  App with 10 channel EQ (frequency equalisation control)*  Reasonable mid and top end clarity, adjustable via EQ*  Comfortable in-ear fit*  Selection of soft rubber tips for correct sizing*  Amazing battery life - I charge mine once a week!*  Nice charging case (USB or wireless chargeable)*  App activated location beep for finding lost buds (great feature!)*  Excellent microphone clarity during phone calls*  Low latencyNEGATIVES*  Some might find these bass-heavy*  Slightly recessed mids*  Sound stage is good, but sometimes feels slightly confinedCONCLUSIONThe slight limitations in the audio can be addressed to a reasonable extent with a bit of EQ tweaking, to suit the genre of the moment.  Having done that, the long list of positives far outweighs any negative issues, and really, for budget buds at this price, the sound they produce is remarkable.  If you're on a budget, and seeking a decent set of buds that give you an engaging and immersive experience, and can also cope well with phone calls, these are well worth considering, in my opinion.

productTitle: Air 2 Wireless Earbuds, Bluetooth 5.3 Earphones with Hi-Res Sound, LDAC Codec, 10mm Wool Drivers, Multipoint Connection, 4 Mics ENC Clear Calls, IPX7 Waterproof, App Customization, 40H Playtime
reviewTitle: A good quality earbud comfortable and sound good
reviewText: This set of earbuds come in a small well presented boxIncluded is the ....Case, ear buds, X8 various sized ear tips, a cleaning tool, short usb charger lead, instructionsThey come with a free app and they have a high res sound certification and ldacAt this price point that's impressive the ear buds are well made the charging case is a mat black finish and made from a tough plasticThe ear buds are glossy finish with touch features volume up down play pause etcThere  not 100% accurate I would say they work like 8 or 9 times out of 10 most are like this very rarly perfect all thetimeThese also sound good in this price range the audio quality is great during calls and music is well balancedThere is some different sized ear tips included for size diferences and comfortThere well made overall connect with no issues and the little led indicators tell you when they need charging and when they are full green light on for full red for charge blue for connectivity on the ear buds pretty good overall and the high res is a massive plus at this price

productTitle: Air 2 Wireless Earbuds, Bluetooth 5.3 Earphones with Hi-Res Sound, LDAC Codec, 10mm Wool Drivers, Multipoint Connection, 4 Mics ENC Clear Calls, IPX7 Waterproof, App Customization, 40H Playtime
reviewTitle: This ear buds are really good!
reviewText: This ear buds are really good! I have paired with my android(Samsung) handset and with my laptop (HP) and on both I could enjoy the sound quality either way listening music and during calls (and meetings). So far the battery is really good and can grants 5 to 7 days of run time (depending on the usage). Also they really confortable and aesthetically beautiful.

productTitle: Air 2 Wireless Earbuds, Bluetooth 5.3 Earphones with Hi-Res Sound, LDAC Codec, 10mm Wool Drivers, Multipoint Connection, 4 Mics ENC Clear Calls, IPX7 Waterproof, App Customization, 40H Playtime
reviewTitle: Bass overload needs EQ to sort it out
reviewText: These are my 4th pair of Earfun buds, and unfortunately my least favourite.I'm a huge Earfun fan and with a bit of EQ their products can sound amazing. I genuinely sing thier praise to anyone who asks for Bluetooth earbuds recommendations. They have some of the best products for the price and beat the competition easily.I own the EarFun Free Pro 2 and 3, and the EarFun Free 2S, and they all sound great. The Free Pro 3 are my daily buds and they beat every other bluetooth pair I own, and I have a lot!OK, these are cheaper that the Free Pros, but about the same price as the free 2s, and I 100% prefer those over these.Let's get the problem out of the way..BASS. How much bass do you really need, enough to make you tap you feet and put a huge smile on your face, or enough to make you fear for the safety of your eardrums? These are the latter, and even though it's impressive, it's far far too much... They go low for sure, but on some tracks the sub bass just bloats and distorts. It's such a shame.I dropped the first 2 bands in the 10 band EQ down a few notches and it improves a lot. I have a test playlist that I use to test earbuds IEMs and headphones, and one of those tracks is Habanero by Matt Hawk, it's my bass test track, and on decent pairs you get a deep but controlled thump in the bass, but with these it's just a mess, the bass extension is just far too overloaded out of the box.When EQd it's better, but you don't seem to be able to completely remove the sub bass, and so it still distorts on some tracks.If BASS is your thing, and not the controlled type, then you will like these, but if you like a balanced sound with a bit of bass extension then I would suggest the Earfun Free Pro 3.EarFun really should put every bud they produce in the hands of OLUV, and let him tune them like he did with the Free pro 2, and to a lesser degree the Free pro 3. (they even have an EQ setting by him in the latter)Maybe there is someone at the top in EarFun who just doesn't get it, and when the engineers offer thier new product with a beautifully balanced tuning, they just shout "MORE BASS" at them.. Its what the kids want right.? I feel for those engineers.Ok the good stuff.Mids are nice and controlled when EQ is applied, but initially they are a bit recessed. Guitars and synths come alive and voices are clear and vibrant.Highs are tunable and sound good when EQd to your taste, they lack a tiny bit of sparkle, but they are pleasing enough when tuned.Fit. Very good, they just slip in and stay put. These would be good for sport, especially with the IP ratingCase, small and slim, but not as good as the free pro 2 and 3, but you do get wireless charging.Overall a disappointing experience compared to everything else I've tried from EarFun.I've given them 4 stars, as everything is great except the stupid bass.I genuinely look forward to reviewing EarFun stuff, and hopefully the next set  are true to the others I've heard.

productTitle: Air 2 Wireless Earbuds, Bluetooth 5.3 Earphones with Hi-Res Sound, LDAC Codec, 10mm Wool Drivers, Multipoint Connection, 4 Mics ENC Clear Calls, IPX7 Waterproof, App Customization, 40H Playtime
reviewText: I wanted an affordable but good quality set of in-ear Bluetooth earbuds and as these were available as a free sample via the Amazon Vine scheme I decided to give them a try.They’re supplied with a very solid charging and storage case and a USB charging cable. However the cable is only 24cm long which is a bit short unless you’re charging them from a laptop or battery pack.Pairing them with my phone was very straightforward.The sound quality to my albeit ageing ears is clear and seems to have an excellent frequency rangeThe buds arrived with one set of rubber covers and three more sets in the box with different sizes to suit different ear shapes.There are no buttons on the earbuds but you can answer end and reject calls, adjust the volume and move backwards and forwards on your track list by various combinations of finger taps on the earbud stems.The only minor issues are that the instruction manual has very small text which needs a magnifying glass to read and the two halves of the box fit together very tightly and I had to insert the end of a ruler to lever them apart.

productTitle: Air 2 Wireless Earbuds, Bluetooth 5.3 Earphones with Hi-Res Sound, LDAC Codec, 10mm Wool Drivers, Multipoint Connection, 4 Mics ENC Clear Calls, IPX7 Waterproof, App Customization, 40H Playtime
reviewText: These are nicely presented, and the manual is properly produced - although in the usual very small format - and the instructions clear.I paired the buds to my computer, they paired very quickly and easily, so that I could do some proper sound comparisons between high quality studio style headphones and the ear buds. I played music tracks that I am extremely familiar with having produced them myself, so I would hear if anything was adversely affected by the bud characteristics.The bass frequency reproduction is quite astonishing, and if I'd been listening to music over loudspeakers instead of ear buds, I'd say that the bass would have been felt in my stomach. It's a very clean bass though, no distortion at-all, and I'd say great for modern genres of music (but good for all music as the sound is of good quality). A slight word of caution... make sure you start at a low volume setting because these buds can go loud! After pairing with the PC they defaulted to 50%, and that was getting near to my top comfortable listening volume. My relaxed listening level is about 30-40%The mid frequency range of the buds was very accurate and clean and represented the original track extremely well. Absolutely no distortion or quirks discernible. The highest frequencies did tail off by only a very small amount, and when I paid attention to vocal sibilance, the sizzle of ss's that one hears in some words, I noticed that it was fractionally reduced, by a very small amount.The bottom line regarding the frequency response is that there is a slight bass boost and a very slight lessening of the highest frequencies, but not destructively, they are still there, just slightly lower in the mix. The overall frequency response of the buds enables them to produce an excellent and well-defined sound that is very pleasant to listen to.The buds fitted my ears well and I found them comfortable. They certainly fitted well enough to isolate me almost completely from external sounds!I lined up some tracks on my PC and wandered around the house and immediate garden. I didn't hear any hint of a disconnect at-all, so I can use these to listen to the radio and music throughout the house, taking just the ear buds with me when on the treadmill etc.As I'd paired them with the PC, I was also able to use a sound recorder app to record what the ear bud microphones picked up when I spoke. The audio quality is actually very good. Perhaps not studio microphone quality, I wouldn't expect that, but excellent overall sound, very suited for Skype/Zoom/Game and mobile phone use. One's voice sounds fairly natural.I'm very pleased with everything about these ear buds. They sound excellent, are comfortable and well fitting, they have good external noise isolation, and Bluetooth pairing was quickly done and reliable... What's not to like?! A well-deserved 5 stars.

productTitle: Air 2 Wireless Earbuds, Bluetooth 5.3 Earphones with Hi-Res Sound, LDAC Codec, 10mm Wool Drivers, Multipoint Connection, 4 Mics ENC Clear Calls, IPX7 Waterproof, App Customization, 40H Playtime
reviewText: I'm impressed by the multipoint connection feature, which allows me to flawlessly switch between devices without any hassle. Whether I'm watching a movie on my tablet or taking a call on my phone, the Air 2 earbuds connect instantly and effortlessly. The four-mic ENC technology effectively filters out background noise. The IPX7 water resistance rating makes the Air 2 perfect for active lifestyles and worry-free listening in any weather. I also appreciate the app customisation options, which allow me to personalise the sound profile and controls to suit my preferences.

productTitle: Air 2 Wireless Earbuds, Bluetooth 5.3 Earphones with Hi-Res Sound, LDAC Codec, 10mm Wool Drivers, Multipoint Connection, 4 Mics ENC Clear Calls, IPX7 Waterproof, App Customization, 40H Playtime
reviewText: Perfect in ear buds. Thrb40hour playback means I can use this for days without thinking of charging. productTitle: Air 2 Wireless Earbuds, Bluetooth 5.3 Earphones with Hi-Res Sound, LDAC Codec, 10mm Wool Drivers, Multipoint Connection, 4 Mics ENC Clear Calls, IPX7 Waterproof, App Customization, 40H Playtime
reviewText: At the time of this review these, with discount code, were £40. For this I think you get some great buds which are comfortable, easy to connect and sound great. productTitle: Air 2 Wireless Earbuds, Bluetooth 5.3 Earphones with Hi-Res Sound, LDAC Codec, 10mm Wool Drivers, Multipoint Connection, 4 Mics ENC Clear Calls, IPX7 Waterproof, App Customization, 40H Playtime
reviewTitle: Full and rich with clear treble
reviewText: I’m delighted with these. Despite my age related hearing loss I could appreciate the full range of sound available, full and rich at the bass end and crystal clear treble at the top. Delighted by the overall quality, easy of pairing and ability to adjust settings. Highly recommended

productTitle: Air 2 Wireless Earbuds, Bluetooth 5.3 Earphones with Hi-Res Sound, LDAC Codec, 10mm Wool Drivers, Multipoint Connection, 4 Mics ENC Clear Calls, IPX7 Waterproof, App Customization, 40H Playtime
reviewText: Very comfortable and really good sound quality.

productTitle: Wireless Earbuds Air 2, Bluetooth 5.3 Earphones with Hi-Res Sound, LDAC, 10mm Wool Drivers, Multi-Connection, 4 Mics ENC Clear Calls, IPX7 Waterproof, App Customization, 40H Playtime, White
reviewText: I have to be honest, I was pleasantly surprised by these earphones. The sound quality from them is brilliant. I consider myself an audiophile and am quite fussy over how I like my music to sound and these really didn't disappoint. I listen to all sorts of music from pop, easy listening through to punk and prog rock and these didn't struggle with any genre.These are louder than most other earphones I've purchased over the years which I think is a good thing as most are too quiet. You can tweak the sound however you like using the EQ settings within the app. The bass is very deep and the treble quite bright but not overly so on default settings but that is how I like my music to sound so I was quite happy. The detail portrayed is very good and I didn't struggle to hear little nuances in the music that I have struggles with on full size headphones costing 3 times more.The pebble shaped case is quite small which is a good thing for those that like to keep it in their pocket. It has a charging point on the base along with a bluetooth sync button.The phones slotted into my ears very well with the set of silicon pads already fitted but they come with an additional 3 size sets which should fit most ear types. These things are comfortable, I didn't have to push or twist to get them to fit snugly, they just fell straight into place in my ears and once in weren't uncomfortable at all and didn't work loose.Battery life is very good and in general use I've only had to charge the case once in a week with the earphones being charged by the case once put away.Earfun even include a cleaning bud for keeping the connections clean and you get a charging cable included along with full instructions.The app is very good and allows you to adjust the tap controls for each earphone separately. You can even use the app to send a beeping sound to help find your earphones. You can update firmware, control duel device settings, adjust which microphone to use for calls and even adjust the voice prompt volume. It also shows the battery life of both the case and earphones separately which is very handy.Overall I'm very happy with these and think they are a bit of a bargain. This coupled with the fact that Earfun customer service is brilliant means you can buy these with confidence and if any issues arise, they will strive to put it right.5+ stars.

productTitle: Wireless Earbuds Air 2, Bluetooth 5.3 Earphones with Hi-Res Sound, LDAC, 10mm Wool Drivers, Multi-Connection, 4 Mics ENC Clear Calls, IPX7 Waterproof, App Customization, 40H Playtime, White
reviewText: Well all I can say is that I am super impressed with these earbuds especially for the cost.They have a beautiful deep range of sound that really allows you to feel the music.The buds sit very nicely in ear and create an excellent natural noise cancellation and I find the sound clear and uninterrupted except when I'm out walking with heavy traffic going passed.The battery life is good enough for most use, I listen to them all day in work and although the buds do run out, the case offers several charges and charged them really quickly as well.They aren't going to match your top of the range buds but for a fraction on the cost I would say the offer at least 90% of the quality.The Earfun app allows you to play around with the equalizer and other features so you can fine tune the buds to your taste.The only minor complaint I have is the touch controls which can be a bit hit or miss so I often end up manually changing songs. Not a deal breaker for me given all the other plus points at this price range.I have buds that cost far more that are now redundant as I want good quality sound and these do the business.All in all I would highly recommend unless your caught up in wanting all the features you get with buds that cost 7 or 8 times as much.

productTitle: Wireless Earbuds Air 2, Bluetooth 5.3 Earphones with Hi-Res Sound, LDAC, 10mm Wool Drivers, Multi-Connection, 4 Mics ENC Clear Calls, IPX7 Waterproof, App Customization, 40H Playtime, White

productTitle: Wireless Earbuds Air 2, Bluetooth 5.3 Earphones with Hi-Res Sound, LDAC, 10mm Wool Drivers, Multi-Connection, 4 Mics ENC Clear Calls, IPX7 Waterproof, App Customization, 40H Playtime, White
reviewText: As an avid fan of EarFun products I was looking to test these against both my Airpods 2’s, Airpods’s Pro, EarFun Air Pro 3’s and EarFun Air S’s (which I do hold in regard as my favourite earphones).Being honest, I did not hold much hope of these fending of the later. However, they are fantastic and I have to say, my favourite pair to date.I always felt something was lacking when comparing the EarFun Pro 3’s to my EarFun Air S’s. They were a quality pair of earphones but the Air’s always managed to steal the show and had me going back to them.The EarFun Air 2’s are better than them all. If you like the ability to adjust your equaliser settings and make your earphones louder, you won’t get better than this pair. They come packaged with extra ear tips, charging cable, manual and cleaning tools, as well as an 18 month warranty if you register them with the company.As always, I can say that the warranty definitely holds up. When a sudden fault occurred in my Air S’s, the company had a replacement set to me within 3 days. I can’t say the same for other companies on Amazon when things have developed a fault.If you are looking for a good pair of earphones with excellent sound quality, customisable app options and a long warranty look no further.Can’t wait for the next release.
