productTitle: Apple AirTag. Track your keys, wallet, luggage, backpack. Replaceable battery. Water-resistant. One-tap set up with iPhone or iPad.
reviewText: Apple AirTag -  good buy - recommendedI think this Apple device wasn’t designed to be a tracker, but it does give very accurate positioning of where your attached tag is to within a few feet/metres, which is what it”s designed for. No fee for finding where your tag is. Just need the ‘Find My’ app.If you want to trace the routes that a car or other moving item goes to and the times that they were at certain places, then a dedicated tracker is much better. Some have monthly fees to pay, others do not (just the initial payment and a SIM card - which is very cheap to run).The Apple AirTag is small and is easily attached/inserted to/in objects. There are many Keyring cases to choose from.PS for android phone uses:You can register an AirTag on your iPad, but tracking it with an Android phone is limited. Apple's Tracker Detect app for Android helps find nearby AirTags, and NFC capability on some phones can read AirTag info. However, precise tracking like on Apple devices isn't possible. For tracking your Android phone, consider dedicated Android tracking solutions.

productTitle: Apple AirTag. Track your keys, wallet, luggage, backpack. Replaceable battery. Water-resistant. One-tap set up with iPhone or iPad.
reviewText: Ever since I slipped an Apple AirTag into my bag, it's like I've been gifted a personal peace of mind. Gone are the days of frantic searches for my keys as I'm rushing out the door or the sinking feeling when I can't locate my backpack in a crowded café. The AirTag is more than just a sleek, coin-sized tracker; it's my little beacon of hope, ensuring that what's important to me stays within reach.Setting it up was a breeze—just a few taps on my iPhone and it was like my belongings were suddenly part of my digital ecosystem. The precision finding feature is a game-changer, leading me straight to my lost items with an ease and accuracy that feels like magic. But what truly amazes me is how seamlessly it integrates into my life. Whether it's slipping it into a suitcase for travel or attaching it to my dog's collar during walks, the AirTag has adapted to my needs in ways I didn't anticipate.The real testament to its impact came when I left my wallet in a taxi. Panic turned to relief as I tracked its journey across town, retrieving it with a grateful heart. It's in these moments that the AirTag proves itself to be not just a gadget, but a tiny guardian angel for my belongings.For anyone who's ever lost something important, the Apple AirTag is more than an accessory; it's an essential. It's given me the confidence to go about my day, knowing I have a little helper keeping an eye on the things that matter most.

productTitle: Apple AirTag. Track your keys, wallet, luggage, backpack. Replaceable battery. Water-resistant. One-tap set up with iPhone or iPad.
reviewTitle: Can't fault it, Apple know what they are doing.
reviewText: Second of these purchased to date, they just work.I have an older iPhone which can only locate tags using the basic bluetooth functionality - so cannot test ultra wideband navigation by arrows for example but, what these tags do - they do well (as you'd expect from Apple).Setup is a breeze, pull out the battery saver clear tab and hold it next to your iPhone.  Your iPhone should detect the tag and offer the necessary setup steps to configure.. done, thats it.I have but two criticisms:1). When using the lost mode (which effectively tracks the device via other iPhones when in BT range).  The tag will likely produce a subtle beep - as it has a tiny in-built speaker (which kind defeats the idea using to protect a something from being stolen) as the thief will simply locate (when notified it has one) and then remove.2). I also understand that since the anti-stalking measures were introduced by Apple - these tags will also notify any iPhone user (within Bluetooth range) of the presence of an Airtag is nearby - again, the thief will simply remove if discovered.The challenge therefore is to conceal your tag on your precious item and ensure you track its location quickly - before a thief realises it has one.  You also have to hope they have an older (non Android v6.0. or better phone) which cannot detect their presence which will buy you more time to track.I hope Apple will provide something in the future to allow this required functionality but, these tags are good otherwise.

reviewTitle: Pretty good but required proximity to an Apple device is limiting.
reviewText: I bought one of these after we has searched for lost keys for ages (twice!) which later, fortunately turned out to be hidden in the house. In this context the tag is excellent as I can always interrogate the tag with my phone or even make it beep so those keys tucked away at the bottom of a bag somewhere are now easy to locate.  However in the wider world the requirement of having to be in surprisingly close proximity to an Apple device to send its location makes the process of finding a lost item 'somewhere out there' pretty hit and miss. Yes I know there are millions of Apple devices out there but in the real world I have found this all to be a bit hit and miss,  if for instance you lost your keys (or whatever) in a field whilst out walking.

productTitle: Apple AirPods (3rd generation) with MagSafe Charging Case (2021)
reviewTitle: Fit well, easy to pose music !
reviewText: I have been using my AirPod 2 over 8 years and it started stop working on one ear. The battery life gets much shorter as 1h30 mins instead of 5 hours. They still work but the time I can use gets shorter and shorter month by month giving me a bit of sweat 😰 to anticipate the end . I started feeling that it is the time to upgrade my AirPod . They did work so long perfectly every day for me . I had hesitation between pro and AirPod 3 . But my silicone lemon case got moulds up and I had been unable to remove them by any antibacterial products. They entered deep inside of silicone material. So I had a great doubt about the silicone ear pods. I had decided for the AirPod 3 without them . The stem is shorter but fits my small ear canal so well . I shake my head and jump to test but they don’t drop from my ear . Great for sports.Shame that I can’t share my test video. Also AirPod3 gives you a stem control and without taking them off like v2 , you can pose the music in a shop to talk to people. I don’t want to be rude nor antisocial so this is much appreciated upgrade . Only the issue is the volume control . You got to ask Siri to increase volume or decrease it . Siri does it but only for one command per one press of your stem . Doesn’t accept my second request : a bit louder please. You got to start again to call Siri . In public place, I do look wierdo possibly. Pro has this stem control for volume . But I insist that I don’t like silicone for hygiene reasons . Overall I am very happy with AirPod 3 . Very comfortable to wear them . The sound quality gets slightly better after 6 uses but you can adjust them in system settings . I prefer not to follow the test as ended in the wrong settings . Try selecting one with you favourite music and compare with other 2 . I prefer balanced tone and make the soft sound come forward strongly . Often Apple products automatically upgrades the quality while we are using so I guess the sound is not that wow on the first music after your set up . Spatial Audio needs to be set up showing your ears. You do feel differences from normal audio settings. Not overly dramatic but good to use it . AirPod 3 is great with the stem control . I no longer need to tap my ear and I can pose music without taking them out  from my ears and hurry to find a case in my bag or pockets not to loose them . Wonderful. I don’t walk without AirPod over 8 years. It is indeed my encouragement for walking. With AirPod I now walk longer and do sports much more. Life gets so much richer with world musics . When I was young it was so hard to get foreign music. Crossing borders was so hard . Compared with the record recorded my first concert at the age4 , we are living in extraordinary time with full choices and stimulation for our creativity. I do recommend AirPod 3 . Affordable with credit without interest in Amazon and simply I cannot live without it ! Thank you for delivering this to me.

productTitle: Apple AirPods (3rd generation) with MagSafe Charging Case (2021)
reviewText: I had my old Apple AirPods Gen 1 for 7 years and was very happy with them. Unfortunately, they recently stopped working, so I decided it was time for an upgrade. I was torn between the Gen 2 and Gen 3 because I heard the fit on the Gen 3 was different. However, I can confidently say that, for me, the Gen 3 fit even better. They are lighter and perform better overall, with 6 hours of battery life and spatial audio. I love them! The spatial audio makes it feel like you're not even wearing headphones. Theres no noise cancelation other than the fact you have something in your ears and the volume is still controlled on your phone, however very responsiveIf you're considering an upgrade, I highly recommend going for it.

They are much larger than my previous Gen2 AirPods and were uncomfortable and I felt I would not be able to wear them for long periods like my Gen2s. I had seen one or two reviews that suggested this might be a problem so would say to anyone considering them that if you have Gen2 AirPods and they 'feel' right to you then these might not. Once I've received a refund I will be buying a replacement pair of Gen2. If the Gen3 turn out to be a comfortable fit for you I believe you will be glad you bought them.

productTitle: Apple EarPods with Lightning Connector - White
reviewTitle: Sometimes it’s the simple things in life
reviewText: To think these used to come free with iPhones and now they’re being chucked out at £19 is a bit annoying but having decided to revert to basics so I can enjoy Apple Music lossless it was money well spent.I actually wear them in opposite ears, right in left, left in right and they fit better, are no longer loose and sound fantastic with highs, mids and lows very discernible.As with all Apple products, they just work but there’s a way to make them sound even better via your iPhone.1: go into settings access the music tab and if on, turn off the EQ2: ensure you have the Apple health app open and then download the Mimi hearing test from the App Store.3: performance the hearing test and screenshot the result to your photos.4: go into settings, find the accessibility tab, open it and then open audio & visual within the accessibility tab.5: turn on Headphone Accommodations and then hit custom audio setup6: you'll be asked to use audiogram, select yes and then download the photo you saved from the hearing test and your phone will analyse it and customise your audio accord to the results of the test.It’s all very clever but the difference it makes is beyond belief and has turned what were already a great set of buds into a fantastic sounding set of budsOne final tip, I also turned the sound check in the music tab off and despite many expects claiming having it on makes no difference, to my unatuned ears, turning it off in conjunction with the actions above also made a difference to the sound.You can thank me later.

productTitle: Apple AirPods Pro (1. generation) with Wireless charging case (2019)
reviewTitle: Best sound I have heard in any ear device.
reviewText: These were my first high-end hearing devices. The moment I put them in and tried them, I could really tell the difference. The sound quality was amazing, the bass, the music, I could hear everything so perfectly, I fell in love with them.It’s been 3+ years since I bought them (maybe 4 but can’t remember), and I’m still using them. Haven’t upgraded them to the latest ones, which I plan on in a year. I have also tried many other headphones since then but these are still my favourite.They come with 3 ear tip sizes and you can test them out to see which are the best by using a built-in sound leak test. They also come with a charger but can also be charged wirelessly. The case is very cool too, it’s satisfying flicking it open and then closing it lol.Noise cancellation was amazing at the start but over time, that deteriorated too. It is inconsistent now and not balanced. I used to use it every day but now I don’t as it feels very weird. Noise transparency is decent. You can hear everything around you but there is also lots of background noise, and it’s very loud so it gets annoying very quickly. The background noise in that mode is higher than other peoples voices and there is also music… it just gets very messy and all loudest thing is the background noise. It was good a few years ago but not it’s not really that great.There are some more negatives to it too, one being the cleaning. Earwax builds up inside them, around them and also in the case. I clean them using cotton buds and that does the job really well externally. As for internally, that’s a whole other problem. Not only until after 3 years did the sound quality drop, or there was uneven audio. That was because of earwax leaking into the AirPods and solidifying in there, blocking the speakers. Had to get Apple to clean them themselves.The connectivity also deteriorates with time, sometimes they don’t connect to your devices automatically and you then have to do it manually. Battery life is still pretty great honestly, and they charge very quickly.After 3 years of using them, I can recommend these to anyone that wants to listen their music in 4k ultra HD.

productTitle: Apple AirPods Max - Green
reviewTitle: "Candid Considerations: An Honest Review of Apple AirPods Max"
reviewText: The Apple AirPods Max offer a premium audio experience in a stylish and sleek design. However, there are a few considerations to keep in mind. Firstly, some users have found them to be less comfortable compared to other headphones in their price range. The weight and clamping force may cause discomfort during extended use.Additionally, while the sound quality is impressive, it may not match the level of the AirPods Pro 2. Audiophiles or individuals seeking the highest level of audio fidelity may find the AirPods Max to be slightly lacking in comparison.Furthermore, the noise-cancelling feature on the AirPods Max does a good job in reducing external noise, but it may not excel in keeping the sound from leaking out. This could be a concern for those in shared or quiet environments where sound leakage is not desired. My wife could hear music even at low volume, other Top end headphones I use do NOT have the some issue.Despite these drawbacks, the AirPods Max still offer a premium and convenient wireless listening experience, with seamless integration into the Apple ecosystem. The build quality is solid, and the adaptive EQ and spatial audio features enhance the overall audio experience. The battery life is commendable as well, allowing for extended listening sessions without frequent recharging.Ultimately, if you value sleek design and seamless integration with Apple devices, along with above-average sound quality and acceptable noise-cancelling capabilities, the AirPods Max could be a suitable choice. However, if comfort and superior noise cancellation are your top priorities, considering other options might be worth exploring.The headphones ARE extremely solidly built as you'd expect from apple. Just wish they were slightly more comfortable as my ears were getting sore after just a couple of hours.MY ONE annoyance and it's a biggie. YOU CANNOT switch the blasted things off.

productTitle: Apple AirPods Max - Green
reviewTitle: "The noise reduction along with the special audio truly enhances every piece of music.
reviewText: "Is £600 worth it? The ergonomics are fantastic with two buttons, one to switch between passive/active reduction modes, and a well-thought-out notched button for volume adjustment. Sound-wise, it's surprisingly dynamic with a good balance of bass smooth and non-aggressive. Apple should take advantage of this to improve the compression quality of their music service. The midrange and treble are also impressive, with a sound power especially noticeable in genres like movie soundtracks.Conversations are clear and even warm, creating an enjoyable experience. Of course, the integration with iOS is seamless. Yes, it's expensive, but one must compare apples to apples. While sound preferences vary, the unmatched build quality and materials used largely justify the price. Other brands use plastic, so it's not quite the same."Downsides: the weight, the smart case that fails to protect the headphones (an impractical design choice), average battery life, no included mini-jack cable, and a relatively high price This is not a hi-fi headset as it isn't closest to the source. While a technological marvel developed in a lab with a DSP working digitally for a flattering sound, audiophiles should steer clear. The H1 chips and heavy algorithmic processing make it sound unnatural, overly bass-heavy, deviating from the true nature of hi-fi. In conclusion, if you're a brand enthusiast seeking well-crafted products with a flattering sound and don't mind the price, go for it.

productTitle: Apple AirPods Max - Green
reviewText: I have had the Apple AirPods Max for a while now, and they have proven to be a premium addition to my audio experience. Here’s an in-depth review of their features, performance, and overall value:Design and ComfortThe AirPods Max exude elegance with their sleek design and high-quality materials. The stainless steel frame, coupled with memory foam ear cups and a breathable knit mesh canopy, ensures both durability and comfort. Despite their weight, they feel surprisingly light and comfortable, even during extended listening sessions. The ear cups fit snugly around the ears, providing excellent noise isolation without causing any discomfort.Sound QualityThe sound quality of the AirPods Max is exceptional. The audio is rich and detailed, with a balanced mix of deep bass, clear mids, and crisp highs. The custom-built drivers deliver an immersive listening experience, whether I'm enjoying music, listening to podcasts and watching movies. The spatial audio feature with dynamic head tracking is particularly impressive, creating a surround sound experience that enhances the overall immersion.Noise Cancellation and Transparency ModeApple's  noise cancellation capabilities of the AirPods Max are impressive. The active noise cancellation effectively blocks out ambient noise, making it perfect for use in noisy environments like airplanes or busy streets. The transparency mode is equally remarkable, allowing me to hear and interact with my surroundings without having to remove the headphones. Switching between these modes is seamless and convenient.Controls and ConnectivityThe intuitive controls on the AirPods Max make them easy to use. The Digital Crown allows for precise volume adjustments, track skipping, and Siri activation, while the noise control button lets me quickly switch between noise cancellation and transparency mode. Pairing with Apple devices is effortless, thanks to the H1 chip, which also ensures a stable and reliable connection.Battery LifeThe battery life is more than adequate for my needs, offering up to 20 hours of high-fidelity audio, talk time, or movie playback with active noise cancellation and spatial audio enabled. The included Smart Case puts the headphones in an ultra-low-power state to preserve battery life when not in use. Fast charging is a bonus, providing around 1.5 hours of listening time with just a 5-minute charge.Price and ValueThe AirPods Max are undoubtedly on the pricier side, but they justify their cost with top-tier features, build quality, and performance. For audiophiles and those who prioritize premium audio experiences, they are a worthwhile investment. However, for casual listeners, the price may be a deterrent.ConclusionThe Apple AirPods Max stand out as one of the best over-ear headphones on the market. They combine luxurious design, exceptional sound quality, and advanced features like active noise cancellation and spatial audio. While they come with a hefty price tag, the overall experience they offer makes them a compelling choice for those seeking high-end headphones. If you’re in the market for premium audio gear and are willing to invest, the AirPods Max will not disappoint.

productTitle: Apple AirPods Max - Green
reviewTitle: Awesome - probably what God uses! And worth every penny.
reviewText: I tested these carefully against the highly rated (and over £200 cheaper) Sony WH-1000XM5.For me there is no comparison - the Apple Airpods max have significantly better sound quality and I find them lots more convenient and intuitive to use than the very fiddly Sonys - not just with Apple devices but also with my Philips TV.I did try the Sony app and equalizer - which helped the sound a bit - but not enough.The volume control knob is genius. I personally love how they stay on (in low power mode?) ready to use when you put them down. Unlike the Sonys - which switch off each time then you have to remember which knob to press and how many seconds you have to hold it down to connect - every time! Battery life hasn't been an issue. Though I normally only use them at home so if they run out of juice I can plug in a long usb lead and keep listening.BRILLIANT for music and BRILLIANT for great sound when watching tv - without disturbing neighbours and without being yourself disturbed by them. Vastly better than the top rated sonos sound bar and better than using optical out to my marantz amp and a big lead across the room to wired philips fidelio headphones - or to some excellent speakers - these apples are far clearer in every case.I didn't know Bluetooth headphones could sound this good!Even MOTD is a revelation with these - besides the commentary you hear the crowd pretty well which adds to the atmosphere.If you get to test these try this :With Qobuz or Tidal, set up Massive Attack's Unfinished SymphonySet volume up to max (at least just to start with)Press PlayWow! - or your money back.(note: like most speakers / earphones the sound quality might well improve after they have run in for a number of hours)I also tested them against Apple AirPods Pro (2nd generation) (small in-ear buds).These latter also sound great (better than the Sonys) and are keepers - but can't match the Airpods Max for a full deep sound.So my Sonys went back but the Airpods Max and Airpods Pro are awesome.I didn't actually like the Sky Blue colour of the Airpods Max  when they arrived so changed them for space gray.

productTitle: Apple AirPods Max - Green
reviewText: I loved them, fantastic headphones - however. Before you drop 500 squids just google 'airpod max condensation problems', I wish I did. To begin with connection started dropping at random times annoying but simple to fix. Then they struggled to connect, then they simply would not connect, then no connection light flashes, then nothing - dead. The only thing that happens now is they charge up but other than that are useless, £500 ear muffs. Sending to Apple for hopefully a warranty repair or replacement - but its a known issue caused by condensation pooling in the cups. Not from gym use, or out for a run - no just normal listening. Many people have had this problem, to my mind Apple should do a recall. If you want to use these for an hour a day they may work for you but then £500 for a pair of headphones that you use for an hour is ridiculous. Buyer beware.*Update - Apple sent them away for repair under warranty and returned them - looks like they just replaced the whole headband and connectors - so far they have been working fine, which is good. only once have they glitched and had connection issues since being fixed. Apart from this issue they are great, but i have owned many sets of different headphones over the years and none of them have i ever had to return, and i most of them have been treated worse than i treat these ie in the gym etc. i have had two sets of lindy headphones that are low cost but great sound, that for the price are amazing and have never failed me once. its a bummer, every so often Apple do something really rubbish like this - the last time was when they released the airport extreme wireless router which was next to useless but they refunded this - they reall should do a recall on these headphones.

reviewText: The Apple AirPods Max offer a premium audio experience in a stylish and sleek design. However, there are a few considerations to keep in mind. The weight and clamping force may cause discomfort during extended use.Additionally, while the sound quality is impressive, it may not match the level of the AirPods Pro 2. Audiophiles or individuals seeking the highest level of audio fidelity may find the AirPods Max to be slightly lacking in comparison.Furthermore, the noise-cancelling feature on the AirPods Max does a good job in reducing external noise, but it may not excel in keeping the sound from leaking out. This could be a concern for those in shared or quiet environments where sound leakage is not desired. My wife could hear music even at low volume, other Top end headphones I use do NOT have the some issue.Despite these drawbacks, the AirPods Max still offer a premium and convenient wireless listening experience, with seamless integration into the Apple ecosystem. The build quality is solid, and the adaptive EQ and spatial audio features enhance the overall audio experience. The battery life is commendable as well, allowing for extended listening sessions without frequent recharging.Ultimately, if you value sleek design and seamless integration with Apple devices, along with above-average sound quality and acceptable noise-cancelling capabilities, the AirPods Max could be a suitable choice. However, if comfort and superior noise cancellation are your top priorities, considering other options might be worth exploring.The headphones ARE extremely solidly built as you'd expect from apple. While a technological marvel developed in a lab with a DSP working digitally for a flattering sound, audiophiles should steer clear. The H1 chips and heavy algorithmic processing make it sound unnatural, overly bass-heavy, deviating from the true nature of hi-fi. In conclusion, if you're a brand enthusiast seeking well-crafted products with a flattering sound and don't mind the price, go for it.

productTitle: Apple AirPods Max - Silver
reviewText: I have had the Apple AirPods Max for a while now, and they have proven to be a premium addition to my audio experience. Here’s an in-depth review of their features, performance, and overall value:Design and ComfortThe AirPods Max exude elegance with their sleek design and high-quality materials. The stainless steel frame, coupled with memory foam ear cups and a breathable knit mesh canopy, ensures both durability and comfort. Despite their weight, they feel surprisingly light and comfortable, even during extended listening sessions. The ear cups fit snugly around the ears, providing excellent noise isolation without causing any discomfort.Sound QualityThe sound quality of the AirPods Max is exceptional. The audio is rich and detailed, with a balanced mix of deep bass, clear mids, and crisp highs. The custom-built drivers deliver an immersive listening experience, whether I'm enjoying music, listening to podcasts and watching movies. The spatial audio feature with dynamic head tracking is particularly impressive, creating a surround sound experience that enhances the overall immersion.Noise Cancellation and Transparency ModeApple's  noise cancellation capabilities of the AirPods Max are impressive. The active noise cancellation effectively blocks out ambient noise, making it perfect for use in noisy environments like airplanes or busy streets. The transparency mode is equally remarkable, allowing me to hear and interact with my surroundings without having to remove the headphones. Switching between these modes is seamless and convenient.Controls and ConnectivityThe intuitive controls on the AirPods Max make them easy to use. The Digital Crown allows for precise volume adjustments, track skipping, and Siri activation, while the noise control button lets me quickly switch between noise cancellation and transparency mode. Pairing with Apple devices is effortless, thanks to the H1 chip, which also ensures a stable and reliable connection.Battery LifeThe battery life is more than adequate for my needs, offering up to 20 hours of high-fidelity audio, talk time, or movie playback with active noise cancellation and spatial audio enabled. The included Smart Case puts the headphones in an ultra-low-power state to preserve battery life when not in use. Fast charging is a bonus, providing around 1.5 hours of listening time with just a 5-minute charge.Price and ValueThe AirPods Max are undoubtedly on the pricier side, but they justify their cost with top-tier features, build quality, and performance. For audiophiles and those who prioritize premium audio experiences, they are a worthwhile investment. However, for casual listeners, the price may be a deterrent.ConclusionThe Apple AirPods Max stand out as one of the best over-ear headphones on the market. They combine luxurious design, exceptional sound quality, and advanced features like active noise cancellation and spatial audio. While they come with a hefty price tag, the overall experience they offer makes them a compelling choice for those seeking high-end headphones. If you’re in the market for premium audio gear and are willing to invest, the AirPods Max will not disappoint.

Vastly better than the top rated sonos sound bar and better than using optical out to my marantz amp and a big lead across the room to wired philips fidelio headphones - or to some excellent speakers - these apples are far clearer in every case.I didn't know Bluetooth headphones could sound this good!Even MOTD is a revelation with these - besides the commentary you hear the crowd pretty well which adds to the atmosphere.If you get to test these try this :With Qobuz or Tidal, set up Massive Attack's Unfinished SymphonySet volume up to max (at least just to start with)Press PlayWow! - or your money back.(note: like most speakers / earphones the sound quality might well improve after they have run in for a number of hours)I also tested them against Apple AirPods Pro (2nd generation) (small in-ear buds).These latter also sound great (better than the Sonys) and are keepers - but can't match the Airpods Max for a full deep sound.So my Sonys went back but the Airpods Max and Airpods Pro are awesome.I didn't actually like the Sky Blue colour of the Airpods Max  when they arrived so changed them for space gray.

productTitle: Apple AirPods Max - Space Gray
reviewText: I loved them, fantastic headphones - however. Before you drop 500 squids just google 'airpod max condensation problems', I wish I did. To begin with connection started dropping at random times annoying but simple to fix. Then they struggled to connect, then they simply would not connect, then no connection light flashes, then nothing - dead. The only thing that happens now is they charge up but other than that are useless, £500 ear muffs. Sending to Apple for hopefully a warranty repair or replacement - but its a known issue caused by condensation pooling in the cups. Not from gym use, or out for a run - no just normal listening. Many people have had this problem, to my mind Apple should do a recall. If you want to use these for an hour a day they may work for you but then £500 for a pair of headphones that you use for an hour is ridiculous. Buyer beware.*Update - Apple sent them away for repair under warranty and returned them - looks like they just replaced the whole headband and connectors - so far they have been working fine, which is good. only once have they glitched and had connection issues since being fixed. Apart from this issue they are great, but i have owned many sets of different headphones over the years and none of them have i ever had to return, and i most of them have been treated worse than i treat these ie in the gym etc. i have had two sets of lindy headphones that are low cost but great sound, that for the price are amazing and have never failed me once. its a bummer, every so often Apple do something really rubbish like this - the last time was when they released the airport extreme wireless router which was next to useless but they refunded this - they reall should do a recall on these headphones.

productTitle: Apple AirPods Pro (2nd generation) ​​​​​​​
reviewTitle: Brilliant tech and a worthwhile investment
reviewText: These were my first pair of Apple AirPods. I was hesitant to buy them due to their high price, but have been delighted with them, and am pleased I opted for the Pros.As expected, the AirPods work flawlessly with my iPhone. They pair quickly and intelligently and don't need constant resetting like so many other Bluetooth devices. Your phone will also tell you the charge status of the AirPods, and lets you customise settings such as motion tracking and noise cancelling features.The sound quality is superb and easily rivals the many other professional earbuds and headphones I have tried over the years. The noise cancelling functions are incredible, providing an option to almost completely mask the noise of trains or planes, for example. During one flight, I put them in without listening to music, just so that the noise-cancelling function could reduce the cabin noise enough for me to get some sleep.I also like that you can control noise transparency, which is much safer when you are out in public; this lets you filter in actual sounds when needed, for example when turning your head to check for traffic, or when speaking to someone. It works brilliantly.The earbuds are very comfortable and at first I thought they would never fall out. After a few months of use, I would revise that by saying they can fall out when you get hot and sweaty, and seem to hold less well since I rinsed them under the tap, as advised.

reviewText: For those considering an upgrade from the first generation of AirPods Pro, the second generation is truly worth the investment. productTitle: Apple AirPods Pro (2nd generation) ​​​​​​​
reviewTitle: Noise cancellation is only partial at best!
reviewText: I am a classical music lover, and use these ear buds almost exclusively for this purpose. Whilst the sound quality is really quite good, the so-called noise cancellation feature is not. The word implies that extraneous sounds will be eliminated. They are not; merely reduced in volume. This is a disappointment to me, as all the softer passages in a piece of music will have to compete with whatever ambient noise is present. Even my wife’s quietly spoken contribution to a telephone call in the same room as me listening to Sibelius is clearly audible in the piano passages. I don’t know whether any of the rival makes of earbuds offers true noise cancellation, but Apple’s certainly don’t.I would also add that controlling the volume by stroking the stalk of the earbud is very much hit and miss. I have still to find the trick of selecting what Apple call ‘transparency’.In short, these ear buds offer reasonable sound quality, but at an unjustifiably high price.

productTitle: Apple AirPods (3rd generation) with Lightning Charging Case ​​​​​​​
reviewTitle: Fit well, easy to pose music !
reviewText: I have been using my AirPod 2 over 8 years and it started stop working on one ear. The battery life gets much shorter as 1h30 mins instead of 5 hours. They still work but the time I can use gets shorter and shorter month by month giving me a bit of sweat 😰 to anticipate the end . I started feeling that it is the time to upgrade my AirPod . They did work so long perfectly every day for me . I had hesitation between pro and AirPod 3 . But my silicone lemon case got moulds up and I had been unable to remove them by any antibacterial products. They entered deep inside of silicone material. So I had a great doubt about the silicone ear pods. I had decided for the AirPod 3 without them . The stem is shorter but fits my small ear canal so well . I shake my head and jump to test but they don’t drop from my ear . Great for sports.Shame that I can’t share my test video. Also AirPod3 gives you a stem control and without taking them off like v2 , you can pose the music in a shop to talk to people. I don’t want to be rude nor antisocial so this is much appreciated upgrade . Only the issue is the volume control . You got to ask Siri to increase volume or decrease it . Siri does it but only for one command per one press of your stem . Doesn’t accept my second request : a bit louder please. You got to start again to call Siri . In public place, I do look wierdo possibly. Pro has this stem control for volume . But I insist that I don’t like silicone for hygiene reasons . Overall I am very happy with AirPod 3 . Very comfortable to wear them . The sound quality gets slightly better after 6 uses but you can adjust them in system settings . I prefer not to follow the test as ended in the wrong settings . Try selecting one with you favourite music and compare with other 2 . I prefer balanced tone and make the soft sound come forward strongly . Often Apple products automatically upgrades the quality while we are using so I guess the sound is not that wow on the first music after your set up . Spatial Audio needs to be set up showing your ears. You do feel differences from normal audio settings. Not overly dramatic but good to use it . AirPod 3 is great with the stem control . I no longer need to tap my ear and I can pose music without taking them out  from my ears and hurry to find a case in my bag or pockets not to loose them . Wonderful. I don’t walk without AirPod over 8 years. It is indeed my encouragement for walking. With AirPod I now walk longer and do sports much more. Life gets so much richer with world musics . When I was young it was so hard to get foreign music. Crossing borders was so hard . Compared with the record recorded my first concert at the age4 , we are living in extraordinary time with full choices and stimulation for our creativity. I do recommend AirPod 3 . Affordable with credit without interest in Amazon and simply I cannot live without it ! Thank you for delivering this to me.

productTitle: Apple AirPods Pro (2nd generation) with MagSafe Case (USB‑C) ​​​​​​​
reviewText: I've been using the AirPods Pro 2 for a few weeks now, and I'm blown away by the improvements Apple has made. As a long-time AirPods fan, I was excited to see what the new Pro 2 model had to offer, and I can confidently say that it's a game-changer.First and foremost, the sound quality is phenomenal. The new drivers deliver a richer, more detailed sound with improved bass response. The spatial audio feature is also incredibly impressive, allowing me to pinpoint the source of sounds in my music and videos with uncanny accuracy.But it's not just the sound quality that's improved - the noise cancellation is also significantly better. The Pro 2 model uses a new adaptive noise cancellation system that can detect and adjust to different types of noise in real-time, making it much more effective at blocking out distractions.One of the biggest surprises for me was the battery life. Apple claims that the Pro 2 model has up to 5 hours of listening time on a single charge, but in my experience, I've gotten even longer battery life. I've easily gotten through a full day of listening without needing to recharge, and I love that the charging case can give me an extra 24 hours of battery life on the go.The design is also a major improvement over the original AirPods Pro. The new earbuds are more comfortable and fit more securely in my ears, thanks to a new silicone tip design. The charging case is also more compact and easy to carry around.Finally, the new features like active noise cancellation and spatial audio are incredibly useful. The ability to customize my noise cancellation settings and adjust the sound to my liking is a major plus.Overall, I'm thoroughly impressed with the AirPods Pro 2. If you're an audiophile or just looking for a reliable and convenient pair of wireless earbuds, these are definitely worth considering. They're not cheap, but they're worth every penny.Pros:Phenomenal sound quality with improved bass responseExceptional noise cancellation with adaptive noise cancellation systemLonger battery life than previous modelsMore comfortable design with new silicone tipsCustomizable noise cancellation settingsCons:None significant enough to mentionRecommendation:If you're in the market for a high-quality pair of wireless earbuds, I highly recommend the AirPods Pro 2. They're an investment, but they're worth every penny. Whether you're an audiophile or just looking for a reliable and convenient pair of earbuds, these are definitely worth considering.

productTitle: Apple AirPods Pro (2nd generation) with MagSafe Case (USB‑C) ​​​​​​​
reviewText: As an avid consumer of tech gadgets, I recently upgraded to the Apple AirPods Pro (2nd generation), and I must say, I'm thoroughly impressed. From the moment I unboxed them, the sleek design and attention to detail were evident. Here's why I believe these AirPods are worth every penny:Impeccable Sound Quality: The sound quality of the AirPods Pro is simply outstanding. The dynamic range is impressive, offering crisp highs, rich mids, and deep, satisfying bass. Whether I'm listening to music, podcasts, or taking calls, every sound is crystal clear.Active Noise Cancellation: One of the standout features of the AirPods Pro is the active noise cancellation. It effectively blocks out external noise, allowing me to fully immerse myself in whatever I'm listening to, even in noisy environments like crowded cafes or on public transportation.Transparency Mode: When I need to stay aware of my surroundings, Transparency Mode comes in handy. It lets in outside sound while still delivering high-quality audio, making it safe and convenient for activities like walking or jogging outdoors.Comfortable and Secure Fit: The AirPods Pro come with interchangeable ear tips, ensuring a customised and comfortable fit for any ear shape. They stay securely in place, even during my vigorous exercise, thanks to the innovative design that contours to the ear.Convenient MagSafe Case: The MagSafe Case adds an extra layer of convenience to the AirPods Pro experience. It securely attaches to my iPhone or MagSafe charger, ensuring that my AirPods are always charged and ready to go whenever I need them.Intuitive Controls: With simple touch controls on the stem of each AirPod, I can easily play, pause, skip tracks, answer calls, and activate Siri without needing to take out my phone.Seamless Integration with Apple Ecosystem: Being an Apple user, the seamless integration of AirPods Pro with my other Apple devices is a major plus. They automatically connect to my iPhone, iPad, and Mac, and switching between devices is effortless.Overall, the Apple AirPods Pro (2nd generation) with MagSafe Case have exceeded my expectations in terms of sound quality, comfort, and convenience. They're a true testament to Apple's commitment to innovation and user experience. If you're in the market for wireless earbuds that deliver exceptional performance and seamless integration with your Apple devices, look no further than the AirPods Pro. Highly recommended!

productTitle: Apple AirPods Pro (2nd generation) with MagSafe Case (USB‑C) ​​​​​​​
reviewTitle: Amazing - wish I brought them sooner!
reviewText: I genuinely had not realised the superb sound quality and convenience of using these AirPods Pro 2 within an Apple ecosystem (iPhone, iPad, and iPad Pro). The way they automatically and seamlessly switch to whichever of these devices I am using is amazing. The sound quality is also far better than I expected, and I am someone who has been brought up with true Hi-Fi, including high-end headphones with a dedicated headphone amp, and am even a Linn Sondek LP12 owner (for those who know their classic turntables). So, I like to think I care and know a bit about what true Hi-Fi sounds like. Audio over Bluetooth has its limits, but these are by far the best Bluetooth earphones I have used. The usability makes them a pleasure to use and also they have very effective and adaptable sound-cancelling modes.I also particularly liked the spatial audio setting. I thought it might be a gimmick as I normally like to have a pure signal and always choose lossless audio if I can. I thought spatial mode would destroy any semblance of Hi-Fi sound through them, but no, it actually can be very pleasant and provides an out-of-head soundstage that is often preferable with stereo music and audiobook sources. I often go for a walk listening to these Airpods Pro 2nd Gen, and with the sound cancellation and the spatial mode, it is extraordinary walking along and makes the experience so much more enjoyable. The transparency mode is so useful to switch on to speak with someone.  I had tried various lower-cost and decently rated cabled and wireless earbuds, as well as more costly cabled and wireless over-ear headphones, but these offer so much in one small package. I can't take my high-end Hi-Fi system with me on the go, but these give a very good and more than acceptable sound quality. Even the bass is decent and well-balanced. I wonder why I wasted my time hesitating to buy these. Well, the Gen 2 was not release in UK until Sep '22, so I would have had to wait until then, but I wish I had grabbed a pair then.)  Highly recommended if you are invested in the Apple ecosystem.  Easy 5* product.

productTitle: Apple AirPods Pro (2nd generation) with MagSafe Case (USB‑C) ​​​​​​​
reviewTitle: An absolute must for an iPhone user
reviewText: There are so many plus points for these earpods, and I must admit I was skeptical as I had come from Bang & Olufsen earbuds so was worried about the audio quality.The audio quality is great, its not burst your eardrums loud, but I like to hear my music, not feel it in my skull.The noise cancellation is very good, and the transparency mode is great if you still need to be aware of your surroundings. It has conversation awareness which if it detects you are speaking it will lower your music volume and turn on transparency mode if not already on. There are loads of customisable features in the iPhone and iPad ecosystem to take advantage of these earbuds. Spatial audio is great too as when you are using a compatible app your headphones make it seem as though the sound is coming from your device all the time even when you turn your head.Swipe gestures to control volume is much better than deciphering different taps, and pinch to answer calls, play/pause and skip track is again much better than trying to tap the right sequence in the right spot.Build in "find my" features on not only the airpods case but the airpods themselves is great if you are prone to losing them. Wireless charging as standard with USB-C charging available too. You can use an apple watch magsafe charger too on it as opposed to a regular QI charger.Case lid snaps shut with a satisfying snap and stays shut to keep the buds protected. Battery life is great and I have not noticed any reduction in quality over my B&O earpods that these have replaced. I am now a wearer of these almost constantly and I carry them literally everywhere.

productTitle: Apple AirPods Pro (2nd generation) with MagSafe Case (USB‑C) ​​​​​​​
reviewText: Well I’ve owned the 1st gen AirPods Pro for nearly 5y. They were quite good overall, but weren’t totally great. Plus for about a year now, they had developed a fault in one ear pod which caused a loud hissing on one side whenever noise cancellation is switched on. The tiny batteries still seemed fine, which is remarkable I think.So I had a couple of gift vouchers from a recent birthday, plus when I saw the AirPods Pro2 were on a Prime day offer I decided to treat myself. Well I expected an incremental improvement but I wasn’t prepared for the massive improvement that this new set brings really!!The sound quality is much better with extended bass and clear bright treble. The battery life is noticeably improved on a single charge (and it was pretty good before). Now at last I can adjust the volume by touching the EarPod (though admittedly it’s still a bit fiddly swiping, there is a knack to it but it works). They seem far more adaptive to the ear canal tip fitting/sealing than before. Even the ear fit test now works with a pass (it very rarely did before).But best of all is the improvement in the noise cancellation. It is nothing less than astonishing. I can now mow the lawn in virtual silence with no need to blast the music so high as to overcome it (they can go very loud though, if that’s you’re thing too, but be careful about hearing damage!).I have several high end pairs of on ear noise cancelling headphones, but the active noise cancellation with these Airpods pro2 is sublime in comparison. It really is in a league of its own. Gobsmacked. I’m not making this up, honestly. I am so glad I took the plunge to upgrade. The engineers have done some seriously good work on this product. They’re not cheap but IMHO they are worth every penny.PS they can double up as pretty remarkable hearing enhancers, if you know how to enable the enhanced transparency modes on the iPhone. It’s in the phone accessibility menu. That’s my only gripe really. The various enhanced settings are not all in one place on the phone. It’s a PITA having to surf various menus to setup the AirPods Pro extended features (it was the same on the previous ones, so nothing lost, I just wish they would overhaul the convoluted menu steps and put everything in one place!). Plus the EQ tone table abomination on the iOS music app is not adequate to bring out the very best that these pods can offer. You ideally need to use a third party music app with a proper graphic equaliser to fully open up the amazing capabilities of these AirPods Pro2.  Definitely recommended.

productTitle: Apple AirPods Pro (2nd generation) with MagSafe Case (USB‑C) ​​​​​​​
reviewText: I'm new to Apple products, at least since 2007 when I had probably a 6th generation iPod classic.I've had an iPhone for the first time and had it less than a month, but I'd heard good things about these headphones. I've watched many reviews, read many reviews, and spent a lot of time thinking about whether I want to replace my PowerBeats Pro and my Bose QC35ii headphones simultaneously. The Airpod Pro 2 would then become my catch-all headphones for everything - travel, working out, and so on. That and my wife, who hates in-ear headphones, has been eyeing up my Bose headphones ever since she tried them on a plane a year ago.I pulled the trigger on these, as you have probably guessed, to compliment my shiny new iPhone and I'm glad I did.The combination of Lossless audio for Apple Music and the audio clarity these Airpods provide is insane. I'm hearing things in tracks I've listened to for 20+ years that I'd never heard before. The presence is phenomenal. There isn't an overabundance of bass, everything hits just right, in my opinion.Next up, extra features you don't get with similarly priced headphones of this style:Gesture control is not something I ever really plan on using generally, however, with these, something about it just makes it feel normal. Slide up and down to adjust the volume. Press once to pause or play, and hold the press to cycle through different noise control settings.Speaking of noise control, I use transparency mode when I'm out and about walking so I'm also aware of my surroundings. It's quite surreal actually, it's almost like you don't have headphones in and it's just music following you around. I use adaptive sometimes, but I'm not quite sure how that one works properly. I'd use the full noise cancellation in loud spaces, it does an amazing job, maybe even better than the Bose headphones.Add these to what is probably more of a gimmick than anything, but the motion-tracked "spatial stereo" - which while I feel is a gimmick, is a gimmick I love - just adds an extra layer, especially on top of transparency mode. You can be sitting there, hearing a lot of your surroundings, and when turning your head, it's like you're turning away from the source of the music - the only thing a little jarring with this, is that the source is always in front of you, no matter what. If you turn around, it's still there in front of you, which feels a little unnatural.Call quality is a bit iffy in my opinion, on both the receiving of the call and what the caller hears. I've read something about noise isolation and there being some kind of setting to improve this, I may have to have a look into this. Overall it works well enough when in a quiet room, but outside I just get complaints of not being able to hear me. Still, it's a step above anything I've had before where calls were nigh on impossible to the point I wouldn't even attempt because I knew it'd immediately start with complaints.Battery life seems good enough, but I'm hammering it with the spatial audio and transparency modes instead of having them off. I'm not getting the 6 hours from this, I'm more likely to get about 3-4 hours. I've had them on for a couple of hours as I speak and they are at 56%. I could see them lasting close to or maybe more than 6 should I turn these features off.The case is good enough - I don't know if the lid is strong enough to withstand being on a lanyard that might get chucked around a bit. I don't plan on keeping mine this way but I did get a cover to protect the case. I'm genuinely considering getting a lockable one to be on the safe side. The case also being MagSafe and having wireless charging is great - it charges pretty quickly, and charges to the Airpods themselves quickly too.Would I recommend these? At the risk of sounding like an Apple fanatic (I'm not, I don't get along with Macs and plan on sticking with Garmin for my watches), I would absolutely recommend these.

productTitle: Apple AirPods Pro (2nd generation) with MagSafe Case (USB‑C) ​​​​​​​
reviewText: Excellent noise cancelling — much better than the first generation, and to my ears it’s just as good as (if not better than) AirPods Max. Audio quality is very very good, and the ability to control volume from the AirPod stem is welcome and handy! The new skin contact sensor to detect when the AirPods are out of your ears is much better than the old optical sensors from the first generation, and doesn’t get confused if you lay the AirPods on a surface.The USB-C port on the case is welcome, and so is the ability to charge with MagSafe or an Apple Watch charger. The Find My functionality works perfectly (especially with the speaker to play sounds from the case) and the lanyard loop is a nice addition as well. You can also see the battery status of the case and AirPods when the case is closed (even works on iPad).My favourite feature is the new adaptive transparency mode, which is phenomenal in blocking out loud sound to let music / calls / audio through, but still let you be aware of your surroundings. Note that the feature needs iOS / iPadOS 17 or macOS Sonoma. However, if you set the long press gesture to switch between (any mode) and adaptive transparency on a device with a supported OS (such as iOS 17), the AirPods will use adaptive transparency when you toggle to it from the long press to the stem even when paired with an OS that doesn’t officially support it (such as macOS Ventura).At least with the USB-C case version, on macOS Ventura the icon in the menu bar when the AirPods are connected is not of the AirPods Pro, but of generic headphones. You also can’t set adaptive transparency from the menu bar toggle. I don’t use the personalised volume (I want to set the volume myself) or the conversation awareness (I prefer to take the AirPods off when talking).The one weird thing that has happened is that when talking on a call whilst in noise cancelling or adaptive transparency, when I hear my voice it sometimes sounds robotic and somewhat metallic? It’s a bit strange and doesn’t happen too often — could be a firmware issue or that my pair has a problem (inclined to think the former).Overall excellent product, and if you can get them at a discounted price below £200 then go for it! I definitely recommend.

reviewTitle: They fell out of my ears and I considered returning them
reviewText: The sound is just right for what I need, which is listening to the radio or music when out for a walk, or watching a film when travelling in a plane, the noise cancelling feature is brilliant for this, I now have the volume at about 50% of what I had with my old wired earbuds.  I’ve also used them a few times for phone calls and the convenience of having the phone in your pocket your hands free to check the calendar for appointments or write notes on a ipad is a small thing but a game changer in my opinion.Now to the bit about them staying in your ears, at first I was trying to jam the silicone tips into my ear canal to get a good seal for the noise cancelling feature, and running the test on my iPhone which kept telling me the seal on my right ear wasn’t good enough, several experiments with the different size tips provided didn’t improve the situation and my ears ached if I had the AirPods in for more than 15 minutes, plus any kind of bending or exercise and they dropped out of my ears.  I was at the point of returning them, but the lure of the features of the AirPods, plus my Son has a pair of 1st generation AirPods and regularly goes cycling or to the gym with no problem of them falling out made me want to persevere, so I bought these Gcioii 3 Pairs for AirPods Pro 2... https://www.amazon.co.uk/dp/B0BDMRVHXB?ref=ppx_pop_mob_ap_shareImmediately the AirPods stayed in my ears no matter what I was doing and the ache and discomfort disappeared meaning I could wear them longer, what this made me realise was that I was trying to insert the AirPods into my ears all wrong, they should sit at the back of your ear with the silicone tips laying at the entrance to the ear.I now use the AirPods without the Gcioli retainers if just sat or generally moving around, but still use them when exercising or cutting the grass etc.

So after a few years they died (sound cancellation only played static), and I went a few months without wireless headphones. I couldn't go back, I came back to AirPods after they switched to USB-C. I can confirm, they do have better noise cancellation than 1st gen. The bass is slightly better (not by a lot but it's there). The sound cancelling is a bit stronger but I feel it gives me a stronger "pressure" feeling in my head, the worst was on an aeroplane it was far worse than my 1st gen. reviewTitle: Good in Ear, even with small canals
reviewText: I’ve used over ears or headphones for nearly 20 years because I’ve never been able to live with in ear buds. My left ear canal is very small and oddly shaped so earbuds fall out constantly.These are the first in ear buds that I’ve been able to use without falling out.The sound is good. There’s always a bit of a drawback because the small speakers are what they are and no amount of trickery can overcome physics, but for general day to day listening these are good enough. If I want the best audio experience I’m using my home studio anyway over any ear or headphone.The built in mode options with the iPhone mean you can set up noise cancelling or adaptive audio to suit, and change modes on the phone or using the pods themselves.I got 8 hours out of them in a trans Atlantic flight in noise cancelling mode which is pretty good. And they charge quickly in their case.Had no issues with connecting them to either my iPhone or iPad and their small size in the case makes them very easy to carry around especially vs a headphone.Obviously they are not cheap. A cheaper product sound wise will probably be as good. But the fact I finally found a in ear bud I can use means this still gets full marks from me.

reviewTitle: Amazingly Good. So much better than 1st Generation.
reviewText: As someone who LOVES Bose, it's hard for me to admit but these are as good as (some) Bose ear buds that I have. I'd previously had the very first AirPods, then the first generation of AirPods Pro (which were a huge improvement on standard AirPods) and now the Gen 2 AirPods Pro.These are leaps ahead of Gen 1.The sounds is brilliant.The detail and space in the sound is more than I expect from something other than Bose. The 'thickness' of kick drums and toms that are so typically flat sounding in most headphones and buds, is something hard to achieve in tiny ear buds but it's there in the AirPods Pro 2.As someone who listens to a lot of older music (Genesis, Pink Floyd, Dire Straits, etc) that contain many acoustic instruments and subtle elements (ie Pink Floyd sound effects) that so often get lost on headphones and buds, I can hear all the details that I have previously relied on Bose for.I can't speak highly enough about these.Oh, and the transparency mode is so much better than Gen 1.Grab these if you are looking for something with great sound, comfort and build quality that won't let you down but would rather save a few quid compared to Bose (and others).Yep, I would certainly recommend them.

productTitle: Apple AirPods Pro (2nd generation) with MagSafe Case (USB‑C) ​​​​​​​
reviewText: My Mum purchased these as she had been using older airpods but they hurt her ears after a while due to the hard plastic. These work much better for her due to the silicone tips and also have an extended set of features such as sliding your fingers on the touch sensitive part to change the sound. They also have multiple new modes that allow you to use them best whatever you are doing.Using noise cancelling can be nice while on a flight or busy office when you need to focus or relax but the transparency mode can be used if cycling  or running as it allows you to still be alert of the outside world while listening to music.They also have a feature which will lower the sound if you start talking which is very useful

But they do cancel the background noises and when paired with listening to something, it’s a really great experience because it’s like only what you listen to exists, you don’t hear anything else, unless it’s very loud. productTitle: Apple EarPods (USB-C) ​​​​​​​
reviewText: The new wired Apple Earbuds are a solid upgrade. They offer great sound quality with clear highs and rich bass. The ergonomic design makes them comfortable to wear for extended periods. The built-in controls and microphone work seamlessly with Apple devices, making it easy to adjust volume, skip music with a double tap , or take calls. The classic wired connection ensures no audio lag and stable performance. However, they might not be ideal for those who prefer the freedom of wireless options. Overall, they're a great choice for Apple fans.

productTitle: Apple EarPods (USB-C) ​​​​​​​
reviewText: These are great! I owned AirPods Pro for almost 2 years until they died, AirPods Pro 2 for a few days until I returned them, and still own AirPods Max. I also had wireless headphones and earbuds from other respectable brands.I'm not satisfied with any wireless earbuds based on the experience I've had. So I've decided that the vast majority of the time I will be using my wired headphones at home or AirPods Max for the commute/office, but will buy wired earbuds for vacations and weekend getaways to travel light and not worry about charging, connectivity, reliability, and loss. And these are perfect for that.Zero complaints about connectivity, they basically just work as soon as I plug them into iPhone, macbook, or Windows laptop. And the sound quality is very good too. No hissing of any sort when no sound is playing (hissing is typical for USB headphones with cheap DACs). Highs and mids are perfect, very good for vocals, bass is ok, I'm satisfied, but some will want more. No active noise cancelling, and passive noise cancelling is basically non-existent too. Can't really expect more for the price. They are comfortable, despite being all plastic with no rubber inserts. Built-in mic quality is perfectly adequate, sounds crisp and clear, just don't expect wonders. And that's about it. Can confidently recommend these.

productTitle: Apple AirPods Max - Pink
reviewTitle: "Candid Considerations: An Honest Review of Apple AirPods Max"
reviewText: The Apple AirPods Max offer a premium audio experience in a stylish and sleek design. However, there are a few considerations to keep in mind. Firstly, some users have found them to be less comfortable compared to other headphones in their price range. The weight and clamping force may cause discomfort during extended use.Additionally, while the sound quality is impressive, it may not match the level of the AirPods Pro 2. Audiophiles or individuals seeking the highest level of audio fidelity may find the AirPods Max to be slightly lacking in comparison.Furthermore, the noise-cancelling feature on the AirPods Max does a good job in reducing external noise, but it may not excel in keeping the sound from leaking out. This could be a concern for those in shared or quiet environments where sound leakage is not desired. My wife could hear music even at low volume, other Top end headphones I use do NOT have the some issue.Despite these drawbacks, the AirPods Max still offer a premium and convenient wireless listening experience, with seamless integration into the Apple ecosystem. The build quality is solid, and the adaptive EQ and spatial audio features enhance the overall audio experience. The battery life is commendable as well, allowing for extended listening sessions without frequent recharging.Ultimately, if you value sleek design and seamless integration with Apple devices, along with above-average sound quality and acceptable noise-cancelling capabilities, the AirPods Max could be a suitable choice. However, if comfort and superior noise cancellation are your top priorities, considering other options might be worth exploring.The headphones ARE extremely solidly built as you'd expect from apple. Just wish they were slightly more comfortable as my ears were getting sore after just a couple of hours.MY ONE annoyance and it's a biggie. YOU CANNOT switch the blasted things off.

productTitle: Apple AirPods Max - Pink
reviewTitle: "The noise reduction along with the special audio truly enhances every piece of music.
reviewText: "Is £600 worth it? The ergonomics are fantastic with two buttons, one to switch between passive/active reduction modes, and a well-thought-out notched button for volume adjustment. Sound-wise, it's surprisingly dynamic with a good balance of bass smooth and non-aggressive. Apple should take advantage of this to improve the compression quality of their music service. The midrange and treble are also impressive, with a sound power especially noticeable in genres like movie soundtracks.Conversations are clear and even warm, creating an enjoyable experience. Of course, the integration with iOS is seamless. Yes, it's expensive, but one must compare apples to apples. While sound preferences vary, the unmatched build quality and materials used largely justify the price. Other brands use plastic, so it's not quite the same."Downsides: the weight, the smart case that fails to protect the headphones (an impractical design choice), average battery life, no included mini-jack cable, and a relatively high price This is not a hi-fi headset as it isn't closest to the source. While a technological marvel developed in a lab with a DSP working digitally for a flattering sound, audiophiles should steer clear. The H1 chips and heavy algorithmic processing make it sound unnatural, overly bass-heavy, deviating from the true nature of hi-fi. In conclusion, if you're a brand enthusiast seeking well-crafted products with a flattering sound and don't mind the price, go for it.

productTitle: Apple AirPods Max - Pink
reviewText: I have had the Apple AirPods Max for a while now, and they have proven to be a premium addition to my audio experience. Here’s an in-depth review of their features, performance, and overall value:Design and ComfortThe AirPods Max exude elegance with their sleek design and high-quality materials. The stainless steel frame, coupled with memory foam ear cups and a breathable knit mesh canopy, ensures both durability and comfort. Despite their weight, they feel surprisingly light and comfortable, even during extended listening sessions. The ear cups fit snugly around the ears, providing excellent noise isolation without causing any discomfort.Sound QualityThe sound quality of the AirPods Max is exceptional. The audio is rich and detailed, with a balanced mix of deep bass, clear mids, and crisp highs. The custom-built drivers deliver an immersive listening experience, whether I'm enjoying music, listening to podcasts and watching movies. The spatial audio feature with dynamic head tracking is particularly impressive, creating a surround sound experience that enhances the overall immersion.Noise Cancellation and Transparency ModeApple's  noise cancellation capabilities of the AirPods Max are impressive. The active noise cancellation effectively blocks out ambient noise, making it perfect for use in noisy environments like airplanes or busy streets. The transparency mode is equally remarkable, allowing me to hear and interact with my surroundings without having to remove the headphones. Switching between these modes is seamless and convenient.Controls and ConnectivityThe intuitive controls on the AirPods Max make them easy to use. The Digital Crown allows for precise volume adjustments, track skipping, and Siri activation, while the noise control button lets me quickly switch between noise cancellation and transparency mode. Pairing with Apple devices is effortless, thanks to the H1 chip, which also ensures a stable and reliable connection.Battery LifeThe battery life is more than adequate for my needs, offering up to 20 hours of high-fidelity audio, talk time, or movie playback with active noise cancellation and spatial audio enabled. The included Smart Case puts the headphones in an ultra-low-power state to preserve battery life when not in use. Fast charging is a bonus, providing around 1.5 hours of listening time with just a 5-minute charge.Price and ValueThe AirPods Max are undoubtedly on the pricier side, but they justify their cost with top-tier features, build quality, and performance. For audiophiles and those who prioritize premium audio experiences, they are a worthwhile investment. However, for casual listeners, the price may be a deterrent.ConclusionThe Apple AirPods Max stand out as one of the best over-ear headphones on the market. They combine luxurious design, exceptional sound quality, and advanced features like active noise cancellation and spatial audio. While they come with a hefty price tag, the overall experience they offer makes them a compelling choice for those seeking high-end headphones. If you’re in the market for premium audio gear and are willing to invest, the AirPods Max will not disappoint.

productTitle: Apple AirPods Max - Pink
reviewTitle: Awesome - probably what God uses! And worth every penny.
reviewText: I tested these carefully against the highly rated (and over £200 cheaper) Sony WH-1000XM5.For me there is no comparison - the Apple Airpods max have significantly better sound quality and I find them lots more convenient and intuitive to use than the very fiddly Sonys - not just with Apple devices but also with my Philips TV.I did try the Sony app and equalizer - which helped the sound a bit - but not enough.The volume control knob is genius. I personally love how they stay on (in low power mode?) ready to use when you put them down. Unlike the Sonys - which switch off each time then you have to remember which knob to press and how many seconds you have to hold it down to connect - every time! Battery life hasn't been an issue. Though I normally only use them at home so if they run out of juice I can plug in a long usb lead and keep listening.BRILLIANT for music and BRILLIANT for great sound when watching tv - without disturbing neighbours and without being yourself disturbed by them. Vastly better than the top rated sonos sound bar and better than using optical out to my marantz amp and a big lead across the room to wired philips fidelio headphones - or to some excellent speakers - these apples are far clearer in every case.I didn't know Bluetooth headphones could sound this good!Even MOTD is a revelation with these - besides the commentary you hear the crowd pretty well which adds to the atmosphere.If you get to test these try this :With Qobuz or Tidal, set up Massive Attack's Unfinished SymphonySet volume up to max (at least just to start with)Press PlayWow! - or your money back.(note: like most speakers / earphones the sound quality might well improve after they have run in for a number of hours)I also tested them against Apple AirPods Pro (2nd generation) (small in-ear buds).These latter also sound great (better than the Sonys) and are keepers - but can't match the Airpods Max for a full deep sound.So my Sonys went back but the Airpods Max and Airpods Pro are awesome.I didn't actually like the Sky Blue colour of the Airpods Max  when they arrived so changed them for space gray.

productTitle: Apple AirPods Max - Pink
reviewTitle: One of the best noise-cancellation headphones out there!
reviewText: There is no second-guessing this! AirPods Max is possibly the best noise-cancellation headphone out there, offering an amazing Spatial Audio experience.The headphones themselves are kind of on the bulkier side (not uncomfortable though) and would not be anyone’s first choice if travelling with them daily (AirPods Pro 2 do a better job in this regard)Also, they work best and sync seamlessly with the Apple Ecosystem, and may not be as seamless with non-Apple products!All said and done, if one is an Apple user, as of 2024 there aren’t any headphones out there that beat this in terms of sound, noise cancellation or Spatial Audio!So worth the hefty price, definitely!

productTitle: Apple AirPods Max - Pink
reviewText: The sound quality and noise cancellation are excellent.  Sadly I am unable to say the same for comfort.  They are heavy, which I suppose is the price you pay for sound quality, but they are tight on the ears and round the top of head.  After a short time the heaviness and pressure can give you a headache.  I have solved the top of head discomfort by purchasing a silicon  band from Amazon, which fits well under the top part and looks good too.  However, the weight and tightness around the ears are a problem.  I stretched the headphones between a few books for 24 hours and that has helped. I will keep them as the sound is so good, but feel for the price, Apple need to address this problem.

productTitle: Apple AirPods Max - Pink
reviewText: I loved them, fantastic headphones - however. Before you drop 500 squids just google 'airpod max condensation problems', I wish I did. To begin with connection started dropping at random times annoying but simple to fix. Then they struggled to connect, then they simply would not connect, then no connection light flashes, then nothing - dead. The only thing that happens now is they charge up but other than that are useless, £500 ear muffs. Sending to Apple for hopefully a warranty repair or replacement - but its a known issue caused by condensation pooling in the cups. Not from gym use, or out for a run - no just normal listening. Many people have had this problem, to my mind Apple should do a recall. If you want to use these for an hour a day they may work for you but then £500 for a pair of headphones that you use for an hour is ridiculous. Buyer beware.*Update - Apple sent them away for repair under warranty and returned them - looks like they just replaced the whole headband and connectors - so far they have been working fine, which is good. only once have they glitched and had connection issues since being fixed. Apart from this issue they are great, but i have owned many sets of different headphones over the years and none of them have i ever had to return, and i most of them have been treated worse than i treat these ie in the gym etc. i have had two sets of lindy headphones that are low cost but great sound, that for the price are amazing and have never failed me once. its a bummer, every so often Apple do something really rubbish like this - the last time was when they released the airport extreme wireless router which was next to useless but they refunded this - they reall should do a recall on these headphones.
